The predicate, on the datasheet of any candidate package:

$$
\mathrm{Iso}_{\rm surviving}(\text{package}) \;=\; \mathfrak{su}(3)_c \,\oplus\, \mathfrak{su}(2)_L \,\oplus\, \mathfrak{u}(1)_Y \qquad \text{(equality, not containment)}.
$$

Convention: "surviving" means after quotients, parities, and bundle data act (§5.1.1). A sharpening non-example — *dimension counting is necessary but not sufficient*: the algebra $\mathfrak{su}(2)^4$ has dimension $12 = 8 + 3 + 1$, matching the SM total, yet fails the predicate because no $\mathfrak{su}(3)$ summand is present. The constraint reads the algebra's *structure* (which simple pieces, with which ranks), never just its size; this is why it is a class predicate the selector can apply mechanically (§4.2).

### 5.1.4 What it demands of the geometry

**Species: existence. Search phase: 1 (shapes). Datasheet fields: D1, D2.**

This is dictionary row 1 of §5.0 — the category-defining translation, so it deserves its full statement once. **Plain:** by the Kaluza–Klein principle (GP.1, *Isometry*), every continuous symmetry of the hidden shape appears in our four dimensions as a gauge force, with the force's carriers arising as specific vibration modes of the geometry itself. So "three forces with these groups exist" translates, with no freedom, into "the compact factors' surviving isometries must be exactly these groups." **Precise:** running the §5.0 procedure — (1) fact: the SM gauge group; (2) predicate: §5.1.3, architectural only (no coupling values read); (3) dictionary row 1 → field D2, with D1 charged as cost; (4) geometric demand: one compact carrier per simple factor, quotient- and bundle-consistent survival, no extra summands; (5) search effect: next subsection. Row 1 is also where the category boundary lives: "forces = isometries" *is* Definition R2.5's core clause, and replacing it (bundle-sourced gauge groups) declares a different category (§2.9, §4.2).

### 5.1.5 What it did to the search

This constraint did the heaviest demolition in the funnel (GS.10 stage 1), almost entirely through facts F1 and F3 of GS.2:

| Eliminated class | Reason | GS row |
|---|---|---|
| All tori $T^n$ and torus orbifolds | Abelian isometries only — no $\mathfrak{su}(3)$ or $\mathfrak{su}(2)$ possible (F1) | GS.4 |
| Calabi–Yau 3-folds, K3 | No continuous isometries at all — no forces (F3) | GS.6 |
| Cosets of the wrong group ($SU(n{>}3)$, $G_2$, $F_4$, $E_6$, $Sp(n)$); $\mathbb{CP}^{n \geq 3}$ | A larger or wrong group survives — fails the equality | GS.5, GS.6 |
| $S^{n \geq 3}$, lens spaces, $\mathbb{RP}^n$ | Wrong surviving groups (and no chirality, previewing Gate 4) | GS.3 |

What it *forced*: one short shelf per factor — the $SU(3)$ carriers $\{K_6, \mathbb{CP}^2\}$ (adjudicated by Gate 4, §5.3), the $SU(2)$ carrier $S^2$, and the $U(1)_Y$ carrier $S^1$ (rescued by folding, §5.3). Each architectural fact of the Standard Model became one eliminative condition here, with no numerical fit anywhere in the step — the original discovery-logic record of this translation is preserved in the per-fact list now distributed across this module and §5.3.

### 5.1.6 The math, simplified then real

*Toy (Appendix T.1, explanatory only).* Compactify one extra dimension on a circle of radius $R$. The metric component $g_{\mu 5}$ — the part mixing our directions with the circle's — behaves in four dimensions exactly as a $U(1)$ gauge field $A_\mu$, and a field's charge is its integer momentum number around the circle. One circle, one force, charges quantized: the dictionary's row 1 in its smallest instance.

*Real check (hand-audit level).* The active branch's declared routing, verified by structure count: $K_6 = SU(3)/T^2$ contributes the simple summand $\mathfrak{su}(3)$ (8 generators, rank 2); $S^2$ contributes $\mathfrak{su}(2)$ (3, rank 1); the fold $S_Y^{\,1}/\mathbb{Z}_2$ preserves the $\mathfrak{u}(1)_Y$ generator (1, rank 1). Total: $8 + 3 + 1 = 12$ generators, rank $4$, simple-summand multiset $\{\mathfrak{su}(3), \mathfrak{su}(2), \mathfrak{u}(1)\}$ — equality holds, and no factor contributes an additional summand. The representation-level verification (every multiplet in the right representation of every summand) is Appendix D's table; the structural count above is what certificate G02 reproduces mechanically.

### 5.1.7 Closure

```text
Claim: The surviving isometry algebra of the active branch equals su(3)⊕su(2)⊕u(1) exactly.
Mechanism: One compact carrier per simple factor; quotient/bundle survival verified per factor.
Why it matters: Wrong or extra surviving forces contradict counted force carriers and null searches.
Closure location: Appendix D (representation-level recovery; authority for Gates 2–3).
Certificate location: certificates/G02_gauge_recovery/ (structural-algebra lint; hashes in R0).
Status: Certificate-complete under declared assumptions (declared routing table of A1).
Falsifier: Exhibit a surviving generator outside the SM algebra, or a missing SM generator, on the frozen package.
```

Mechanism demonstration: Appendix T.1 (explanatory only). Root gate — consumes no upstream gate; Gates 3, 4, 5, 7, 8, 9, 10 all consume its output per the Dependency Graph.


## 5.2 Charge and the $\mathbb{Z}_6$ Rule (Gate 3)

### 5.2.1 How to read this module

Assumed: rungs 1–3 of §2.1, Gate 2's output, and GP.1's $\mathbb{Z}_n$ entry. *New concept 1 — the center of a group.* **Plain:** inside every symmetry group there is a small core of moves that commute with everything — for $SU(3)$ it is three special "global phase clicks," for $SU(2)$ it is two. The core moves are invisible one group at a time, but when several groups act on the same objects, the cores can be *locked together*, and the lock dictates which charge combinations are allowed to exist. **Precise:** $Z(SU(3)) = \mathbb{Z}_3$, $Z(SU(2)) = \mathbb{Z}_2$; a simultaneous center transformation can be undone by a $U(1)_Y$ phase precisely when hypercharges satisfy a divisibility condition — the $\mathbb{Z}_6$ rule. *New concept 2 — local vs. global structure.* **Plain:** two theories can have identical forces locally and still differ in which charge lists are globally consistent — like a circle and a line: indistinguishable up close, different in what can wind. **Precise:** the SM's global gauge group is $[SU(3) \times SU(2) \times U(1)_Y]/\mathbb{Z}_6$, a quotient invisible to the Lie algebra (Gate 2 cannot see it) but binding on representations.

### 5.2.2 The constraint in plain English

The observed charges are bizarre and rigid. The down quark carries exactly $-\tfrac{1}{3}$ of the electron's charge — not $-0.33$, not approximately a third, but exactly, to better than one part in $10^{21}$ (the neutrality of bulk matter is one of the most precise null results in physics). And the fractions follow a pattern: thirds appear only on colored particles, and the proton's two up quarks and one down quark sum to *exactly* $+1$, canceling the electron. *What would happen if it weren't true:* shift any one hypercharge by a sliver and atoms are no longer neutral — bulk matter self-repels, chemistry and stars as we know them are impossible, and tabletop neutrality experiments falsify the theory at absurd significance. A theory in which charges are free dials has no answer to *why this pattern*; this gate demands the pattern be forced.

### 5.2.3 The constraint in simple math

Two predicates per multiplet, both exact, both architectural. With $t = +1, -1, 0$ for color $\mathbf{3}, \bar{\mathbf{3}}, \mathbf{1}$ and $d = 1, 0$ for weak doublet, singlet:

$$
Q \;=\; T_3 + Y \;\;\text{on every component}, \qquad\qquad \frac{t}{3} + \frac{d}{2} + Y \;\in\; \mathbb{Z} \quad (\text{the } \mathbb{Z}_6 \text{ rule}).
$$

Check the quark doublet: $t/3 + d/2 + Y = \tfrac{1}{3} + \tfrac{1}{2} + \tfrac{1}{6} = 1 \in \mathbb{Z}$ ✓, and its components carry $Q = \pm\tfrac{1}{2} + \tfrac{1}{6} = +\tfrac{2}{3}, -\tfrac{1}{3}$ ✓. A sharpening non-example — and note it is a *different* ledger from §3.3's: take $Y(Q_L) = \tfrac{1}{5}$. The $\mathbb{Z}_6$ sum becomes $\tfrac{1}{3} + \tfrac{1}{2} + \tfrac{1}{5} = \tfrac{31}{30} \notin \mathbb{Z}$ — eliminated *here*, by global consistency, before the anomaly ledgers of Gate 5 ever get to vote. The two gates fail the same wrong charge for independent reasons, which is exactly what "two faces of one list" (§1.3) looks like in practice.

### 5.2.4 What it demands of the geometry

**Species: existence. Search phase: 2 (discrete structure on the survivor). Datasheet fields: D4.**

Dictionary row 4. **Plain:** the charge pattern is a statement about how the three forces' cores interlock — so it translates into a demand on the *global* assembly of the survivor's factors: the fold on the hypercharge circle must identify points in a way that is simultaneously consistent with the $SU(3)$ core on $K_6$ and the $SU(2)$ core on $S^2$. The fold is one object touching all three groups at once; its consistency condition *is* the $\mathbb{Z}_6$ rule. **Precise:** procedure run — (1) fact: the observed fractional pattern and exact neutrality; (2) predicates of §5.2.3, value-free; (3) row 4 → field D4 ($\pi_1$, centers, quotient consistency); (4) demand: the $\mathbb{Z}_2$ quotient data on $S_Y^{\,1}$, jointly with the center actions, realize $[SU(3) \times SU(2) \times U(1)_Y]/\mathbb{Z}_6$ — making non-conforming hypercharges not merely unobserved but *inconsistent* on the geometry; (5) effect: next.

### 5.2.5 What it did to the search

This constraint searched **no manifolds**. Its candidate space — the first phase-2 search in the funnel — is the finite set of parity and center-identification assignments on the already-retained fold (§4.11; GS.12): which fields are even or odd at the two endpoints, and how the endpoint identification threads the $SU(3) \times SU(2)$ centers. Assignments that break the $\mathbb{Z}_6$ consistency were eliminated; the survivor is the frozen parity table, hash `ac4d2df3e708` (R1.3), which simultaneously serves Gate 4's mirror projection — one frozen object, two gates reading it (a deliberate economy the dependency graph records, not a coincidence).

### 5.2.6 The math, simplified then real

*Toy (Appendix T.2, explanatory only).* On a circle, charge is a winding integer — quantization for free, but only integers. Now take two dials and *identify* configurations under a simultaneous click of both ($\theta_1 \to \theta_1 + \tfrac{2\pi}{3}$ with $\theta_2 \to \theta_2 + \pi$): single-dial windings are no longer well-defined alone, and consistency forces the allowed charge pairs onto a lattice containing fractions in locked ratios. Locking cores together is how thirds and halves enter without ever being chosen.

*Real check (hand-audit level).* Run both §5.2.3 predicates over the six multiplets of the frozen spectrum (the same file Gate 5's certificate reads): all six pass the $\mathbb{Z}_6$ sum exactly, and all eleven Weyl components return the observed $Q$ values from $T_3 + Y$. Certificate G03 performs precisely this in exact fractions, with the $Y = \tfrac{1}{5}$ non-example as its negative control.

### 5.2.7 Closure

```text
Claim: Every multiplet of the frozen spectrum satisfies Q = T3 + Y componentwise and the Z6 rule exactly.
Mechanism: Fold/center identification realizing [SU(3)×SU(2)×U(1)]/Z6; parity table frozen.
Why it matters: Exact matter neutrality (~10^-21) and the fractional pattern are forced, not fitted.
Closure location: Appendix D (charge tables; authority for Gate 3) + dossier C4.
Certificate location: certificates/G03_charge_z6/ (exact-fraction check; reuses the G05 spectrum file).
Status: Certificate-complete under declared assumptions (Gate-2 routing; frozen parity table).
Falsifier: Any multiplet with t/3 + d/2 + Y ∉ ℤ, or any component with Q ≠ T3 + Y, on the frozen spectrum.
```

Mechanism demonstration: Appendix T.2 (explanatory only). Consumes Gate 2; its frozen parity table is co-read by Gate 4; downgrades propagate per the Dependency Graph.


## 5.3 Chirality, No Mirrors, Three Families (Gate 4)

### 5.3.1 How to read this module

This is the pedagogically deepest module — the constraint that historically killed clean Kaluza–Klein unification and the one whose translation uses the dictionary's two index rows at once. Assumed: GP.2 *Spinor and chirality*, GP.3 in full, §3.4's spectrum-as-output rung. *New concept 1 — zero mode.* **Plain:** of all the vibration patterns a field can form on the hidden shape, a special few cost no energy at all; those massless patterns are the particles we actually see, and *counting them* is the whole game. **Precise:** elements of $\ker D$ for the internal Dirac operator on the (base, bundle) pair; the observed spectrum is the zero-mode inventory. *New concept 2 — one-sided boundary projection.* **Plain:** on a closed shape, left- and right-handed patterns come in matched pairs and the net count is zero; an *edge* breaks the matchmaking — boundary conditions can admit one handedness and refuse its mirror, which is the only mechanism in the category that produces a handed world. **Precise:** the APS index on the orbifold interval, under the frozen parity assignments, returns $(n_L, n_R) = (+3, 0)$ — a one-sided count impossible on any closed factor (GS.2, fact F2).

### 5.3.2 The constraint in plain English

Three facts, all hard. *First:* every matter particle comes in exactly **three** families — copies identical in every charge, differing only in mass. *Second:* the count is not "at least three": the $Z$ boson's measured decay width counts the light neutrino species at $2.984 \pm 0.008$ — three, with the fourth excluded at overwhelming significance. *Third:* the weak force touches **only the left-handed** version of each particle, and no mirror partner (a wrong-handed duplicate) has ever appeared, despite LEP, SLD, the Tevatron, and the LHC having the sensitivity to produce them. *What would happen if it weren't true:* a mirror electron would have been pair-produced at LEP by the thousands; a fourth light family would have widened the $Z$ by a measurable step. And there is a subtler demand hiding in "exactly three": the count must be *forced*. A theory where three is the setting of a dial explains nothing — turn the dial and you'd have predicted four just as happily.

### 5.3.3 The constraint in simple math

Two predicates, both integers:

$$
\bigl|\mathrm{ind}\bigr| \;=\; \bigl|\,n_L - n_R\,\bigr| \;=\; 3 \quad \text{as a topological invariant}, \qquad\qquad n_R \;=\; 0 \quad \text{(no mirrors)},
$$

with the convention that the index is computed on the frozen (base, bundle) pair and "topological" means invariant under every continuous deformation inside the declared category. The sharpening non-example is the heart of this gate: **"three by dial" fails even though it equals three.** A geometry whose zero-mode count is a continuous function of a bundle modulus can be *set* to 3 — and the predicate rejects it anyway, because the requirement is on the *kind* of number (a deformation-proof integer), not its value. Same digit, different epistemic status; the anti-fitting firewall (§4.9) is what elevates the difference into a pass/fail condition.

### 5.3.4 What it demands of the geometry

**Species: existence. Search phases: 1 + 2. Datasheet fields: D3, D7.**

Dictionary rows 2 and 3 together. **Plain:** "exactly three, forced" demands a shape whose particle-counting machinery returns the integer 3 with no dial anywhere near it — which, by GP.3, means a shape where the count is computed by an index theorem on a classified bundle. "One handedness only" demands an edge, because closed shapes are handedness-neutral (F2). So the gate's two clauses translate into two geometric purchases: an index-rigid family counter, and a fold. **Precise:** procedure run — (1) facts: three families ($Z$-width), no mirrors (collider nulls); (2) predicates of §5.3.3; (3) rows 2 + 3 → fields D3 (index machinery, boundaries) and D7 (the bundle half of the pair); (4) demand: a Kähler factor with Borel–Weil–Bott-classified bundles whose frozen index is $\pm 3$, plus orbifold boundary data whose APS index is one-sided; (5) effect: next — including the comparison that exhibits the "forced beats cheap" rule in action.

### 5.3.5 What it did to the search

The decisive eliminations of the funnel's phase 1–2 boundary (GS.10 stage 2), and the stage where the 1981 program's ghost is laid to rest:

| Candidate | Fate | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| Bare $S^1$ | Eliminated, then **rescued by folding** | Closed and odd-dimensional → both handednesses survive, every fermion mirrored (F2); the $\mathbb{Z}_2$ fold's endpoints re-open the chirality channel |
| $\mathbb{CP}^2$ — the cheaper $SU(3)$ carrier | **Eliminated** | Its family count is a continuous bundle-moduli choice: "three by dial" (§5.3.3's non-example made flesh); the selector pays two extra dimensions for $K_6$'s forced count (§4.6) |
| Witten's $\mathbb{CP}^2 \times S^2 \times S^1$ | Eliminated | Passes Gate 2 perfectly; dies here — historically and in this funnel (GS.11) |
| $K_6 = SU(3)/T^2$ | **Forced** | Borel–Weil–Bott index of the frozen spin-$\mathbb{C}$ bundle $= -3$: a topological integer |
| Fold parity assignments | Selected (jointly with Gate 3) | The frozen parity table `ac4d2df3e708` projects every mirror candidate and survives exactly the SM multiplets |

This is also where the spectrum that Gates 3 and 5 consume is *created*: the surviving multiplet list — the frozen input of certificates G03 and G05 — is this gate's output, which is why the dependency graph routes so much through Gate 4.

### 5.3.6 The math, simplified then real

*Toy (Appendix T.3, explanatory only).* Put a fermion on an interval and assign parities at the two endpoints: the left-handed component is taken even (it survives at both walls), the right-handed component odd (forced to vanish there). Solve the zero-mode condition and one handedness has a constant surviving mode while its mirror has none — a one-sided count from boundary conditions alone, in one line of algebra.

*Real check (hand-audit level).* Read three rows of the frozen parity table: $Q_L$ carries $(+,+)$ at $\theta \in \{0, \pi\}$ — survives; its mirror $Q_L^c$ carries an odd assignment — projected; the same pattern repeats per field, and the family multiplicity of every survivor is the $K_6$ index magnitude, $3$. The complete table, the $\chi(K_6, \mathcal{E}) = -3$ statement, and the APS result $(n_L, n_R) = (+3, 0)$ are Appendix E (chirality half); certificate G04 lints the full table — survivors exactly the SM set, every mirror projected, counts consistent with the declared indices — under those declared index values.

### 5.3.7 Closure

```text
Claim: The frozen (base, bundle) pair yields exactly three chiral families and zero mirror survivors, as topological integers.
Mechanism: Borel–Weil–Bott index −3 on K6; APS one-sided index (+3, 0) under the frozen fold parities.
Why it matters: Z-width counts three; mirror nulls are decisive; an unforced count explains nothing.
Closure location: Appendix E — Chirality Closure (split per the one-claim-one-module rule).
Certificate location: certificates/G04_chirality/ (parity-table and index-consistency lint; hashes in R0).
Status: Certificate-complete under declared assumptions (declared index values; frozen parity table).
Falsifier: Any mirror surviving the frozen parities; family count ≠ 3; or the count shown to depend on a continuous modulus.
```

Mechanism demonstration: Appendix T.3 (explanatory only). Consumes Gate 2 and co-reads Gate 3's parity table; its spectrum output is consumed by Gates 5, 7, 9, 10 — the dependency graph's busiest node.


## 5.4 Anomaly Cancellation (Gate 5)

This constraint is the manuscript's worked deep example: Section 3 develops it end to end at full depth — its module subsections 5.4.1–5.4.6 *are* §3.1–§3.6, and readers should go there. This module therefore records only the closure block (5.x.7).

**Species / phase / datasheet:** consistency; evaluated on the phase-1/2 survivor's spectrum; reads D3 and D7 outputs (the frozen Gate-4 spectrum), searches nothing itself (§4.3: satisfiable vacuously — it constrains only what the existence gates force into being).

**What it did to the search:** no candidate that reached this stage was eliminated here — the backbone's index output is exactly one SM generation per family, so the Standard Model's own cancellation is inherited (GS.10 stage 4; §1.3 circularity point 2). The gate's force is prospective: any revision of the Gate-4 spectrum re-runs this filter automatically, and a single nonzero trace kills the branch.

```text
Claim: Given the Gate-4 frozen spectrum, all six anomaly traces vanish exactly.
Mechanism: Exact rational cancellation identities on one SM generation × 3.
Why it matters: A nonzero trace = quantum inconsistency = dead branch.
Closure location: Appendix E′ (full derivation, this gate only).
Certificate location: certificates/G05_anomaly_cancellation/ (run.sh; hashes in R0).
Status: Certificate-complete under declared assumptions (conditional on Gate 4).
Falsifier: Any trace ≠ 0 in exact arithmetic, or odd SU(2) doublet count, on the frozen spectrum.
```

Mechanism demonstration: Appendix T.4 (explanatory only). Consumes the frozen output of Gate 4; downgrades propagate to Gates 7, 9, 10 per the Dependency Graph.

## 5.5 Stabilization (Gate 6)

### 5.5.1 How to read this module

Assumed: GP.2 *Moduli and stabilization*, §4.7's freeze rule. *New concept 1 — a modulus.* **Plain:** geometries come with leftover dials — the radius of the circle, the overall size of $K_6$, the chamber's shape setting. Nothing so far has fixed them, and every quantitative output secretly depends on them. **Precise:** flat or light directions in the space of internal metrics and bundle data; any output computed on the geometry is a function on moduli space until the relevant directions are pinned. *New concept 2 — witness vs. tuned potential.* **Plain:** there are two ways to pin a dial. A *witness* is a structural reason it cannot move — a symmetry that holds it, an integer it must be, a special point it must sit at. A *tuned potential* is a hand placing it where the answers come out right. Both produce a fixed dial; only the first produces an explanation, and only the first survives the anti-fitting firewall. **Precise:** an admissible witness is drawn from a declared finite type list (rigidity, fixed point, integrality); a potential whose minimum location was selected using comparison data is post-hoc under §4.9 row 4.

### 5.5.2 The constraint in plain English

A theory whose predictions depend on dials nobody fixed has not made predictions — it has drawn a family of curves and pointed at the one passing through the data. *What would happen if it weren't true:* let the $K_6$ size drift by one percent and the entire KK spectrum shifts, every threshold correction of Gate 7 moves, and the claimed coupling unification becomes a statement about a dial setting rather than about nature. Worse, the dial could then be quietly adjusted *after* comparison — the exact failure mode the freeze rule exists to criminalize. This gate demands that, before any number is compared, every dial any downstream gate touches has a named structural reason to be where it is.

### 5.5.3 The constraint in simple math

$$
\forall\, m \in \mathcal{M}_{\rm used}: \;\; \exists\, W(m) \in \{\text{rigid chamber},\ \text{modular fixed point},\ \text{integer winding},\ \text{declared rigidity}\},\;\; W(m) \text{ frozen before comparison},
$$

where $\mathcal{M}_{\rm used}$ is the set of moduli read by any Gate 1–10 output. Convention: moduli *not* used downstream are declared free parameters of the category, not claimed stabilized (honesty clause, Appendix F). Sharpening non-example: "the radius is stabilized by a scalar potential whose minimum we chose at $R_0$" *passes a naive stabilization test and fails this gate* — the minimum's location is an input smuggled as a mechanism. The predicate tests the witness's *type*, not merely the dial's fixedness.

### 5.5.4 What it demands of the geometry

**Species: consistency. Search phase: 3 (chamber data and witnesses). Datasheet fields: D6.**

Dictionary row 5, witness clause. **Plain:** "predictions must be possible" translates into "every used dial carries a structural lock" — so the demand is not for new shapes but for *rigidity data on the shapes already selected*: which directions are frozen by symmetry, which settings are special points, which quantities are integers. **Precise:** procedure run — (1) fact: quantitative claims exist downstream (Gates 7–9); (2) predicate §5.5.3, value-free; (3) row 5 → field D6 (moduli load and witness hooks); (4) demand: a complete witness assignment over $\mathcal{M}_{\rm used}$ from the admissible type list; (5) effect: next.

### 5.5.5 What it did to the search

A pure pruning pass: no shapes searched, no shapes added. Branch-variants of the survivor carrying any downstream-used modulus without an admissible witness were eliminated (fail-closed condition 5, §4.10). What it *forced* is the witness ledger itself: the Weyl-rigid chamber locking $K_6$'s shape directions; the chamber modulus pinned at the modular fixed point $\tau = \omega$ — the most symmetric value possible, selected by symmetry rather than by data; and the integer Wilson-line winding $n_H$ (consumed by Gate 8). Compact radii read by the threshold spectrum carry their declared rigidity witnesses in the same ledger (Appendix F), and the scope honesty is recorded there too: stabilization is claimed exactly for $\mathcal{M}_{\rm used}$, with full cosmological vacuum history outside the claim (§2.9).

### 5.5.6 The math, simplified then real

*Toy (Appendix T.5, explanatory only).* Let a "prediction" be $y = R^2$ with $R$ unfixed: $y$ is not a number but a curve, and any measured value of $y$ can be "matched" by reading the curve backwards — zero content. Declare $R$ by witness (say, an integer constraint $R \in \mathbb{Z}$ with $R = 1$ frozen) and $y$ becomes a falsifiable number. The entire gate is this toy applied to every dial the certificates touch.

*Real check (hand-audit level).* Read the witness ledger: every row of $\mathcal{M}_{\rm used}$ (the $K_6$ shape directions, $\tau$, $n_H$, the compact radii the spectrum reads) carries exactly one witness of an admissible type, frozen with an R1 hash. Certificate G06 lints precisely this — coverage, type-admissibility, uniqueness — and its negative control deletes one witness to demonstrate the lint can fail.

### 5.5.7 Closure

```text
Claim: Every modulus used by any downstream gate carries exactly one admissible, frozen stabilization witness.
Mechanism: Weyl-rigid chamber (K6 shape), modular fixed point τ = ω, integer windings, declared radius rigidity.
Why it matters: An unfixed used dial converts every downstream "prediction" into a curve, not a number.
Closure location: Appendix F (witness ledger, tachyon check, sensitivity summary; authority for Gate 6).
Certificate location: certificates/G06_stabilization/ (witness-coverage lint; hashes in R0).
Status: Claimed certificate pass for M_used (per Appendix F); Diagnostic only applies to no modulus on the active branch.
Falsifier: Exhibit a downstream-used modulus without a witness, or a witness whose location was selected post-comparison.
```

Mechanism demonstration: Appendix T.5 (explanatory only). Consumes Gates 2–4 (which define $\mathcal{M}_{\rm used}$); Gates 7, 8, 9 consume its ledger.

## 5.6 Threshold Unification (Gate 7)

### 5.6.1 How to read this module

Assumed: Gate 6's witness ledger (the spectrum this gate reads is only meaningful because Gate 6 pinned the dials it depends on), GP.2 *KK modes*. *New concept 1 — running couplings.* **Plain:** the three forces' strengths are not constants; they drift slowly and calculably with the energy of the process probing them. Measured precisely at accessible energies, each strength extrapolates upward along a computable line — and a unified theory predicts the three lines meet. **Precise:** renormalization-group evolution of $\alpha_1, \alpha_2, \alpha_3$ from the comparison scale $M_Z$, with one-loop coefficients fixed by the surviving spectrum. *New concept 2 — threshold corrections, and why they are the honesty battleground.* **Plain:** near the meeting point, the heavy KK overtones of the hidden geometry each give the lines a small calculable nudge. Whether the lines meet *quantitatively* depends on the sum of nudges — and a dishonest theory can always make three lines meet by adjusting nudges after looking. The constraint is that every nudge be computed from the frozen spectrum, under a declared scheme, *before* the meeting is checked. **Precise:** $(\delta_1, \delta_2, \delta_3)$ from the frozen KK spectrum under the declared regulator and scheme, frozen prior to comparison.

### 5.6.2 The constraint in plain English

The fact: $\alpha_1, \alpha_2, \alpha_3$ are measured at $M_Z$ to high precision, and their bare one-loop extrapolations *almost* meet — famously, tantalizingly, not quite. A real unification claim lives or dies in that "not quite": the geometry's own heavy spectrum must supply exactly the corrections that close the gap, with nothing adjustable. *What would happen if it weren't true* — if thresholds were retunable: any three lines whatsoever can be made to intersect by sliding step-positions, so "unification" would be an empty word. The historical record is blunt here: threshold flexibility is how dead GUTs lingered, and the manuscript's own weak-links table names this gate as a prime overfit suspect — which is precisely why its certificate is a frozen ledger rather than a paragraph.

### 5.6.3 The constraint in simple math

$$
\delta_i \;=\; \delta_i\bigl[\text{frozen KK spectrum},\ \text{declared scheme}\bigr], \qquad \bigl(\delta_1, \delta_2, \delta_3\bigr)_{\rm ledger} \;=\; (+4.8424,\ -3.1112,\ -1.7313), \qquad \text{residual at } M_U \;\leq\; \text{declared floor},
$$

with the convention that spectrum, scheme, regulator, comparison scale, $M_U$, and uncertainty rule are all freeze-rule objects (§4.7 cluster 2). Sharpening non-example: shift one KK mass by $2\%$ *after* seeing the residual and the three lines can be walked onto each other — numerically a "pass," formally a §4.9-row-4 violation that voids the certificate. The predicate's force is entirely in the word *frozen*; the arithmetic is easy, the discipline is the content.

### 5.6.4 What it demands of the geometry

**Species: consistency. Search phase: evaluation on the survivor (no new search). Datasheet fields: D1, D6.**

Dictionary rows 1 + 5. **Plain:** the demand is that the survivor's stage — its sizes and spectra — *be the calculator*: the same $\times$-layer data that Gate 2 bought for forces must now yield a finite, reproducible nudge ledger. The gate purchases nothing; it audits what was already purchased. **Precise:** procedure run — (1) fact: measured $\alpha_i(M_Z)$ and near-meeting; (2) predicate §5.6.3 — note this gate's certificate-face is the manuscript's most numerical, which is exactly why its constraint-face is pure discipline (declaredness, freeze) rather than any value; (3) rows 1 + 5 → D1 (the spectrum-bearing dimensions) and D6 (the pinned moduli the spectrum depends on); (4) demand: finite heat-kernel ledger from the frozen spectrum, column-summing to a frozen target; (5) effect: next.

### 5.6.5 What it did to the search

No manifolds, no discrete structures: this consistency gate's eliminations were *variants* — any version of the survivor whose scheme or spectrum was undeclared at evaluation time (fail-closed condition 6, §4.10), and any whose threshold story required post-declaration adjustment. What it forced is bookkeeping with teeth: the heat-kernel ledger (`appendix_F_heat_kernel_ledger.csv`), the frozen target vector above, the declared comparison scale and $M_U$, and the uncertainty-propagation rule — the complete cluster-2 freeze of §4.7, bound to hashes in R0.

### 5.6.6 The math, simplified then real

*Toy (Appendix T.6, explanatory only).* Two couplings, one heavy threshold: above the step, the slopes change. Slide the step's location and the meeting point slides with it — which is the whole con, and the whole cure: declare the step *first* and the meeting point becomes a prediction; declare it after and the meeting point is a choice.

*Real check (audit level).* The claim is column arithmetic on a frozen file: the per-factor packets of the heat-kernel ledger must sum, column by column, to the frozen target vector, with closure residual at the declared numerical floor (Appendix G, G.3.2). This is the one gate in this section whose hand-audit *is* a spreadsheet sum — deliberately so. Certificate G07 is the reproduction harness for exactly that sum; it runs against the ledger CSV from the Appendix G artifact set, and reports honestly when the CSV is not yet mounted (see closure block).

### 5.6.7 Closure

```text
Claim: The frozen heat-kernel ledger column-sums to (+4.8424, −3.1112, −1.7313), closing unification with residual at the declared floor.
Mechanism: One-loop KK threshold corrections computed from the frozen spectrum under the declared scheme — frozen before comparison.
Why it matters: Retunable thresholds make any three lines "unify"; frozen ones make the meeting a prediction.
Closure location: Appendix G (ledger, scheme, scales, residuals; authority for Gate 7).
Certificate location: certificates/G07_thresholds/ — reproduction harness; status AUDIT pending mount of
  appendix_F_heat_kernel_ledger.csv from the Appendix G artifact set (exact missing item declared; no doc-only promotion).
Status: Claimed certificate pass (per Appendix G); reproduction-harness status AUDIT until the ledger CSV is imported.
Falsifier: Column sums ≠ frozen targets on the ledger; residual above floor; or any spectrum/scheme edit post-comparison.
```

Mechanism demonstration: Appendix T.6 (explanatory only). Consumes Gates 4 (spectrum content) and 6 (pinned moduli); Gates 8 and 9 read its comparison-scale machinery.
