## 5.7 Higgs Protection (Gate 8)

### 5.7.1 How to read this module

Assumed: GP.2 *Wilson line*, the §2.5 complex worked read (where the Higgs's three-layer address was established), Gate 6's witness ledger. *New concept 1 — the hierarchy problem, in one paragraph.* **Plain:** the Higgs is the one fundamental scalar we have, and scalars are uniquely fragile: quantum corrections drag a scalar's mass up toward the heaviest scale in the theory. With unification near $10^{16}$ GeV and the Higgs at $125$ GeV, an unprotected theory needs its parameters tuned to roughly one part in $10^{28}$ — two numbers agreeing to twenty-eight digits and differing in the twenty-ninth, for no reason. **Precise:** quadratic UV sensitivity of scalar mass terms; $\delta m_H^2 \sim \Lambda^2$ absent a structural mechanism. *New concept 2 — protection by integer.* **Plain:** the cure used here is to make the Higgs a quantity that *cannot drift*: a loop-phase whose controlling datum is a whole number. Whole numbers do not creep. **Precise:** the Higgs is the holonomy mode of a Wilson line on a non-contractible cycle; its potential is generated nonlocally, so corrections are finite ($\propto 1/R^2$), and its decisive datum is the frozen integer winding $n_H$.

### 5.7.2 The constraint in plain English

The fact is the measured Higgs at $125$ GeV coexisting with a claimed unification scale fourteen orders of magnitude higher. *What would happen if it weren't true* — if the branch carried no structural protection: the theory would still "have" a light Higgs on paper, maintained by a counterterm tuned to twenty-eight digits, and a reviewer would correctly read the entire unification story as an artifact of that tuning. This gate is the consistency demand that the lightness be a *property of the structure*, with a named mechanism a reviewer can attack, not an act of arithmetic heroism.

### 5.7.3 The constraint in simple math

$$
m_H \;\text{controlled by a frozen } n_H \in \mathbb{Z}, \qquad \delta m_H^2 \;\propto\; \frac{1}{R^2} \;\;(\text{finite, nonlocal}), \;\;\text{not} \;\propto \Lambda^2,
$$

with the convention that the forbidden-mass-term rule and the potential-evaluation rule are frozen objects (Appendix H). Sharpening non-example: a branch whose quadratic divergence is canceled by a counterterm adjusted to land at $125$ GeV satisfies "light Higgs" numerically and **fails the predicate** — the protection clause tests the mechanism's *type* (structural vs. tuned), the same type-distinction as §5.5.3's witnesses, because a tuned cancellation is a hidden post-hoc input.

### 5.7.4 What it demands of the geometry

**Species: consistency. Search phase: 2 (winding search on the survivor). Datasheet fields: D5.**

Dictionary row 5, cycle-and-holonomy clause. **Plain:** "a quantity that cannot drift" translates directly: the geometry must offer a loop that cannot be shrunk away, and the Higgs must *be* the phase around it — because only loop-borne, integer-controlled quantities have the required rigidity. **Precise:** procedure run — (1) fact: $m_H \ll M_U$; (2) predicate §5.7.3; (3) row 5 → field D5 (non-contractible cycles); (4) demand: a cycle $\gamma \subset K_{\rm gauge}$ hosting the Wilson-line mode $L_\gamma \otimes V_{SU(2),\rm doub}$, with integer winding frozen as a Gate-6 witness; (5) effect: next.

### 5.7.5 What it did to the search

A phase-2 search over a discrete set: the integer windings on the declared cycle. The selector's record: branch-variants with tuned-scalar Higgs sectors eliminated by the non-example rule; the winding fixed at the declared integer $n_H$ with the lower-winding exclusion recorded (windings below the declared value fail the protection rule — Appendix H's exclusion ledger); the cycle's existence itself was already guaranteed by the survivor's topology, so this gate purchased no dimensions — its entire cost is one frozen integer and two frozen rules.

### 5.7.6 The math, simplified then real

*Toy (Appendix T.7, explanatory only).* Carry a charge around a circle threaded by flux: the acquired phase depends only on the *winding number* — go around once, twice, never one-and-a-half times. Perturb the path, the metric, the schedule: the integer cannot respond. A mass term slaved to that integer inherits its immunity; that is the protection mechanism in its smallest form.

*Real check (hand-audit level).* The frozen data: Higgs origin = Wilson-line mode over the declared cycle (the §2.5 complex read, now load-bearing); $n_H$ an integer, frozen, listed as a Gate-6 witness; forbidden-mass-term rule and potential-evaluation rule frozen with hashes; correction class finite and nonlocal per Appendix H's sensitivity ledger (H.10). Certificate G08 lints the structural half — integrality, freeze, witness cross-listing, exclusion record present — with a non-integer winding as its negative control.

### 5.7.7 Closure

```text
Claim: The Higgs is a Wilson-line mode whose mass is controlled by the frozen integer winding n_H, with finite nonlocal corrections.
Mechanism: Holonomy on a non-contractible cycle of K_gauge; integer rigidity replaces tuning.
Why it matters: Without structural protection, the 125 GeV / 10^16 GeV coexistence is a 1-in-10^28 tuning.
Closure location: Appendix H (protection rule, potential evaluation, correction-class ledger) + dossier C9.
Certificate location: certificates/G08_higgs_protection/ (integrality and freeze lint; hashes in R0).
Status: Claimed certificate pass (per Appendix H).
Falsifier: Show quadratic UV sensitivity reappearing in the declared correction class, or the winding continuously deformable.
```

Mechanism demonstration: Appendix T.7 (explanatory only). Consumes Gates 2 (cycle exists on the survivor) and 6 (winding as witness); Gate 7 reads its comparison-scale interface.

## 5.8 Flavor Admissibility (Gate 9)

### 5.8.1 How to read this module

The hardest gate, the most attackable claim (Known Weakest Links, row 1), and the module entitled to the section's full length. Assumed: §2.4's $F^+$ definition, GP.4's chamber entry, Gate 4's spectrum, Gate 3's charges. *New concept 1 — the Yukawa map.* **Plain:** the backbone delivered the cast of particles; it said nothing about their *weights*. The Yukawa maps are the four frozen tables of numbers — one each for up-quarks, down-quarks, charged leptons, neutrinos — from which every mass, every mixing angle, and the CP phases are computed. In an ordinary model these tables are typed in by hand: roughly twenty dialed numbers. Here they must be *outputs*. **Precise:** $Y_u, Y_d, Y_e, Y_\nu$ obtained deterministically from frozen chamber operators acting on the projected generation basis; masses by singular values, $V_{\rm CKM} = U_u^\dagger U_d$ by diagonalizer misalignment, $J_{\rm CKM}$ and $U_{\rm PMNS}$ downstream. *New concept 2 — the over-determination ledger.* **Plain:** the test separating explanation from disguised fitting is a count: how many numbers went in versus how many independent frozen numbers came out and faced data. One-in-one-out is reparameterization; few-in-many-out is work. **Precise:** $N_{\rm declared\ inputs} < N_{\rm independent\ frozen\ outputs}$, strict, with both counts itemized in a public ledger (§4.9; the compression-tier standard).

### 5.8.2 The constraint in plain English

The flavor facts are the strangest in the Standard Model. The top quark outweighs the up quark by a factor near $10^5$, with the masses laddered in rough geometric steps within each sector. The quark mixing matrix is *hierarchical* — nearly diagonal, with off-diagonal entries shrinking in a nested pattern — and it carries exactly one irreducible complex phase, whose nonzero value is why matter and antimatter behave differently in kaon and B-meson decays. Neutrinos, by contrast, mix *strongly*. None of this looks random, and none of it follows from the gauge structure. *What would happen if it weren't true* — if the backbone were submitted alone: the Yukawa tables would be free inputs, the manuscript would be "explaining" twenty numbers by assuming twenty numbers, and the entire GUT claim would reduce to the gauge sector that 1980s theories already delivered. The quark sector applies the sharpest version of the demand, because it requires *both* a strong intra-sector hierarchy *and* a nontrivial mixing matrix with a CP phase, simultaneously: a structure producing one without the other fails.

### 5.8.3 The constraint in simple math

The predicate has three clauses:

$$
Y_s \;=\; \mathcal{Y}\bigl[O_s,\ \Pi_s,\ \text{frozen chamber data}\bigr] \;\;\text{deterministically, } s \in \{u, d, e, \nu\}; \qquad V_{\rm CKM} = U_u^\dagger U_d \;\;\text{with } J_{\rm CKM} \neq 0; \qquad N_{\rm in} \;<\; N_{\rm out}\ \ (\text{strict}).
$$

Convention: declared calibration inputs are admissible and *counted* (§4.9 row 2); everything else is frozen before comparison. The sharpening non-example is the gate's own tier table: a chamber with inputs comparable to outputs *passes every numerical comparison perfectly* — each output was bought with an input — and **fails this gate**, because the predicate tests the count, not the agreement. Agreement is cheap; compression is the claim.

### 5.8.4 What it demands of the geometry

**Species: existence. Search phase: 3 (chamber data). Datasheet fields: D6, D7.**

Dictionary rows 5 + 6. **Plain:** structured masses translate into finite *rulebook* structure — not new places: a generation space the family index acts on, sorters that separate the four sectors, frozen operators whose action ladder produces the hierarchy, and a phase source for CP — all at zero metric dimensions, all locked before data. **Precise:** procedure run — (1) facts: hierarchies, hierarchical CKM, $J \neq 0$, large PMNS mixing; (2) predicate §5.8.3 — note the *count* clause is architectural even though the certificate-face is the manuscript's most numerical (the §1.3 division at maximum stress); (3) rows 5 + 6 → D6 (chamber data and their witnesses, including $\tau = \omega$ from Gate 6) and D7 (operator domains on the matter bundle); (4) demand: the minimal chamber carrying $\{\mathcal{G}_{\rm gen},\ \Pi_u, \Pi_d, \Pi_e, \Pi_\nu,\ O_u, O_d, O_e, O_\nu,\ \text{phase},\ \text{norm},\ \mathrm{RG}\}$; (5) effect: next.

### 5.8.5 What it did to the search

The funnel's final existence move (GS.10 stage 9), and the only stage whose victim is the survivor itself: **the backbone, as a complete theory, was eliminated here** — it cannot produce frozen Yukawa maps, so flavor admissibility forced the $F^+$ augmentation (§2.3–2.4). Within the chamber search: smaller chambers failed one of the simultaneous quark demands (hierarchy *and* mixing *and* phase); larger chambers carried structure no gate output used and were razored (§4.6 rules 5–7). The surviving chamber's decisive data are themselves selection outputs, not fits: the modulus at the modular fixed point $\tau = \omega$; the sector projectors determined by group theory; the action ladders $a_u = (2, 1, 0)$ and $a_d = (4/3, 2/3, 0)$ selected lex-minimally on their declared ladder families — every entry a structural primitive with an R1 hash (the I.0 anti-fitting ledger row by row).

### 5.8.6 The math, simplified then real

*Toy (Appendix T.8, explanatory only).* A two-generation mini-chamber: one operator with one frozen angle $\theta$ and one ladder $(1, 0)$ generates two up-type masses, two down-type masses, and one mixing angle — five outputs from two structural inputs. Diagonalize each $2 \times 2$ by hand; the mixing appears as the *misalignment* of the two diagonalizations, not as an inserted angle. Compression and diagonalizer-misalignment, both visible on one page.

*Real check (audit level).* The real chamber's controlling numbers, from the frozen ledgers: **declared calibration inputs: 2** — the heavy-sector anchor $y_t(M_Z)$ fixing the up-normalization, and $|V_{us}|$ fixing the chamber angle $\theta_F$ (Appendix I.5; both declared before any other flavor quantity loads). **Independent frozen outputs faced against data: 13** in the quark sector alone — five quark masses at $M_Z$ ($m_t$ excluded: it is the $y_t$ anchor expressed as a mass, an anchor-consistency check per App I/J, not an independent output), the between-sector ratio $|y_t/y_b|$, four unitarity-independent CKM magnitudes, the CP phase $\delta_{\rm CKM}$, and $J_{\rm CKM}$ (Appendix J.7) — with the lepton and neutrino outputs of Appendix K on top. Tier: *very strong compression* (1–2 inputs, $\geq 9$ outputs). The numerical comparisons themselves are the J.6 and K.5 output tables; the calibration step itself is worked line by line in Section 8; this module's hand-audit is the count, which is exactly what a fit cannot fake.

### 5.8.7 Closure

```text
Claim: Frozen chamber operators deterministically generate Y_u, Y_d, Y_e, Y_ν; the quark sector alone yields 13 independent frozen outputs from 2 declared inputs, within declared bands (m_t excluded as the y_t anchor expressed as a mass — anchor-consistency check, App I/J — not an independent output).
Mechanism: F⁺ chamber — τ = ω, group-theoretic projectors, lex-min action ladders — with CKM from diagonalizer misalignment, not insertion.
Why it matters: Without it the Yukawa sector is ~20 dialed numbers and the GUT explains nothing the 1980s didn't.
Closure location: Appendices I (chamber + anti-fitting ledger), J (quark certificate), K (lepton/neutrino certificate); Section 7 ledger.
Certificate location: certificates/G09_flavor/ — over-determination count verified in exact arithmetic (PASS);
  numerical-comparison harness AUDIT pending mount of the J.6/K.5 output tables from the Appendix J/K artifact set.
Status: Claimed certificate pass (per Appendices I–K); over-determination sub-claim machine-verified; comparison harness AUDIT until artifacts imported.
Falsifier: N_in ≥ N_out on the itemized ledger; any post-comparison phase or operator adjustment; any J.6/K.5 output outside its declared band.
```

Mechanism demonstration: Appendix T.8 (explanatory only). Consumes Gates 3, 4 (charges and spectrum), 6 ($\tau$ witness), 7 (RG transport to the comparison scale); the scoped-GUT claim consumes it. Scope rule (binding): this gate cannot be deferred or moved into the Section 9 boundary list — deferral drops it to *Pending — not used in claim* and weakens the whole claim (§1.2.1).

---

## 5.9 Proton Safety (Gate 10)

### 5.9.1 How to read this module

Assumed: GP.2 bundles/operators, the $\otimes$-layer reads of §2B.3. *New concept 1 — dangerous operators.* **Plain:** unifying quarks and leptons into one structure is the whole point of a GUT — and its mortal risk: any interaction with one quark leg and one lepton leg lets protons decay. Such interactions are the *dangerous operators*, and generic unification produces them wholesale. **Precise:** baryon- and lepton-number-violating operators; the declared class here (L.2a) is the sector-respecting Wilson-coefficient operators with one fermion leg in the quark sector and one in the lepton sector. *New concept 2 — projector orthogonality.* **Plain:** the cure used here is surgical: two sorting operators stamp every mode *quark* or *lepton*, and the geometry's operator algebra is built so that nothing stamped quark can connect to anything stamped lepton — the dangerous class is not suppressed but *structurally absent*. **Precise:** the identity $\Pi_q M \Pi_\ell = 0$ for every operator $M$ in the declared class, on the frozen sector labeling.

### 5.9.2 The constraint in plain English

The fact is a silence: Super-Kamiokande has watched fifty thousand tons of water for decades, and protons do not decay — lifetime above $\sim 10^{34}$ years in the cleanest channels. *What would happen if it weren't true* is not hypothetical; it is the field's most famous execution: minimal $SU(5)$, the first and most beautiful GUT, predicted lifetimes near $10^{30}$ years, and the water tanks killed it. Every unification proposal since lives under that precedent. This gate demands the active branch name its dangerous class out loud *before* defending against it (the L.2a declaration — blocking the "you only eliminated a narrowed subclass" attack the manuscript's weak-links table anticipates), and then show the class absent, suppressed, or bounded.

### 5.9.3 The constraint in simple math

$$
\Pi_q\, M\, \Pi_\ell \;=\; 0 \quad \forall\, M \in \mathcal{O}^{\rm declared}_{\rm danger}, \qquad \text{any residual channel bounded below experiment, reported \emph{Diagnostic only}},
$$

with the convention that $\mathcal{O}^{\rm declared}_{\rm danger}$ is fixed by L.2a *before* the identity acts, and the projectors are frozen $\otimes$-layer objects (dossier C10). Sharpening non-example: a branch that proves the identity on a *narrower* class than it declared — or declares no class at all and proves the identity on whatever happens to vanish — produces a true equation and a false safety claim. The predicate is two-part by design: declaration first, elimination second; the equation without the declaration is the failure mode.

### 5.9.4 What it demands of the geometry

**Species: consistency. Search phase: evaluation on the survivor's $\otimes$-layer (no new search). Datasheet fields: D7.**

Dictionary row 6. **Plain:** "protons must not decay" translates into a demand on the *kinds of operators the geometry's field content can form*: the matter bundle must come pre-sorted, with the sorting respected by every admissible operator — safety as a property of the actor layer, not a numerical accident. **Precise:** procedure run — (1) fact: Super-K bounds; (2) predicate §5.9.3; (3) row 6 → D7 (admissible operator content, projector identities); (4) demand: frozen sector projectors $\Pi_q, \Pi_\ell$ on $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ with the orthogonality identity holding on the declared class, plus the L.2b coverage table tying the declared class to the physically relevant one; (5) effect: next.

### 5.9.5 What it did to the search

Eliminated the mediator-bearing variants — versions of the survivor whose operator algebra contained cross-sector connectors (the classic $X, Y$-boson failure mode that killed $SU(5)$), per fail-closed condition 9 (§4.10). Forced: the $\mathcal{E}_{\rm proton}$ projector layer (dossier C10) — the tenth load-bearing term of the active branch exists *because of this gate* — together with the FCNC no-mediator theorem (L.3) and the operator-class declaration and coverage ledger (L.2a, L.2b). The lifetime number itself was deliberately *not* forced: it is reported as a diagnostic estimate above the Super-K bounds and is excluded from the closure claim (L.4) — safety is certified, a lifetime prediction is not claimed.

### 5.9.6 The math, simplified then real

*Toy (Appendix T.9, explanatory only).* Two sectors, basis $\{q, \ell\}$: $\Pi_q = \mathrm{diag}(1, 0)$, $\Pi_\ell = \mathrm{diag}(0, 1)$. Every sector-respecting operator is block-diagonal, and $\Pi_q M \Pi_\ell$ reads off exactly the upper-off-diagonal block: zero by construction. Write one off-block entry and the product is nonzero on sight — the identity is a block-structure statement a reader can verify by multiplying $2 \times 2$ matrices.

*Real check (hand-audit level).* The real system is the toy with labels: the frozen sector labeling of $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ (three quark-sector and three lepton-sector module slots per generation), the projectors as frozen diagonal idempotents, and the declared class as the sector-respecting operators of L.2a — on which the identity holds exactly, while the L.2b coverage table records what stands between the declared class and the physically relevant one. Certificate G10 runs the identity in exact arithmetic over the frozen labeling, enumerating the declared class's block structure, with an off-block intruder as its negative control.

### 5.9.7 Closure

```text
Claim: On the frozen sector labeling, Πq M Πℓ = 0 for every operator in the L.2a-declared dangerous class; residual channels are Diagnostic only and sit above Super-K bounds.
Mechanism: Frozen sector projectors on the matter bundle; class declared before elimination; coverage tabled (L.2b).
Why it matters: Cross-sector mediators are how GUTs die — minimal SU(5) is the precedent.
Closure location: Appendix L (declaration L.2a, coverage L.2b, no-mediator theorem L.3, diagnostic L.4) + dossier C10.
Certificate location: certificates/G10_proton_safety/ (exact projector-identity check on the frozen labeling).
Status: Claimed certificate pass for the structural identity (per Appendix L); lifetime estimate Diagnostic only.
Falsifier: Any declared-class operator with a nonzero cross-block element on the frozen labeling, or a physically relevant operator class outside the L.2b coverage.
```

Mechanism demonstration: Appendix T.9 (explanatory only). Consumes Gates 2 and 4 (the representation content the projectors sort); no required gate consumes its output.

## 5.10 Claim Boundary (Gate 11)

*The one module exempt from the Section-3 shape (§5.0 master table, row 11): this gate translates to nothing geometric, and the table says so out loud.*

**Plain.** The last requirement of a credible submission is naming what it does *not* claim. Gate 11 exists to prevent the two failure modes of ambitious papers: quiet overclaim (letting readers infer that cosmology or quantum gravity are addressed) and quiet escape (rescuing a failing required gate by re-filing it as "out of scope"). The excluded sectors — quantum-gravity UV completion, full cosmology, dark matter, dark energy, baryogenesis, strong CP — are listed in Section 9, and the binding rule of §1.2.1 holds: **required gates cannot be closed by exclusion.**

**Precise.** The gate's certificate is a *scope-consistency lint over the manuscript itself*, with two clauses: (i) every excluded sector appears explicitly in the Section 9 boundary ledger; (ii) no required-gate certificate (Gates 1–10, Section 6 cards and Appendices D–L) cites an excluded sector for closure or carries the status *Excluded from scope*. Both clauses are mechanical text checks — scriptable and falsifiable, which is the point: the claim boundary is enforced by a program, not by authorial restraint.

```text
Claim: Every excluded sector is declared in Section 9, and no required gate is closed by exclusion or carries an exclusion status.
Mechanism: Scope-consistency lint executed over the manuscript text.
Why it matters: Overclaiming damages credibility even when the core construction is sound; closure-by-exclusion would be fraud by filing.
Closure location: Section 9 (boundary ledger); §1.2.1 (binding rule).
Certificate location: certificates/G11_claim_boundary/ (lint run against the manuscript file; PASS recorded).
Status: Claimed certificate pass.
Falsifier: An excluded sector missing from Section 9, or any required-gate closure text citing an excluded sector.
```

No toy model (nothing to demonstrate). Consumes the status outputs of Gates 1–10; nothing consumes it — it is the manuscript's outermost honesty layer.

---

# 6. Gate-by-Gate Certificate Review

**Claim strength:** Certificate claim, not theorem-level proof.

Every required gate has a frozen object, a certificate claim, a falsification path, and a downgrade rule.

This manuscript does not ask the reader to accept that Gates 1–10 are finally closed in nature. It claims that, under the declared search category, primitive anchors, frozen active branch, and gate-specific assumptions, each required scoped-GUT gate has a certificate sufficient for hostile review.

Gate 11 is different: it is the claim-boundary gate. It does not close additional physics. It states which non-GUT sectors are excluded from the submitted claim.

Each gate below carries its seven-line **gate card** — frozen objects, output, failure mode, verification, status. The requirement narrative, mechanism, plain-language development, and search history for each gate live in its matching Section 5 constraint module (Gate 1 in Sections 2–2B); the machine-reproduction folder for each gate is named on its pointer line; the consolidated falsification paths and downgrade rules are Section 6.12.

The active branch may claim scoped-GUT certificate closure only if every required Gate 1–10 is assigned an approved status label — *Claimed certificate pass* or *Certificate-complete under declared assumptions* — and each such label is backed by a falsifiable certificate and reproduction pointer. Section 6.12 (Gate-by-Gate Falsification Map) summarises the falsification path and downgrade rule for each gate; the per-gate subsections below carry the certificate evidence.


## 6.0 Master Gate Status

The full gate roster is summarized here; each row is unpacked below.

| Gate | Requirement | Active-branch response | Status | Certificate |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1. Geometry specification | The submitted active branch must be a specific, frozen three-layer object — base ($\times$), finite ($\oplus$), tensor ($\otimes$) — with every primitive object in A0 and every derived object reconstructed in A1–A3 | Submitted active branch $\mathfrak{B}_{\rm active}$ of Section 2.2.1, frozen as R1 manifest meta-hash `a5b1e6f9d951` and reconstructed across A1 / A2 / A3 / B2 | *Claimed certificate pass* | Appendices A0 / A1 / A2 / A3 / B2 |
| 2. Gauge recovery | Recover $SU(3)_c \times SU(2)_L \times U(1)_Y$ at low energy | $K_{\rm gauge} = K_6 \times S^2 \times S_Y^{\,1}$ delivers the SM gauge algebra as the surviving compact-factor isometry | *Claimed certificate pass* | [Appendix D](#appendix-d-standard-model-recovery) |
| 3. Hypercharge and electric charge | Correct $Y$ and $Q = T_3 + Y$ on every multiplet | $S_Y^{\,1}/\mathbb{Z}_2$ projection plus global $\mathbb{Z}_6$ identification fixes the charge embedding | *Claimed certificate pass* | [Appendix D](#appendix-d-standard-model-recovery) |
| 4. Chirality and no mirrors | Three chiral generations, no surviving mirror partners | Spin-$\mathbb{C}$ index on $K_6$ returns family count $-3$; orbifold projection on $S_Y^{\,1}$ removes mirrors | *Claimed certificate pass* | [Appendix E](#appendix-e-chirality-and-anomaly-closure) |
| 5. Anomaly cancellation | Vanishing gauge and gauge–gravity anomalies on the surviving spectrum | Anomaly ledger across $SU(3)^3$, $SU(2)^3$, $U(1)_Y^3$, and mixed anomalies | *Claimed certificate pass* | [Appendix E](#appendix-e-chirality-and-anomaly-closure) |
| 6. Stabilization | Declared stabilization witnesses for the relevant moduli | Compactification potential / witness data covers every modulus used downstream; moduli-controlled under admissibility + local/phenomenological sufficiency assumptions; not claimed as full global stabilization | *Claimed certificate pass* | [Appendix F](#appendix-f-stabilization) |
| 7. Threshold unification | Finite, frozen threshold corrections matching measured couplings | KK-threshold spectrum + frozen RG scheme; residual at the numerical-pipeline floor | *Claimed certificate pass* | [Appendix G](#appendix-g-threshold-unification) |
| 8. Higgs protection | Higgs mass protected by structural mechanism, not tuned counterterms | Wilson-line Higgs with integer winding count on $K_{\rm gauge}$ | *Claimed certificate pass* | [Appendix H](#appendix-h-higgs-protection) |
| 9. Flavor closure | Quark, charged-lepton, and neutrino masses and mixings from frozen operators | $F^+$ chamber: 2 declared anchors → frozen $Y_u, Y_d, Y_e, Y_\nu$ → 19 or more independent flavor outputs (parameter-counted compression) | *Certificate-complete under declared assumptions* | Appendices I / J / K |
| 10a. Proton safety — operator class | Dangerous declared baryon- and lepton-number-violating operator class absent or suppressed on the active branch | Dangerous declared operator class killed by sector projector identity $\Pi_q M \Pi_\ell = 0$; FCNC / mediator no-go theorem on the active branch | *Claimed certificate pass* | [Appendix L](#appendix-l-proton-safety) / C10 |
| 10b. Proton lifetime estimate | Numerical proton-lifetime estimate reported for context | Numerical lifetime computed from the operator basis and reported for context; not used as a hard closure claim | *Diagnostic only* | [Appendix L](#appendix-l-proton-safety) |
| 11. Claim boundary | Non-GUT sectors explicitly excluded | Quantum-gravity UV completion, full cosmology, dark matter, dark energy, baryogenesis, strong CP — none claimed | *Outside scoped-GUT claim* | Section 9 / [Appendix R0](#appendix-r0-freeze-certificates-and-code-hashes) |

The status labels are drawn from the approved Review Status Vocabulary in the front matter: *Claimed certificate pass*, *Certificate-complete under declared assumptions*, *Diagnostic only*, *Open / not claimed*, *Outside scoped-GUT claim*. No vague descriptors ("promising", "suggestive", "near-complete") are used.

**Per-term cross-index to [Appendix C](#appendix-c-term-by-term-construction-dossiers).** Every gate above is also indexed in [Appendix C](#appendix-c-term-by-term-construction-dossiers) dossier-by-dossier: C1 ($\mathcal M_4$) anchors the low-energy interpretation; C2 ($K_6$) supports Gates 2, 4, 5, 6, 7; C3 ($S^2$) supports Gates 2, 3, 5, 7; C4 ($S_Y^{\,1}/\mathbb{Z}_2$) supports Gates 3, 4, 5, 7; C5 ($F^+_{\rm finite}$) supports Gates 9 and the sector-orthogonality leg of Gate 10; C6 ($\mathcal C_{\rm admiss}$) supports Gates 1, 9, 10, 11 via discipline; C7 ($\mathcal E_{\rm matter}$) supports Gates 2, 3, 4, 5, 9; C8 ($\mathcal E_{\rm gauge}$) supports Gates 2, 5, 7; C9 ($\mathcal E_{\rm Higgs}$) supports Gate 8; C10 ($\mathcal E_{\rm proton}$) supports Gate 10. Each dossier records the freeze hashes, alternative-elimination ledger, and named failure mode for its term. A reviewer who wants to attack a gate at the term level — *which term's removal would open this gate?* — should read the corresponding C*x*.8 failure-if-removed table.

## 6.1 Gate 1 — Geometry Specification
**Narrative module:** Sections 2–2B (geometry) + §5.0 row 1.  **Machine certificate:** `certificates (R0 manifest reproducer)`

**Gate card.**
*Plain-language problem:* the manuscript must commit to a specific, frozen geometry object before any gate can be evaluated against it.
*Frozen objects:* $\mathfrak{B}_{\rm active}$ (R1 hash `dcc66f1b2685`); $K_6 = SU(3)/T^2$, $S^2$, $S_Y^{\,1}/\mathbb{Z}_2$ (R1.2); $F^+$ chamber and projectors (R1.4 + R1.6); manifest meta-hash `a5b1e6f9d951`.
*Output:* the layered active object that Gates 2–10 are evaluated against.
*Failure mode:* missing layer; missing primitive; layer-smuggling detected; hash mismatch.
*Verification:* A0 + A1 + A2 + A3 + B2; reproducer regenerates every hash.
*Status:* Claimed certificate pass.

**Certificate.** Appendices A0 + A1 + A2 + A3 + B2 + C (C1–C10 per-term dossiers). Reproducibility: [Appendix R0](#appendix-r0-freeze-certificates-and-code-hashes).

## 6.2 Gate 2 — Standard Model Gauge Recovery
**Narrative module:** §5.1.  **Machine certificate:** `certificates/G02_gauge_recovery/`

**Gate card.**
*Plain-language problem:* the low-energy world shows exactly three gauge forces — strong, weak, and hypercharge — and no extras.
*Frozen objects:* $K_{\rm gauge} = K_6 \times S^2 \times S_Y^{\,1}$ with $K_6 = SU(3)/T^2$; R1.2 / R1.4 isometry data.
*Output:* surviving gauge algebra $SU(3)_c \times SU(2)_L \times U(1)_Y$ with generators in the [Appendix D](#appendix-d-standard-model-recovery) basis and KK-mode → SM-multiplet map.
*Failure mode:* an extra unobserved gauge factor, or a missing SM factor, surviving in the low-energy isometry algebra.
*Verification:* [Appendix D](#appendix-d-standard-model-recovery); freeze ledger in [Appendix R0](#appendix-r0-freeze-certificates-and-code-hashes).
*Status:* Claimed certificate pass.

## 6.3 Gate 3 — Hypercharge and Electric Charge Recovery
**Narrative module:** §5.2.  **Machine certificate:** `certificates/G03_charge_z6/`

**Gate card.**
*Plain-language problem:* every observed particle has an integer-multiple-of-$1/6$ hypercharge, and electric charge follows $Q = T_3 + Y$ exactly.
*Frozen objects:* parent circle $S_Y^{\,1}$ with orbifold quotient $S_Y^{\,1}/\mathbb{Z}_2$; global $\mathbb{Z}_6$ identification of $SU(3) \times SU(2)$ centres; R1.4 hypercharge lattice.
*Output:* charge table for $Q_L, u_R, d_R, L_L, e_R, H$ as a geometric output, not a fit; $Q = T_3 + Y$ on every multiplet.
*Failure mode:* fractional-charge exotics or per-multiplet hypercharge fitting.
*Verification:* [Appendix D](#appendix-d-standard-model-recovery); A1.7 + A2.3.
*Status:* Claimed certificate pass.

## 6.4 Gate 4 — Chirality and No Surviving Mirror Fermions
**Narrative module:** §5.3.  **Machine certificate:** `certificates/G04_chirality/`

**Gate card.**
*Plain-language problem:* the world shows three chiral generations and no mirror partners at LEP / Tevatron / LHC energies.
*Frozen objects:* Borel–Weil–Bott family index on $K_6$ returning $-3$; Atiyah–Singer–Patodi index on $S_Y^{\,1}/\mathbb{Z}_2$ returning $(n_L, n_R) = (+3, 0)$ for the relevant bundles.
*Output:* exactly three chiral generations; mirror sector projected out at the boundary; surviving chiral spectrum matches the SM.
*Failure mode:* surviving vectorlike pairs, or family count $\neq 3$.
*Verification:* [Appendix E](#appendix-e-chirality-and-anomaly-closure); orbifold freeze R1.3 `ac4d2df3e708`.
*Status:* Claimed certificate pass.

## 6.5 Gate 5 — Anomaly Cancellation
**Narrative module:** §5.4 → Section 3 (worked deep example).  **Machine certificate:** `certificates/G05_anomaly_cancellation/`

**Gate card.**
*Plain-language problem:* the SM is quantum-consistent — all triangle and gauge–gravity anomalies cancel on the observed fermion content.
*Frozen objects:* surviving chiral spectrum from Gate 4; anomaly traces over $SU(3)^3$, $SU(2)^3$ (Witten), $U(1)_Y^3$, mixed gauge, and gauge–gravity contributions.
*Output:* vanishing anomaly ledger entry-by-entry; SM cancellation pattern recovered without adding content after the spectrum is fixed.
*Failure mode:* any nonvanishing trace — uncancelled anomaly = quantum-inconsistent branch.
*Verification:* [Appendix E](#appendix-e-chirality-and-anomaly-closure); matter content A2.3; full derivation Appendix E′.
*Status:* Claimed certificate pass.

## 6.6 Gate 6 — Stabilization
**Narrative module:** §5.5.  **Machine certificate:** `certificates/G06_stabilization/`

**Gate card.**
*Plain-language problem:* a predictive compactification cannot have its shape and size drifting freely; every modulus used downstream must be pinned.
*Frozen objects:* Weyl-rigid $K_6$ chamber; modular fixed point $\tau = \omega$; integer Wilson-line winding on $K_{\rm gauge}$; declared witnesses for $S^2$, $S_Y^{\,1}$, and Cartan-torus completion of $F^+$.
*Output:* list of stabilized moduli with witnesses; declared band on any residual modulus; freeze record.
*Failure mode:* an unfixed modulus used downstream — any output becomes a hidden function of it.
*Verification:* [Appendix F](#appendix-f-stabilization).
*Status:* Claimed certificate pass.

## 6.7 Gate 7 — Threshold Unification
**Narrative module:** §5.6.  **Machine certificate:** `certificates/G07_thresholds/`

**Gate card.**
*Plain-language problem:* the three SM gauge couplings, run upward, must meet at a single high-energy scale once finite threshold corrections are included.
*Frozen objects:* KK spectrum of $K_{\rm gauge}$ under the declared regulator; threshold vector $(\delta_1, \delta_2, \delta_3) = (+4.8424, -3.1112, -1.7313)$; RG-transport equations and comparison scale at $M_Z$.
*Output:* unification scale $M_U \sim 10^{16}$ GeV; residual at the numerical-pipeline floor ($\sim 10^{-8}$), inside the PDG band ($\sim 10^{-3}$).
*Failure mode:* threshold corrections arbitrary, scheme-dependent in a post-hoc-adjustable way, or unable to unify the couplings.
*Verification:* [Appendix G](#appendix-g-threshold-unification); certificates/appendix_F_*.csv; threshold output A1.11.
*Status:* Claimed certificate pass.

## 6.8 Gate 8 — Higgs Protection
**Narrative module:** §5.7.  **Machine certificate:** `certificates/G08_higgs_protection/`

**Gate card.**
*Plain-language problem:* the Higgs is light compared to the unification scale; this lightness must be structural, not the result of a tuned counterterm.
*Frozen objects:* Wilson-line / Hosotani Higgs on the named cycle of $K_{\rm gauge}$; one Higgs doublet $n_H = 1$; integer winding count; Berezin–Kontsevich coefficient $\eta_{BK} = 0.009721281516312024$.
*Output:* predicted electroweak VEV $v = 246.02$ GeV and Higgs mass $m_h = 123.82$ GeV with declared bands; one structural source for two outputs ($v$ and $\lvert y_t/y_b\rvert$).
*Failure mode:* high-scale scalar mass sensitivity reintroduced through a counterterm.
*Verification:* [Appendix H](#appendix-h-higgs-protection); protection freeze A1.12.
*Status:* Claimed certificate pass.

## 6.9 Gate 9 — Flavor Closure
**Narrative module:** §5.8.  **Machine certificate:** `certificates/G09_flavor/`

**Gate card.**
*Plain-language problem:* quark, charged-lepton, and neutrino masses and mixings show structured hierarchies that any GUT must reproduce from frozen operators, not from per-observable fits.
*Frozen objects:* $F^+$ chamber operators $O_u, O_d, O_e, O_\nu$ from declared geometric data; two anchors $y_t$ and $\lvert V_{us}\rvert$; frozen Yukawa maps $Y_u, Y_d, Y_e, Y_\nu$.
*Output:* within-sector quark hierarchies, off-diagonal CKM magnitudes, $\delta_{\rm CKM}$ / Jarlskog, charged-lepton mass ratios, and neutrino quantities — 19+ independent frozen outputs from 2 declared inputs.
*Scope (binding):* flavor is a required scoped-GUT gate; it cannot be deferred to future work or moved into the Section 9 boundary list — if any flavor sector is incomplete, the gate drops to *Pending — not used in claim* and the complete-scoped-GUT claim weakens accordingly (§1.2.1).
*Failure mode:* inserting arbitrary Yukawa matrices, deferring flavor, or mixing inputs and outputs.
*Verification:* Appendices I / J / K; certificates/appendix_I_*.csv; certificates/appendix_J_*.csv; chamber freeze R1.6, anchors R1.8.
*Status:* Certificate-complete under declared assumptions.

## 6.10 Gate 10 — Proton Safety
**Narrative module:** §5.9.  **Machine certificate:** `certificates/G10_proton_safety/`

**Gate card.**
*Plain-language problem:* Super-Kamiokande has not seen proton decay; any GUT candidate must avoid the excluded $X$ / $Y$-mediator channels.
*Frozen objects:* compact-factor structure of $K_{\rm gauge}$ (no embedding into a single simple GUT group with heavy mediators); FCNC / mediator no-go theorem on the active branch; sector projector identity $\Pi_q M \Pi_\ell = 0$.
*Output:* operator basis with selection rules removing dangerous baryon- and lepton-number-violating channels; separately, a diagnostic lifetime prediction with bound comparison.
*Failure mode:* a surviving dangerous operator with unsuppressed coefficient, or a closure claim resting on the numerical lifetime.
*Verification:* [Appendix L](#appendix-l-proton-safety); FCNC no-go R1.6 `fff4b433b7b3`; operator-class `551488d06011`; projectors A2.8.
*Status:* Claimed certificate pass (operator level); Diagnostic only (lifetime).

## 6.11 Gate 11 — Claim Boundary
**Narrative module:** §5.10.  **Machine certificate:** `certificates/G11_claim_boundary/`

**Gate card.**
*Plain-language problem:* the manuscript must say out loud which sectors it does not claim — and may not rescue a failing required gate by exclusion (§1.2.1 binding rule).
*Frozen objects:* boundary ledger (Section 9; [Appendix R0](#appendix-r0-freeze-certificates-and-code-hashes)); excluded-sector list — quantum-gravity UV completion, full cosmology, dark matter, dark energy, baryogenesis, strong CP.
*Output:* every excluded sector named with status *Outside scoped-GUT claim*; a scope-consistency lint over the manuscript text verifying both clauses.
*Failure mode:* an excluded sector missing from Section 9, or any required Gate 1–10 closed by exclusion or invoking an excluded sector for closure.
*Verification:* Section 9 + R0 boundary ledger; `certificates/G11_claim_boundary/`.
*Status:* Claimed certificate pass (the boundary holds; the listed sectors are *Outside scoped-GUT claim*).

## 6.12 Gate-by-Gate Falsification Map

For each required Gate 1–10, this table summarises the **falsification path** (how a reviewer can attack the gate) and the **downgrade rule** (what happens when the attack succeeds). The downgrade rules are binding per the Downgrade Rules section in the front matter.

| Gate | Requirement | Certificate claim | Falsification path | Downgrade if falsified |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Gate 1 — Geometry / search category** | Active branch is fully specified; declared search category contains it | *Claimed certificate pass under declared search category* | Show the active branch is under-defined, inconsistent, or missing a load-bearing term; OR show the declared search category excludes a natural competitor without justification | *Open / not claimed* (under-defined branch) or *Category-relative diagnostic* (search-category attack) |
| **Gate 2 — Gauge recovery** | Surviving 4D gauge algebra matches $\mathfrak{su}(3) \oplus \mathfrak{su}(2) \oplus \mathfrak{u}(1)$ at the comparison scale | *Claimed certificate pass* | Show the surviving gauge algebra or representation content does not match the SM (e.g., extra $U(1)$, missing $SU(2)$, wrong rep content) | *Open / not claimed* |
| **Gate 3 — Hypercharge / electric charge** | Every SM multiplet receives the observed $Y$ and $Q = T_3 + Y$ | *Claimed certificate pass* | Show any multiplet receives the wrong $Y$ or $Q$; OR show the $\mathbb{Z}_6$ quotient identification is inconsistent with the surviving spectrum | *Open / not claimed* |
| **Gate 4 — Chirality / no mirrors / family count** | Three chiral families; no surviving mirror partners; integer index $\lvert \mathrm{Index}\rvert = 3$ | *Claimed certificate pass* | Show mirror modes survive the orbifold projection; OR show the spin-$\mathbb{C}$ index does not produce the integer family count; OR show the family count depends on a continuous moduli choice | *Open / not claimed* |
| **Gate 5 — Anomaly cancellation** | Every gauge / mixed / gravitational anomaly trace vanishes over the surviving chiral content | *Claimed certificate pass* | Show any anomaly trace ($[SU(3)]^2 U(1)_Y$, $[SU(2)]^2 U(1)_Y$, $[U(1)_Y]^3$, $[\mathrm{grav}]^2 U(1)_Y$) is non-zero on the active-branch spectrum | *Open / not claimed* |
| **Gate 6 — Stabilization** | Used compact moduli are stabilised at the Weyl-rigid chamber center under declared assumptions | *Claimed certificate pass under declared admissibility and moduli-control assumptions* | Show uncontrolled moduli remain that move a Gate 1–10 output beyond its tolerance; OR show "admissibility restriction" is mislabeled as "stabilization" (per [Appendix F](#appendix-f-stabilization)'s claim-type ledger) | *Diagnostic only* (if Hessian falsified) or *Open / not claimed* (if phenomenological sufficiency falsified) |
| **Gate 7 — Threshold unification** | Frozen threshold corrections close the three couplings at $M_U \sim 10^{16}$ GeV within the propagated PDG band | *Claimed certificate pass under frozen scheme, regulator, spectrum, and comparison scale* | Reproduce the threshold pipeline from [Appendix R0](#appendix-r0-freeze-certificates-and-code-hashes) and show a numerical mismatch; OR identify a hidden knob that was not in the No-Hidden-Knob Threshold Audit; OR show the regulator / scheme / spectrum / cutoff was adjusted after comparison | *Diagnostic only* if reproducible-discrepancy; *Open / not claimed* if hidden knob found |
| **Gate 8 — Higgs protection** | The Higgs mass is protected against a tree-level high-scale counterterm by the Wilson-line / winding mechanism | *Claimed certificate pass at one-loop within the protected sector; Diagnostic only at higher loops* | Show the Wilson-line construction does not protect the relevant mass term; OR exhibit a one-loop computation within the protected sector that gives $\delta m_H^2 \sim M_*^2$ residual; OR show an admissible operator bypasses the cycle-$\gamma$ topology | *Higgs protection mechanism candidate* (downgraded from *Claimed certificate pass*) |
| **Gate 9 — Flavor closure** | Two declared anchors ($y_t(M_Z), \lvert V_{us}\rvert$) generate $\geq 19$ frozen flavor outputs from the $F^+$ chamber without per-entry tuning | *Certificate-complete under declared assumptions* | Show $F^+$ uses hidden per-entry tuning (Flavor Lock Table row violated); OR show outputs do not follow from frozen operators (Anti-Fitting Ledger violated); OR show the chamber was selected using output observables | *Diagnostic only* (if Flavor Lock Table row violated) or *Open / not claimed* (if chamber selected post-hoc) |
| **Gate 10 — Proton safety (operator level)** | Every dangerous Wilson coefficient $C_i \in \mathcal{O}_{\rm danger}^{\rm declared}$ vanishes identically by the projector identity $\Pi_q M \Pi_\ell = 0$ | *Claimed certificate pass at the operator level; Diagnostic only at the lifetime level* | Show a physically relevant operator outside the declared class; OR show the sector projectors are not orthogonal on the active-branch matter bundle; OR identify a covered operator class whose mechanism fails | *Open / not claimed* (if operator outside class) or *Diagnostic only* (if mechanism fails) |
| **Gate 11 — Claim boundary** | Excluded sectors are not used to support required Gates 1 – 10 | *Claimed certificate pass* | Show any excluded sector (cosmology, dark matter, baryogenesis, strong-CP, quantum gravity) is invoked to support a required gate output | *Required gate downgrades* (the gate that improperly invoked the excluded sector) |

> **Binding rule.** A successful falsification of any row downgrades the affected gate per the front-matter Downgrade Rules. No row may be silently re-upgraded after a successful falsification; the manuscript must explicitly patch the certificate and record the patch in the freeze / migration ledger (A3) before the gate can return to *Claimed certificate pass*.

---

This table is the single auditable surface for Gate 1 – 11 falsification. The detailed per-gate cards earlier in Section 6 contain the certificate evidence; this table is the reviewer's attack-and-downgrade map.

## 6.13 Certificate-Status Summary

The $F^+$-augmented active branch has claimed certificate status for each required scoped-GUT gate under the declared assumptions.

Standard Model gauge recovery, hypercharge and electric charge recovery, chirality without mirror partners, and gauge / gauge–gravity anomaly cancellation are *Claimed certificate pass* on the surviving compact-factor isometry algebra and orbifold projection rules of $K_{\rm gauge}$. Compactification moduli-control, finite frozen threshold unification, and Wilson-line Higgs protection are *Claimed certificate pass* under their declared witnesses, schemes, and correction-class limits. Flavor closure is *Certificate-complete under declared assumptions* via the $F^+$ chamber, with two declared calibration anchors against a larger set of independent frozen quark, charged-lepton, and neutrino outputs. Proton safety is *Claimed certificate pass* at the operator-class level by the FCNC / mediator no-go theorem on the active branch, while the numerical lifetime estimate remains *Diagnostic only*.

The non-GUT sectors — quantum-gravity UV completion, full cosmology, dark matter, dark energy, baryogenesis, and strong CP — are *Outside scoped-GUT claim* and recorded in the boundary ledger.

The manuscript therefore presents a scoped GUT candidate with a frozen certificate chain. This status remains conditional on the mathematical sufficiency, freeze discipline, and reproducibility of the certificates referenced above. Every required gate has an explicit certificate claim, falsification path (Section 6.12), and downgrade rule (front-matter Downgrade Rules).

# 7. Flavor Closure

**Claim strength:** *Certificate-complete under declared assumptions* (two declared anchors generate 19+ frozen flavor outputs; per-entry tuning explicitly forbidden by the anti-fitting rulebook).

## 7.1 Why Flavor Has Its Own Section

The flavor sector is the one the manuscript would most naturally fail. A GUT candidate that recovers the gauge sector while leaving every Yukawa entry as a free input has converted unification into renaming; a candidate that defers flavor to "later work" while claiming completeness has misnamed its own scope. Sections 2–5 placed the flavor chamber inside the active branch and recorded the flavor gate as *Certificate-complete under declared assumptions*. This section gives the flavor result in compressed form: the chamber definition, the calibration inputs, the parameter ledger, the frozen Yukawa structure, and the per-sector observable summaries that the chamber produces post-freeze.

The discipline is unchanged from the constraint modules (§4.9; §5.8). Two numerical anchors are declared as calibration inputs and counted in the ledger; every other flavor quantity reported here is a frozen output of the chamber, computed under a fixed RG-transport rule at a fixed comparison scale, with a declared uncertainty band. The strength of the result is measured by over-determination — declared inputs strictly fewer than independent frozen outputs — not by zero-input claims the parameter ledger would not support.

## 7.2 The $F^+$ Chamber in Compressed Form

$F^+$ is the minimal flavor chamber required by §5.8 (with §2.3) to claim certificate closure for quark, charged-lepton, and neutrino flavor on the Standard-Model-routing backbone under the declared assumptions. Operationally, the chamber supplies:

1. **Generation-space structure.** Three families act on the chamber as the Borel–Weil–Bott family index of $K_6$ already produced, with no independent per-family multiplicity.
2. **Sector projectors.** Up-type vs. down-type, quark vs. lepton, charged-lepton vs. neutrino. The projectors are part of the geometry and are frozen at the same time as the chamber.
3. **Chamber operators.** The set $\{O_u, O_d, O_e, O_\nu\}$ acts on the projected mode basis. Each operator is a frozen geometric object — not a free $3 \times 3$ matrix.
4. **Phase data.** The chamber's phase structure — including the holonomy phase that produces the CP-violating CKM angle — is read from the geometry of the Cartan-torus completion at its fixed point of order three, rather than retuned after a CKM comparison.
5. **Yukawa map.** A declared deterministic procedure maps $\{O_u, O_d, O_e, O_\nu\}$ to the Yukawa matrices $\{Y_u, Y_d, Y_e, Y_\nu\}$. The map does not introduce per-entry free parameters; once the chamber operators are fixed, the Yukawa entries follow.
6. **Normalization and RG interface.** Sector-level normalization rules (no family-level free normalizations) and the RG-transport boundary conditions used to bring chamber outputs to the comparison scale.

The chamber as a whole is part of the active geometry $\mathcal{M}_{\rm GUT} = \mathcal{M}_4 \times K_{\rm gauge} \times F^+$ defined in Section 2; the full operator definitions, boundary conditions, and chamber coordinates are in [Appendix I](#appendix-i-flavor-chamber-f). From this section's standpoint the chamber is the input to a pipeline whose outputs are physical observables.

## 7.3 The Flavor Pipeline

The chamber produces flavor outputs through a single forward pipeline:

$$
(y_t,\;\lvert V_{us}\rvert) \;\xrightarrow{\text{calibration}}\; F^+ \;\xrightarrow{\text{frozen ops}}\; O_u, O_d, O_e, O_\nu \;\xrightarrow{\text{Yukawa map}}\; Y_u, Y_d, Y_e, Y_\nu \;\xrightarrow{\text{diagonalize \& RG}}\; \{m_q, V_{\rm CKM}, J_{\rm CKM}, m_\ell, U_{\rm PMNS}\}.
$$

The two declared anchors $y_t$ and $\lvert V_{us}\rvert$ enter at the calibration step; everything downstream of $F^+$ is a frozen output. The CKM matrix is obtained by diagonalization, $V_{\rm CKM} = U_u^\dagger U_d$, where $U_u$ and $U_d$ are themselves outputs of the frozen pipeline — not free unitaries. The CP-violating phase is read from the chamber's frozen phase data rather than from any post-comparison adjustment.

## 7.4 Calibration Inputs

Only two calibration anchors enter $F^+$: the heavy-sector scale $y_t(M_Z) = 0.9665$, which fixes the up-sector normalization $N_u$, and the mixing magnitude $\lvert V_{us}\rvert = 0.22436$, which fixes the chamber angle $\theta_F$ (R1.8; Appendix I.5). Both are declared before any other flavor quantity is loaded, count in the ledger below, and are excluded from the prediction count of Section 7.6. The fixing step itself — the two one-line equations, the cascade each releases, and the handcuff moment — is worked in full as its own section: **Section 8**.

## 7.5 Parameter Ledger

The parameter ledger below tracks every quantity used by the flavor pipeline, marking it as a declared input, a frozen output, or a derived quantity that does not enter the ledger.

| Quantity | Sector | Role | Declared input? | Frozen output? |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| $y_t(M_Z)$ | Quark / up sector | Heavy-sector anchor | Yes | No |
| $\lvert V_{us}\rvert$ | Quark / mixing | Mixing anchor | Yes | No |
| $m_t/m_c,\;m_c/m_u$ | Quark / up-sector within-sector hierarchy | Output | No | Yes |
| $m_b/m_s,\;m_s/m_d$ | Quark / down-sector within-sector hierarchy | Output | No | Yes |
| $m_t/m_b$ (between-sector ratio) | Quark | Output: certified $\lvert y_t/y_b\rvert(M_Z) = 57.50 \approx 58$ (pull $0.10$); $1/\eta_{BK} \approx 102.87$ is the raw determinant before $K_{tb}/R_{tb}$ running | No | Yes |
| $\lvert V_{cb}\rvert,\;\lvert V_{ub}\rvert$ | Quark / mixing | CKM magnitudes | No | Yes |
| $\lvert V_{cd}\rvert,\;\lvert V_{cs}\rvert$ | Quark / mixing | CKM magnitudes | No | Yes |
| $\lvert V_{td}\rvert,\;\lvert V_{ts}\rvert,\;\lvert V_{tb}\rvert$ | Quark / mixing | CKM magnitudes | No | Yes |
| $\delta_{\rm CKM}$ / $J_{\rm CKM}$ | Quark / CP | CP phase / Jarlskog | No | Yes |
| $m_e,\,m_\mu,\,m_\tau$ | Charged-lepton | Mass spectrum | No | Yes (subject to certificate status, [Appendix K](#appendix-k-lepton-neutrino-certificate)) |
| $\Delta m_{21}^2,\;\Delta m_{32}^2$ | Neutrino | Mass-squared splittings | No | Yes (subject to certificate status) |
| $\sin^2 \theta_{12},\;\sin^2 \theta_{13},\;\sin^2 \theta_{23}$ | Neutrino | PMNS mixing | No | Yes |
| Leptonic $\delta_{CP}$ | Neutrino | PMNS CP phase | No | Yes |

Two declared inputs against, in the quark sector alone, a substantially larger set of independent frozen outputs (within-sector hierarchies in both up and down sectors; eight independent CKM magnitudes other than $\lvert V_{us}\rvert$ — only four are independent after unitarity, but the chamber produces all magnitudes consistent with the unitarity constraint; the CP phase). Adding the charged-lepton and neutrino frozen outputs takes the count well past nineteen independent observables to two inputs. The compression strength tier of §5.8.3 (over-determination standard; §4.9) is therefore *very strong* (1–2 inputs producing nine or more independent outputs), not first-principles derivation and not zero-input — but well clear of reparameterization. The chamber is calibrated, then handcuffed. The declared anchors fix the chamber; after that, the Yukawa matrices must come out of frozen operators or the certificate fails.

**Flavor system interface.**
*Inputs:* two declared flavor anchors: $y_t(M_Z)$ and $\lvert V_{us}\rvert$ (R1.8 hashes `548d7099ef18` and `a1bc510bc7cd`). No charged-lepton anchor; no neutrino anchor.
*Frozen objects:* $F^+$ chamber + sector projectors $\Pi_u, \Pi_d, \Pi_e, \Pi_\nu$ (R1.4 `3b8d68559f5e`); chamber operators $O_u, O_d, O_e, O_\nu$ (R1.6 hashes `07be17dd8a1c`, `50ef768bb146`, `08ff25117d00`, `495ddbdcedb9`); Yukawa map procedure (R1.6 `1f20935643cf`); chamber angle $\theta_F$ (R1.6 `1ff57f48d45a`); $\tau = \omega$ (R1.6 `03b30a9c931a`); $N_u, N_d, N_e$ (R1.6 `20dc4e0b8220`); RG transport (R1.7 `f531205a9159`).
*Generated outputs:* $Y_u, Y_d, Y_e, Y_\nu$; six quark masses; nine CKM magnitudes; $\delta_{\rm CKM} = +60.0°$ (Wolfenstein-aligned; raw holonomy $-2\pi/3 = -120°$); $J_{\rm CKM} = 2.92 \times 10^{-5}$; three charged-lepton masses; $\Delta m^2_{21}$, $\lvert\Delta m^2_{31}\rvert$; three PMNS angles; $\delta_{CP}^{\,\ell} = 260.2°$.
*Verification record:* Appendices I / J / K + `certificates/appendix_I_quark_outputs.csv` + `certificates/appendix_J_lepton_neutrino_outputs.csv` (both byte-equal to printed tables) + [Appendix R0](#appendix-r0-freeze-certificates-and-code-hashes).
*Failure mode:* any Yukawa matrix entry inserted directly; any phase changed post-comparison; any anchor undeclared in R1.8.

## 7.6 Frozen Output Summary — Quark Sector

The quark-sector outputs computed by the frozen pipeline, before any output value is read against experiment, are:

| Quark-sector output | Pipeline form | Comparison status |
|---|---|---|
| Within-sector hierarchies | $m_{t,c,u}$ and $m_{b,s,d}$ from a single chamber angle $\theta_F$ acting on the frozen up- and down-sector operators | Within declared band; full ratios in [Appendix J](#appendix-j-quark-certificate) |
| Between-sector ratio $\lvert y_t/y_b\rvert$ | $\exp(2\beta_1^\star) = 1/\eta_{BK} \approx 102.87$ is the raw determinant (before $K_{tb}/R_{tb}$) from the same finite determinant that produces $v_{\rm pred}$ | The between-sector prediction is the certified $M_Z$ value $\lvert y_t/y_b\rvert(M_Z) = 57.50 \approx 58$ (pull $0.10$), obtained once $K_{tb}^{\rm crit}$ and $R_{tb}$ factors run the raw determinant to $M_Z$; full chain in [Appendix J](#appendix-j-quark-certificate) |
| $m_t$ | $m_t = y_t \cdot v/\sqrt{2}$ with $y_t(M_Z)$ the declared up-sector anchor | $y_t$ is the anchor and $m_t$ is that anchor expressed as a mass, so the 0.4% agreement with PDG (when the $y_t$ anchor is referenced) is an **anchor-consistency check**, not an independent postdiction (App I classifies this as "calibration for $m_t$"; App J states the 0.4% holds when the $y_t$ anchor is referenced). $m_t$ is therefore NOT counted among the independent frozen outputs. |
| CKM magnitudes other than $\lvert V_{us}\rvert$ | Diagonalization product $V_{\rm CKM} = U_u^\dagger U_d$ from frozen $Y_u, Y_d$ | Within declared band; magnitudes and signs in [Appendix J](#appendix-j-quark-certificate) |
| CP phase $\delta_{\rm CKM}$ | Read from the chamber's order-three holonomy phase | Emergent at $-2\pi/3 = -120°$ (raw holonomy); Wolfenstein-aligned value $+60.0°$ compared to PDG $65.5°$ at $0.79\sigma$ (10% structural precision); the sign is structural. *Convention: the raw chamber holonomy is $-2\pi/3 = -120°$; the value compared to PDG is the Wolfenstein-aligned $+60.0°$.* |
| Jarlskog invariant $J_{\rm CKM}$ | Computed from $V_{\rm CKM}$ and $\delta_{\rm CKM}$ | Output consistent with frozen $\delta_{\rm CKM}$ and CKM magnitudes |

Every entry in this table is a frozen output. None of them was used to choose, tune, or retroactively adjust any part of the chamber, the operators, the phase data, or the RG-transport rule. The full per-observable comparison band, the comparison-scale rule, and the certificate-pipeline hash that backs each line are in [Appendix J](#appendix-j-quark-certificate).

## 7.7 Frozen Output Summary — Charged-Lepton Sector

The charged-lepton sector is treated by the same chamber under sector-level projection, with the certificate evaluated as *Certificate-complete under declared assumptions* in [Appendix K](#appendix-k-lepton-neutrino-certificate). The output spectrum is the three charged-lepton masses

$$
m_e,\quad m_\mu,\quad m_\tau,
$$

generated from the chamber operator $O_e$ and the corresponding Yukawa map $Y_e$, with the same RG-transport rule and the same comparison scale as the quark outputs. The masses are produced as ratios with the same overall normalization rule as the up-sector and down-sector ratios; no charged-lepton anchor enters the ledger. The certificate status, comparison band, and explicit numerical comparison are recorded in [Appendix K](#appendix-k-lepton-neutrino-certificate).

## 7.8 Frozen Output Summary — Neutrino Sector

The neutrino sector is treated by the chamber operator $O_\nu$ under the declared assumptions, with the Dirac and Majorana data declared in the chamber definition of [Appendix I](#appendix-i-flavor-chamber-f) and the corresponding Yukawa / mass map written to [Appendix K](#appendix-k-lepton-neutrino-certificate). The outputs are:

- Solar and atmospheric mass-squared splittings $\Delta m_{21}^2$ and $\Delta m_{32}^2$;
- PMNS mixing angles $\sin^2 \theta_{12}$, $\sin^2 \theta_{13}$, $\sin^2 \theta_{23}$;
- Leptonic CP-violating phase $\delta_{CP}^{\ell}$, predicted from the chamber's second cycle's Berry-phase data, within the current NuFIT band.

The neutrino certificate of [Appendix K](#appendix-k-lepton-neutrino-certificate) is reported under the same status-label discipline as the quark certificate. Any neutrino quantity not currently certificate-complete is labeled *Pending — not used in claim* in [Appendix K](#appendix-k-lepton-neutrino-certificate) and is not used to support the closure of the flavor gate.

## 7.9 Compression Claim in Compact Form

The frozen-output count is summarized below; only outputs not used as calibration inputs appear.

| Sector | Independent frozen outputs | Calibration inputs |
|---|---|---|
| Quark / up within-sector | $m_t/m_c$, $m_c/m_u$ | — |
| Quark / down within-sector | $m_b/m_s$, $m_s/m_d$ | — |
| Quark / between-sector | $\lvert y_t/y_b\rvert(M_Z)$ | $m_t$ at $M_Z$ ($= y_t \cdot v/\sqrt{2}$; anchor-fixed via $y_t$, App I/J — anchor-consistency check, not an independent output) |
| Quark / mixing | $\lvert V_{cb}\rvert, \lvert V_{ub}\rvert, \lvert V_{cd}\rvert, \lvert V_{cs}\rvert, \lvert V_{td}\rvert, \lvert V_{ts}\rvert, \lvert V_{tb}\rvert$ | — |
| Quark / CP | $\delta_{\rm CKM}$ / $J_{\rm CKM}$ | — |
| Charged-lepton | $m_e, m_\mu, m_\tau$ | — |
| Neutrino | $\Delta m_{21}^2, \Delta m_{32}^2, \sin^2\theta_{12}, \sin^2\theta_{13}, \sin^2\theta_{23}, \delta_{CP}^\ell$ | — |
| **Total frozen outputs** | **19 or more** | **2 ($y_t$, $\lvert V_{us}\rvert$)** |

The compression strength is therefore *very strong* in the sense of the §5.8.3 tier table: two declared inputs producing nineteen or more independent frozen outputs. This is the operational meaning of the *Certificate-complete under declared assumptions* status assigned to Gate 9 in Section 6; the per-observable numerical certificate tables (model values, PDG comparison values, pulls) are filled in [Appendix J](#appendix-j-quark-certificate) (quark) and [Appendix K](#appendix-k-lepton-neutrino-certificate) (charged-lepton and neutrino), and every frozen chamber operator carries a SHA-256 content hash in the manifest of [Appendix R1](#appendix-r1-frozen-active-branch-parameter-manifest).

## 7.10 What the Flavor Result Is Not

The flavor result is not a zero-input derivation of the Standard Model Yukawa structure. The chamber uses two declared anchors, both counted, and the manuscript does not claim otherwise. The forbidden phrases in any flavor discussion are explicit: the manuscript does not assert *CKM solved*, *complete flavor theory achieved*, *full quark closure*, or *full flavor closure* unless and until the per-observable certificate evaluations in Appendices J and K upgrade individual rows to closure status; the current submission is parameter-counted compression of the flavor sector inside the same off-hard-gate scoping under which Gates 1–9 carry *Claimed certificate pass* status as hard-gate items.

The flavor result is also not arbitrary Yukawa fitting. The four operational differences from Yukawa-matrix fitting were listed in §5.8 and Appendix I.0: no free $3 \times 3$ matrices, diagonalization rather than insertion, frozen phase structure, and frozen RG transport. Every quark, charged-lepton, and neutrino quantity reported in this section is consistent with those four conditions; any quantity that is not is reported as *Diagnostic only* or *Pending — not used in claim* in the relevant appendix.

## 7.11 Appendix Pointer

The full $F^+$ chamber construction — operator definitions, projectors, phase data, normalization rules, RG-transport boundary conditions, and the chamber's compatibility with the closure of Gates 1–9 — is in [Appendix I](#appendix-i-flavor-chamber-f). The quark certificate, including the full input ledger, the per-observable post-freeze comparison band, and the operator-level audit for the within-sector hierarchies, the between-sector ratio, the CKM magnitudes, and the CP phase, is in [Appendix J](#appendix-j-quark-certificate). The charged-lepton and neutrino certificate, with per-observable status labels and the explicit treatment of any neutrino quantity not currently certificate-complete, is in [Appendix K](#appendix-k-lepton-neutrino-certificate). The pipeline-version hashes and the freeze records that back every entry of the parameter ledger are in [Appendix R0](#appendix-r0-freeze-certificates-and-code-hashes).

The reader should leave this section holding three things: two declared calibration inputs ($y_t$ and $\lvert V_{us}\rvert$) against nineteen or more independent frozen flavor outputs; a chamber that generates Yukawa matrices by diagonalization rather than by per-entry insertion; and a parameter ledger that makes the compression claim auditable. Section 8 works the fixing step in full; Section 9 records the boundary; Section 10 completes the scoped-GUT argument, and Section 11 adds the engineering coda (the geometry, used forward, builds a quantum computer — at simulated grade, changing no gate status).
