# Appendix C1 — $\mathcal{M}_4$ Dossier (Observed Spacetime) — Model Appendix

**Load-bearing role.** Authoritative source for the term-level necessity of $\mathcal{M}_4$ in the active branch per the Claim-to-Appendix Authority Map (front matter). Conflicts with other appendices on whether $\mathcal{M}_4$ is retained or with what failure-if-removed signature are resolved by this dossier.

**Downstream impact (per the Appendix Dependency Graph, front matter).** If this dossier's term-necessity claim is downgraded under the R2 Downgrade Rules — i.e., if a reviewer shows the term is removable or smuggled — every gate certificate that depends on $\mathcal{M}_4$ drops one rung in Section 6, and the dossier's failure-if-removed table (C1.8 or equivalent) records the propagation path. Gates affected: Gates 1, 7 (interpretation manifold + threshold comparison scale); also affects the Section 6.12 Falsification Map row for Gate 1.

**Purpose.** Apply the Section 3 construction recipe to the term $\mathcal{M}_4$ of the full active-branch object

$$
\mathfrak{B}_{\rm active} \;=\; [\mathcal{M}_4 \times K_6 \times S^2 \times S_Y^{\,1}] \;\oplus\; [F^+_{\rm finite} \oplus \mathcal{C}_{\rm admiss}] \;\otimes\; [\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter} \oplus \mathcal{E}_{\rm gauge} \oplus \mathcal{E}_{\rm Higgs} \oplus \mathcal{E}_{\rm proton}].
$$

This dossier extends the Section 3 worked example to the full Term-Dossier Template (C1.0–C1.15). It is the *observational-anchor appendix* of the [Appendix C](#appendix-c-term-by-term-construction-dossiers) series: every other Appendix-T term (C2 = $K_6$, C3 = $S^2$, C4 = $S_Y^{\,1}/\mathbb{Z}_2$, C5 = $F^+$, C6 = $\mathcal{C}_{\rm admiss}$, C7–C10 = matter / gauge / Higgs / proton bundles) is *consumed at* $\mathcal{M}_4$.

**Main-text references.** Section 2 (selected geometry); Section 2B.2.1 (three-layer rule, $\times$-base content); Section 2B.3 (why $\times$ alone is not enough); Section 3 (Term-Dossier Template); Section 3.4 (pattern summary); Section 6 (gate cards — every gate consumes a 4D comparison surface); Appendices A0 / A1 / A2 / A3 / B2 / L.

**Orientation.** This dossier explains *why* $\mathcal{M}_4$ exists in the active branch and *what fails* if it is removed. A0 / A1 / A2 / A3 / B2 / L control authority over what it *is*, where it is frozen, and how it is reproduced. $\mathcal{M}_4$ is the *external comparison surface* — the low-energy interpretation manifold on which every Standard Model output is read after Kaluza–Klein reduction. It is *not* derived as a cosmological solution; cosmology is Gate-11 excluded (Section 9). Per A1.1.2 row 1, $\mathcal{M}_4$ is the unique $\times$-layer factor whose role is *observational anchor* rather than *internal symmetry source*.

> **Key sentence (binding).** $\mathcal{M}_4$ is a *primitive observational anchor*, not a derived gauge-factor. It does not source any internal Yang–Mills group, does not stabilize via a moduli witness, and does not carry a Wilson-line / chamber datum. It is *given by observation*: the Standard Model is measured in four dimensions with mostly-plus Lorentz signature $(-,+,+,+)$, and the active branch must carry that surface — not derive it — for its certificates to be empirical statements.

---

## C1.0 One-Sentence Role

> $\mathcal{M}_4 = \mathbb{R}^{3,1}$ is retained because it is the *minimal external comparison manifold* inside the declared search category — four-dimensional, Lorentz-signature $(-,+,+,+)$, flat — on which every Standard Model output is *read after Kaluza–Klein reduction* from the 13D active branch; without $\mathcal{M}_4$ as a primitive observational anchor, the surviving low-energy effective theory has no target manifold, no asymptotic state space, no rest frame for the SM fields, and no comparison scale, and Gates 4 (chirality), 5 (stabilization defining the 4D EFT), 6 (threshold unification at the 4D scale $M_U$), 8 (Higgs protection in 4D EFT), and 10 (proton safety in 4D EFT) cannot be *posed*.

## C1.1 Plain-Language Picture

The active branch is a thirteen-dimensional product space. Four of those dimensions are the spacetime we observe — three spatial axes plus time, with Lorentz signature for causal light cones. The remaining nine dimensions are compact and very small ($\sim 10^{-33}\,{\rm m}$), and that is where the internal gauge structure of the Standard Model is hidden. $\mathcal{M}_4$ is the *visible part* — the surface on which detectors sit, on which $M_Z = 91.1876$ GeV is measured, on which the top Yukawa $y_t(M_Z) = 0.9665$ is read, on which $|V_{us}| = 0.22436$ is extracted, and on which every gate certificate eventually lands.

The plain-language reading-protocol framing of the manuscript distinguishes three "kinds of dimension":

| Manuscript framing | What kind of dimension | Carried by |
|---|---|---|
| "Where" — the place where things sit | external 3D space | $\mathcal{M}_4$ ($M_3$ component) |
| "When" — the temporal axis on which comparison happens | external 1D time | $\mathcal{M}_4$ ($M_1$ component) |
| "What exists" — what gauge / matter / flavor structure things have | internal 9D compact + chamber data | $K_6 \times S^2 \times S_Y^{\,1}/\mathbb{Z}_2 \times F^+$ |

$\mathcal{M}_4$ is the *where + when* surface; C2–C10 supply the *what exists*.

## C1.2 Exact Technical Object

**Exact notation.** $\mathcal{M}_4 = \mathbb{R}^{3,1}$, flat four-dimensional Minkowski spacetime equipped with the Lorentzian metric

$$
\boxed{\;ds_{\mathcal{M}_4}^2 \;=\; \eta_{\mu\nu}\,dx^\mu\,dx^\nu,\qquad \eta_{\mu\nu} \;=\; \mathrm{diag}(-1,\,+1,\,+1,\,+1).\;}
$$

The signature convention is **mostly-plus** $(-,+,+,+)$, consistent with the chirality-projector convention of A1.1.3 ($P_\chi = \tfrac{1}{2}(1 + \gamma_5\,\Gamma_8)$) acting on the Lorentzian Dirac spinor bundle $S_{3,1}$ of A2.2. Coordinates are $x^\mu = (x^0, x^1, x^2, x^3) = (t, \vec x)$; the isometry group is the inhomogeneous Lorentz / Poincaré group

$$
\mathrm{ISO}(3,1) \;=\; \mathbb{R}^{3,1} \rtimes \mathrm{SO}(3,1),
$$

acting globally on $\mathcal{M}_4$. This is the spacetime symmetry of the surviving low-energy theory — *not* a Yang–Mills gauge group (C1.12).

**Topology.** $\mathbb{R}^{3,1}$ is non-compact, connected, simply connected, contractible. Volume $\mathrm{Vol}(\mathcal{M}_4) = \infty$; Euler characteristic $\chi(\mathcal{M}_4) = 1$. No boundary, no quotient, no orbifold action. $\mathcal{M}_4$ is taken to be exactly flat ($R_{\mu\nu} = 0$ identically) and exactly Minkowskian; curving into $dS_4$ or $AdS_4$ would import a cosmological-constant or holographic claim outside the GUT scope (Gate 11 / Section 9 — see C1.5 row 2).

## C1.3 Observed Facts / Closure Requirements

| # | Observed fact or required gate | Empirical / structural source | Constraint imposed on the active branch |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | SM physics observed in 4D Lorentz-invariant spacetime at energies far below the GUT scale | All particle physics since 1932; LEP, LHC, Super-K, NuFIT 5.3 | The active branch must contain a 4D Lorentz-signature factor in its $\times$-layer as external comparison surface |
| 2 | Empirical referents (gauge couplings, masses, mixings) are PDG values at $M_Z = 91.1876$ GeV | PDG 2024; LEP electroweak fits | Output-comparison scale lives on a 4D Minkowski background; R1.7 `a6852c7a6b00` is a 4D quantity |
| 3 | Cluster decomposition, particle states, S-matrix defined on $\mathbb{R}^{3,1}$ | LSZ reduction; Haag–Ruelle scattering theory | Asymptotic states of every certificate defined on $\mathcal{M}_4$; $\mathfrak{B}_{\rm active}$ must factorise as $\mathcal{M}_4 \times (\text{compact internal})$ |
| 4 | Locality / microcausality: $[\mathcal{O}(x), \mathcal{O}(y)] = 0$ for spacelike $(x-y)^2 > 0$ | Microcausality of QED / QCD | 4D Lorentz signature $\mathrm{diag}(-1,+1,+1,+1)$; light-cone causal structure |
| 5 | Lorentz / Poincaré invariance of every observable comparison; no preferred frame | PDG; CPT; Michelson–Morley; Lorentz-violation bounds $\sim 10^{-15}$ | $\mathcal{M}_4$ flat; isometry $\mathrm{ISO}(3,1)$; no curving into $dS_4$, $AdS_4$ |
| 6 | SM fields propagate in 4D after KK reduction; no observed extra dimensions at present collider energies | LHC large-extra-dim searches; no resonances below $\sim 4$ TeV | $\mathcal{M}_4$ is *external* in the KK reduction $\mathcal{H}_{\rm total} \to \mathcal{H}_{\mathcal{M}_4} \otimes (\text{compact mode spaces})$ of A2.1 |
| 7 | R1.8 anchors ($M_{\rm Pl}$, $\alpha_i^{-1}(M_Z)$, $y_t(M_Z)$, $|V_{us}|$) are 4D quantities | R1.8 ledger; PDG | $\mathcal{M}_4$ supplies the manifold on which each anchor is defined; $M_Z$ is the $Z$-boson rest energy in the 4D asymptotic Hilbert space |

The seven constraints in the right column are the **operative constraint set for $\mathcal{M}_4$**. A candidate active branch without a $\mathbb{R}^{3,1}$ factor cannot stage Gates 2–10 *as comparison statements*; every gate certificate would be a statement in an internal space with no empirical referent.

## C1.4 Constraint Translation

The constraint translation for $\mathcal{M}_4$ is the *fact-to-base-factor* mapping:

> Fact 1 (SM observed in 4D) $\Rightarrow$ a 4D Lorentzian factor in the $\times$-layer of $\mathfrak{B}_{\rm active}$ ($\mathcal{M}_4 = \mathbb{R}^{3,1}$ as the first entry of $[\mathcal{M}_4 \times K_6 \times S^2 \times S_Y^{\,1}]$).
>
> Fact 2 (PDG values at $M_Z$) $\Rightarrow$ the comparison scale of R1.7 (`a6852c7a6b00`) lives on $\mathcal{M}_4$'s energy axis.
>
> Fact 3 (S-matrix / cluster decomposition) $\Rightarrow$ asymptotic structure factorises as $\mathcal{M}_4 \times (\text{compact internal})$; $\mathcal{H}_{\rm asymp} = \mathcal{H}_{\mathcal{M}_4} \otimes (\text{KK zero modes})$.
>
> Fact 4 (microcausality) $\Rightarrow$ Lorentz signature $\mathrm{diag}(-1,+1,+1,+1)$; $P_\chi$ inherits its Lorentzian meaning.
>
> Fact 5 (Poincaré invariance) $\Rightarrow$ flatness ($R_{\mu\nu} = 0$); isometry $\mathrm{ISO}(3,1)$; no curving.
>
> Fact 6 (no observed extra dimensions) $\Rightarrow$ $\mathcal{M}_4$ is *external* in the KK reduction; internal factors have $R_{\rm int} \sim 10^{-33}\,{\rm m}$.
>
> Fact 7 (anchors are 4D) $\Rightarrow$ $\mathcal{M}_4$ supplies the manifold on which $M_{\rm Pl}$, $\alpha_i(M_Z)$, $y_t(M_Z)$, $|V_{us}|$ are defined.

The formal selector $\mathcal{S}$ of [Appendix B1](#appendix-b1-constraint-selector-occams-razor-formalism).3 reads each constraint as a closure requirement; the elimination ledger of [Appendix B1](#appendix-b1-constraint-selector-occams-razor-formalism).6 records why every alternative below fails inside the declared search category.

$\mathcal{M}_4$ is the *only* $\times$-layer factor not pinned by a derived radius or moduli-stabilization equation: its role is observational. The radii $R_6, R_2, R_Y$ are derived from threshold-vector closure (R1.2 + A1.2); the modulus $\tau = \omega$ is pinned by the order-three modular fixed point (R1.6 `03b30a9c931a`); the $\mathbb{Z}_2$, $\mathbb{Z}_6$ quotients are pinned by chirality / charge gates. $\mathcal{M}_4$ is *primitive observational*, not *derived geometric*.

The active branch chooses **not** to derive $\mathcal{M}_4$ from a cosmological equation (importing dark-energy and inflationary sectors is Gate-11-excluded), **not** to curve $\mathcal{M}_4$ into $dS_4$ / $AdS_4$ (cosmological-constant / holographic claim outside GUT scope), and **not** to extend $\mathcal{M}_4$ into a higher-dimensional non-compact background (brane-world warping outside GUT scope).

## C1.5 Alternatives Considered and First-Failed Gate

| # | Candidate alternative | Why considered | First failed constraint | Reason eliminated (proof tier per B2.0.2) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Non-4D background ($\mathbb{R}^{2,1}$ or $\mathbb{R}^{4,1}$ low-energy) | Naive enlargement / reduction | Constraint 1 (4D); Gate 1 | SM observed in 4D; certificate comparisons cannot be evaluated against PDG; asymptotic Hilbert space has wrong dimension for S-matrix interpretation; 3D Lorentz group $SO(2,1)$ cannot carry $(1/2,0)+(0,1/2)$ spinor reps. **Tier CI**. *Eliminated.* |
| 2 | $dS_4$ as low-energy background | Curvature-corrected frame ($\Lambda > 0$) | Constraint 5; Gate 11 (claim boundary) | Promotes cosmological-constant claim into GUT scope; cosmology is Gate-11 excluded (Section 9); violates freeze-before-compare barrier B.5. **Tier OX** (out-of-scope boundary exclusion). *Eliminated.* |
| 3 | $AdS_4$ as comparison background | Holographic context (AdS/CFT) | Gate 11 (claim boundary) | Same boundary argument; conformal boundary of $AdS_4$ is not the observed asymptotic structure of the SM. **Tier OX**. *Eliminated.* |
| 4 | Compactified Euclidean $T^4$ | Lattice / Wick-rotated framework | Constraint 4; Gate 4 (chirality), Gate 7 (anomaly) | Euclidean signature is a computational tool, not the observed background; $\gamma_5$ in $P_\chi$ has different meanings under Euclidean / Lorentzian Clifford algebras; chirality / anomaly statements require Lorentzian signature for unitary interpretation. **Tier CI**. *Eliminated.* |
| 5 | Foliated 3+1 split (ADM-style canonical frame) | Hamiltonian framework | Constraint 5; Gate 1 (manifest covariance) | Explicit time-foliation breaks manifest Lorentz invariance; certificate outputs would not be frame-independent. ADM is a valid *computational* foliation, not a *base manifold* choice. **Tier SI**. *Eliminated.* |
| 6 | Higher-dimensional Lorentzian (5D / 11D) with $\mathcal{M}_4$ as brane | Randall–Sundrum / DGP context | Constraint 6; Gate 11 | Imports warping / brane-tension data; active branch claims no extended-gravity sector. **Tier OX**. *Eliminated.* |
| 7 | Non-orientable 4D manifold | Topological exotic background | Constraint 5; Gate 4 (chirality) | $\Gamma_5$ globally undefined on non-orientable base; $P_\chi$ has no global meaning; spin-$\mathbb{C}$ data of R1.4 `0fd19c9ae0c1` cannot consistently couple to a non-orientable base. **Tier CI**. *Eliminated.* |
| 8 | 4D Minkowski with Lorentz-violation operators ($c \neq 1$) | Test-of-Lorentz-invariance candidate | Constraint 5 | Modern bounds $|c-1| < 10^{-15}$ exclude observable violation at certificate level $\sim 10^{-3}$; anchors become frame-dependent. **Tier CI**. *Eliminated.* |

Every alternative is eliminated by a named first-failed-constraint route, tied to a B2.0.2 proof tier (SI = structural impossibility, CI = certificate impossibility, OX = out-of-scope boundary exclusion). The retained construction is:

$$
\boxed{\;\mathcal{M}_4 \;=\; \mathbb{R}^{3,1},\qquad \eta_{\mu\nu} \;=\; \mathrm{diag}(-1, +1, +1, +1),\qquad \mathrm{ISO}(3,1) \;=\; \mathbb{R}^{3,1} \rtimes \mathrm{SO}(3,1).\;}
$$

## C1.6 Selected Object and Minimality Argument

**Exact notation (binding).** $\mathcal{M}_4 = \mathbb{R}^{3,1}$, flat 4D Minkowski with signature $(-,+,+,+)$ and metric $\eta_{\mu\nu} = \mathrm{diag}(-1,+1,+1,+1)$. Coordinates $x^\mu = (t, x, y, z)$.

**Layer classification.** *Pure $\times$ (base / metric geometry).* No $\oplus$-rulebook content of its own; no native $\otimes$-content (serves as codomain manifold for $\otimes$-bundles after KK reduction — see C1.7).

**Primitive / derived status.** *Primitive observational anchor.* This is the binding statement: $\mathcal{M}_4$ is not derived from any stabilization equation, moduli witness, threshold equation, or chamber operator. Its presence is required by *observation* — A1.1.2 row 1 ("layer $\times$, metric yes, adds dimension $+4$, primitive"). Per A3 row 13, $\mathcal{M}_4$ ("$\mathcal{M}_{3,1}$") is *Retained* with no migration label — no historical absorbed / superseded counterpart, because $\mathcal{M}_4$ has always been the external comparison surface.

**Minimality argument.** $\mathcal{M}_4 = \mathbb{R}^{3,1}$ is minimal in three precise senses among candidate external comparison surfaces:

1. **Dimension minimality.** Four is the minimum dimension for a Lorentzian manifold supporting the observed Lorentz group $SO(3,1)$ acting non-trivially; three dimensions ($\mathbb{R}^{2,1}$, $SO(2,1)$) cannot carry the $(1/2,0)+(0,1/2)$ Lorentz spinor reps of the SM fermions; five+ dimensions are eliminated by C1.5 rows 1 + 6.
2. **Curvature minimality.** Flat ($R_{\mu\nu} = 0$) is the minimum geometric structure for an *observational* manifold; any non-zero curvature requires a cosmological-constant claim or a curvature-source field, both Gate-11-excluded.
3. **Symmetry minimality.** Isometry $\mathrm{ISO}(3,1)$ is the minimum symmetry compatible with global Lorentz + translation invariance; broken-boost / foliated splits (C1.5 row 5) cannot stage frame-independent comparison statements.

**Crucial distinction (binding).** $\mathcal{M}_4$ is *not chosen* to source any internal symmetry. The SM gauge group $G_{\rm SM} = (SU(3)_c \times SU(2)_L \times U(1)_Y)/\mathbb{Z}_6$ is sourced by the *internal* factors $K_6$ ($SU(3)_c$), $S^2$ ($SU(2)_L$), $S_Y^{\,1}/\mathbb{Z}_2$ ($U(1)_Y$). $\mathcal{M}_4$ is the *observational anchor* — *given* by observation, not *derived* as a gauge-source factor. C1.12 enforces this with the formal smuggling check.

## C1.7 Full $\times / \oplus / \otimes$ Decomposition

| Layer | Contribution of $\mathcal{M}_4$ | Adds metric dimensions? | Full record |
|---|---|:---:|---|
| $\times$ | $\mathcal{M}_4 = \mathbb{R}^{3,1}$; Lorentzian metric $\eta_{\mu\nu} = \mathrm{diag}(-1,+1,+1,+1)$; flat ($R_{\mu\nu} = 0$); $\mathrm{Vol}(\mathcal{M}_4) = \infty$; $\chi(\mathcal{M}_4) = 1$; isometry $\mathrm{ISO}(3,1) = \mathbb{R}^{3,1} \rtimes \mathrm{SO}(3,1)$; coordinates $x^\mu = (t, \vec x)$ | **yes** ($+4$) | A1.1.2 row 1; A1.9 ($D = 4+6+2+1 = 13$) |
| boundary / quotient | None — no quotient action, no boundary, non-compact, orientable | no | — |
| $\oplus$ | None native — $\mathcal{M}_4$ accepts standard 4D physics and has no finite admissibility rulebook of its own. The comparison scale $M_Z$ (R1.7 `a6852c7a6b00`), RG transport rule (R1.7 `f531205a9159`), uncertainty rule (R1.7 `61b0d93507e7`) are $\oplus$-objects living *on* $\mathcal{M}_4$'s energy axis but part of $\mathcal{C}_{\rm admiss}$ — *named cross-layer dependency* (C1.12), not a smuggle | no | (empty); R1.7; C6 cross-reference |
| $\otimes$ | **Codomain manifold** for every $\otimes$-bundle after KK reduction: (i) 4D Dirac spinor bundle $S_{3,1}$ over $\mathcal{M}_4$ in $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ (A2.3); (ii) 4D gauge connection $A_\mu^a(x)$ on $\mathcal{M}_4$ in $\mathcal{E}_{\rm gauge}$ (A2.4 — $E_{\rm gauge} = T^*(\mathcal{M}_4) \otimes \mathrm{ad}(P)$); (iii) 4D Higgs scalar $H(x)$ on $\mathcal{M}_4$ in $\mathcal{E}_{\rm Higgs}$ (A2.5); (iv) 4D effective four-fermion operators on $\mathcal{M}_4$ (the domain of $\mathcal{E}_{\rm proton}$ — A2.8); (v) propagators / asymptotic states $\mathcal{H}_{\rm asymp} = \mathcal{H}_{\mathcal{M}_4} \otimes (\text{KK zero modes})$ on which Gates 2–10 are compared | no | A2.1 (Hilbert decomposition); A2.2 (spinor bundle); A2.3, A2.4, A2.5, A2.8 |

**Notational consistency.** $\mathcal{M}_4$ contributes $+4$ to the propagating metric-dimension count $D = 4 + 6 + 2 + 1 = 13$ (A1.9). $F^+$ contributes $0$ (non-metric per A1.13 + A3.7 Option B). The $\otimes$-entries (4D spinors, gauge connection, Higgs) are *fibers over* $\mathcal{M}_4$, not extra dimensions.

**Local instance of the global B2 result.** $\mathcal{M}_4$ is the canonical $\times$-only base factor — no $\oplus$ or $\otimes$ content of its own. This makes it the *trivial calibration case* of the three-layer rule: a reader who treats the active branch as "just $\mathcal{M}_4$" commits to a $\times$-only reading; the three-layer rule then says $\times$-alone is insufficient (B2.3), so additional internal factors and bundle / chamber content are required. C1 anchors the calibration; C2–C10 supply the additional layered content.

## C1.8 All Math / Show-Your-Work Ladder

This section is the **math-readability ladder** for $\mathcal{M}_4$. It follows the seven-block format: a Symbol Dictionary, the Required Input Facts, the Construction Steps, the Calculations / Identities / Maps, the Outputs Produced, the Gate Consumption, and the Removal Failure. Every formula is preceded by a one-sentence statement of purpose and followed by a one-sentence plain-English interpretation, then a sentence naming the gate that consumes the result.

**Job label (binding).** $\mathcal{M}_4$ is the **comparison surface** of the active branch — the observed-spacetime stage on which every 4D effective-theory measurement is read after Kaluza–Klein reduction from 13D.

**Summary.** $\mathcal{M}_4$ is the ordinary four-dimensional stage where experiments happen; the compact geometry explains hidden particle structure, but predictions must return to this visible stage.

**Hostile-review sentence.** $\mathcal{M}_4$ is an observational primitive, not a derived internal gauge factor; it is retained because all scoped GUT certificates produce 4D effective observables.

### C1.8.1 Symbol Dictionary

No symbol may be used in this math ladder without appearing here first.

| Symbol | Plain-English meaning | Technical meaning | First used in |
|---|---|---|---|
| $\mathcal{M}_4$ | "the 4D world we observe" | flat 4D Lorentzian manifold $\mathbb{R}^{3,1}$ | C1.2, C1.8.4 (a) |
| $x^\mu$ | "a point in 4D spacetime" | coordinates $(x^0, x^1, x^2, x^3) = (t, x, y, z)$ on $\mathcal{M}_4$ | C1.8.4 (a) |
| $\eta_{\mu\nu}$ | "the rule that turns coordinate differences into proper-time / proper-distance" | flat Minkowski metric with mostly-plus signature $(-,+,+,+)$ | C1.8.4 (b) |
| $ds^2$ | "infinitesimal proper-time/distance squared" | the line element $\eta_{\mu\nu}\,dx^\mu\,dx^\nu$ | C1.8.4 (b) |
| $R_{\mu\nu}$ | "how curved spacetime is at a point" | Ricci tensor; $R_{\mu\nu} = 0$ identically on $\mathcal{M}_4$ | C1.8.4 (b) |
| $\mathrm{ISO}(3,1)$ | "all rigid motions of 4D Minkowski spacetime" | inhomogeneous Lorentz / Poincaré group $\mathbb{R}^{3,1} \rtimes \mathrm{SO}(3,1)$ | C1.8.4 (c) |
| $P_\mu, M_{\mu\nu}$ | "translations and rotations/boosts" | 10 Poincaré generators (4 translations + 6 Lorentz) | C1.8.4 (c) |
| $D$ | "total propagating metric dimension count of the active branch" | $D = \dim \mathcal{M}_4 + \dim K_6 + \dim S^2 + \dim(S_Y^{\,1}/\mathbb{Z}_2) = 13$ | C1.8.4 (d) |
| $X_{\rm int}$ | "the compact internal geometry (nine small extra dimensions)" | $X_{\rm int} = K_6 \times S^2 \times S_Y^{\,1}/\mathbb{Z}_2$, $\dim_{\mathbb{R}} X_{\rm int} = 9$ | C1.8.4 (e) |
| $\Phi(x^\mu, y^a)$ | "a generic 13D field separated into 4D and internal coordinates" | a section of a bundle over $\mathfrak{B}_{\rm active}$ | C1.8.4 (e) |
| $\phi_n(x^\mu)$ | "the 4D part of one Kaluza–Klein mode" | a 4D effective field on $\mathcal{M}_4$ | C1.8.4 (e) |
| $\psi_n(y^a)$ | "the internal-geometry shape of the $n$-th KK mode" | an orthonormal basis function on $X_{\rm int}$ | C1.8.4 (e) |
| $m_n$ | "the mass of the $n$-th KK mode" | $m_n \sim 1/R_{\rm int}$ for $n \geq 1$ (heavy); $m_0 = 0$ for the surviving 4D zero mode | C1.8.4 (e) |
| $\mathcal{H}_{\rm asymp}$ | "the space of all observable scattering states" | asymptotic Hilbert space; factorises into 4D part × KK-zero-mode part | C1.8.4 (e) |
| $M_Z$ | "the comparison energy at which the SM is measured" | $Z$-boson rest energy, $91.1876$ GeV | C1.8.4 (f) |
| $M_{\rm Pl}$ | "the 4D Planck mass" | $1.2209 \times 10^{19}$ GeV; one of the four declared anchors of R1.8 | C1.8.4 (g) |
| $M_*$ | "the fundamental 13D mass scale before Kaluza–Klein reduction" | $7.467050992135091 \times 10^{16}$ GeV (A1.10) | C1.8.4 (g) |
| $\mathrm{Vol}(X_{\rm active})$ | "the 9D volume of the compact internal geometry" | $3.704417261398702 \times 10^{-148}\,{\rm GeV}^{-9}$ (A1.3) | C1.8.4 (g) |
| $\gamma^\mu$, $\gamma_5$ | "4D Dirac matrices and 4D chirality matrix" | Clifford-algebra generators on the 4D spinor bundle $S_{3,1}$; $\gamma_5 = i\gamma^0\gamma^1\gamma^2\gamma^3$ with $\gamma_5^2 = +1$ | C1.8.4 (h) |
| $P_\chi$ | "the chirality projector that keeps only one handedness of fermions" | $P_\chi = \tfrac{1}{2}(1 + \gamma_5\Gamma_8)$ from A1.1.3 | C1.8.4 (h) |

### C1.8.2 Required Input Facts

| # | Input fact | Source | Why it matters |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Observed Standard Model physics happens in four-dimensional spacetime | All particle physics since 1932; LEP, LHC, Super-K, NuFIT 5.3 | Forces a 4D Lorentz-signature factor in the active branch |
| 2 | Lorentz signature is $(-,+,+,+)$ (mostly-plus), not Euclidean | Microcausality and unitarity of QED / QCD; modern Lorentz-violation bounds $\sim 10^{-15}$ | Pins the signature convention for chirality, anomaly, and asymptotic states |
| 3 | Spacetime is observed flat to good precision at lab scales | No detected curvature in particle experiments; PDG | Forces $R_{\mu\nu} = 0$; excludes $dS_4$ / $AdS_4$ from the GUT scope |
| 4 | Comparison values are quoted at $M_Z = 91.1876$ GeV | PDG; LEP electroweak fits | Pins the energy axis on which the active branch is compared |
| 5 | Cluster decomposition and the LSZ S-matrix hold | Haag–Ruelle scattering theory | Forces asymptotic Hilbert-space factorisation $\mathcal{H}_{\rm asymp} = \mathcal{H}_{\mathcal{M}_4} \otimes \mathcal{H}_{\rm KK\,zero}$ |
| 6 | No extra dimensions are observed at current collider energies | LHC large-extra-dimension searches | Forces $\mathcal{M}_4$ to be the **external** factor in the KK reduction; internal radii must be $\ll$ collider scale |
| 7 | The four declared R1.8 anchors ($M_{\rm Pl}$, $\alpha_i^{-1}(M_Z)$, $y_t(M_Z)$, $|V_{us}|$) are 4D measured quantities | R1.8 ledger; PDG | Forces $\mathcal{M}_4$ to supply the manifold on which every anchor is defined |

### C1.8.3 Construction Steps

The construction of $\mathcal{M}_4$ as the active branch's external comparison surface is the following algorithm. Each step has one sentence stating why it is allowed.

1. **Start with observation.** Take the empirical fact that all Standard Model measurements are performed in 4D Lorentz-signature spacetime (Input Fact 1). *Allowed because* the GUT submission is empirically anchored: anchors of R1.8 are 4D measured numbers.
2. **Place a 4D Lorentzian factor first in the $\times$-layer.** Add $\mathcal{M}_4 = \mathbb{R}^{3,1}$ as the first entry of the base product $[\mathcal{M}_4 \times K_6 \times S^2 \times S_Y^{\,1}]$. *Allowed because* the three-layer rule of Section 2B reserves the $\times$-layer for base / metric geometry, and $\mathcal{M}_4$ is the only proposed factor whose role is observational.
3. **Equip $\mathcal{M}_4$ with the mostly-plus Lorentz metric.** Set $\eta_{\mu\nu} = \mathrm{diag}(-1,+1,+1,+1)$ (Input Fact 2). *Allowed because* this is the signature under which microcausality and unitarity hold; alternatives (Euclidean signature) are computational tools, not base manifolds (see C1.5 row 4).
4. **Impose flatness.** Demand $R_{\mu\nu} = 0$ identically on $\mathcal{M}_4$ (Input Fact 3). *Allowed because* any non-zero curvature on $\mathcal{M}_4$ would import a cosmological-constant or graviton-source claim that is Gate-11-excluded by Section 9.
5. **Read off the dimension contribution.** $\mathcal{M}_4$ contributes $\dim_{\mathbb{R}}\mathcal{M}_4 = +4$ to the total dimension count $D$. *Allowed because* $\mathbb{R}^{3,1}$ is a 4-real-dimensional manifold by construction.
6. **Add the compact internal geometry.** Combine with the other three $\times$-factors to get the total active-branch base: $\mathfrak{B}_{\rm active}^{\,\times\text{-layer}} = \mathcal{M}_4 \times X_{\rm int}$ where $X_{\rm int} = K_6 \times S^2 \times S_Y^{\,1}/\mathbb{Z}_2$ (Input Fact 6). *Allowed because* Sections 4 and 5 (discovery logic) show this is the minimal admissible internal geometry that closes Gates 2–10.
7. **Perform the Kaluza–Klein reduction.** Decompose every 13D field $\Phi(x^\mu, y^a)$ into a basis $\Phi = \sum_n \phi_n(x^\mu) \psi_n(y^a)$. *Allowed because* $X_{\rm int}$ is compact, so its Laplacian has a discrete spectrum $\{\psi_n\}$.
8. **Keep only the 4D zero modes at low energy.** Retain $\phi_0(x^\mu)$ as the surviving 4D effective field on $\mathcal{M}_4$; integrate $\phi_n(x^\mu)$ for $n \geq 1$ out at the threshold gate. *Allowed because* heavy KK modes have $m_n \sim 1/R_{\rm int} \sim M_U \sim 10^{16}$ GeV ([Appendix G](#appendix-g-threshold-unification) threshold packet) — far above all collider energies (Input Fact 6).
9. **Read every SM observable on $\mathcal{M}_4$.** Anchor values, gate-certificate outputs, and S-matrix elements are all evaluated as 4D quantities on $\mathcal{M}_4$. *Allowed because* the four declared anchors of R1.8 (Input Fact 7) are 4D measured numbers; the comparison scale $M_Z$ (Input Fact 4) is a 4D rest-energy.

### C1.8.4 Calculation / Identity / Map

**(a) Coordinates on $\mathcal{M}_4$.**

*The purpose of the next formula is to give names to points in the 4D world.*

$$\boxed{\;x^\mu = (x^0, x^1, x^2, x^3) = (t, x, y, z),\qquad \mu \in \{0, 1, 2, 3\}.\;}$$

*This means each event is labelled by one time coordinate and three space coordinates.*

*This result is consumed by Gate 1 (geometry specification) because the active branch's $\times$-layer needs a coordinatised base factor before any gate certificate can be posed.*

**(b) Metric and line element.**

*The purpose of the next formula is to write down the rule that turns coordinate differences into measurable proper-time and proper-distance.*

$$\boxed{\;ds_{\mathcal{M}_4}^2 = \eta_{\mu\nu}\,dx^\mu\,dx^\nu,\qquad \eta_{\mu\nu} = \mathrm{diag}(-1,+1,+1,+1).\;}$$

Expanded: $ds_{\mathcal{M}_4}^2 = -dt^2 + dx^2 + dy^2 + dz^2$. The Ricci tensor vanishes identically: $R_{\mu\nu} = 0$.

*This means: the timelike direction enters with the opposite sign from the three spatial directions, which is what gives light cones, causal structure, and relativistic mass shells $E^2 - \vec p^{\,2} = m^2$.*

*This result is consumed by Gates 4 (chirality) and 7 (anomaly), because both require Lorentzian signature for $\gamma_5$ and the anomaly polynomial to have their physical (unitary) meaning.*

**(c) Isometry group.**

*The purpose of the next formula is to identify the global symmetry of $\mathcal{M}_4$.*

$$\boxed{\;\mathrm{ISO}(3,1) = \mathbb{R}^{3,1} \rtimes \mathrm{SO}(3,1).\;}$$

The Poincaré algebra has 10 generators: 4 translations $P_\mu$ and 6 Lorentz $M_{\mu\nu}$.

*This means: $\mathcal{M}_4$ is invariant under translations and Lorentz rotations / boosts — every 4D observable comparison is frame-independent.*

*This result is consumed by every gate, because all certificate outputs must be Poincaré-invariant scalars on $\mathcal{M}_4$. It also feeds the layer-smuggling check (C1.12): $\mathrm{ISO}(3,1)$ is a global spacetime symmetry, **not** a Yang–Mills gauge group.*

**(d) Dimension count of the active branch.**

*The purpose of the next formula is to show that $\mathcal{M}_4$ contributes exactly four metric dimensions out of the total thirteen.*

$$\boxed{\;D = \underbrace{\dim_{\mathbb{R}}\mathcal{M}_4}_{=4} + \underbrace{\dim_{\mathbb{R}} K_6}_{=6} + \underbrace{\dim_{\mathbb{R}} S^2}_{=2} + \underbrace{\dim_{\mathbb{R}}(S_Y^{\,1}/\mathbb{Z}_2)}_{=1} = 4 + 6 + 2 + 1 = 13.\;}$$

$F^+$ contributes $0$ (non-metric per A1.13 + A3.7 Option B).

*This means: of the thirteen total dimensions, the four we can see come from $\mathcal{M}_4$ and the nine we cannot see come from the compact internal geometry.*

*This result is consumed by Gate 1 (geometry specification) and Gate 6 (threshold unification): $D = 13$ is the binding dimension count that fixes the Planck-normalization exponent and the Kaluza–Klein spectrum.*

**(e) Kaluza–Klein reduction and asymptotic factorisation.**

*The purpose of the next formula is to separate every 13D field into a 4D part and an internal-geometry part.*

$$\Phi(x^\mu, y^a) = \sum_{n} \phi_n(x^\mu)\,\psi_n(y^a),\qquad m_n^2 \sim \frac{1}{R_{\rm int}^2}\,\text{Laplacian eigenvalue}.$$

Zero modes ($n = 0$) descend to surviving 4D effective fields $\phi_0(x^\mu)$ on $\mathcal{M}_4$. Higher modes ($n \geq 1$) have $m_n \sim M_U \sim 10^{16}$ GeV and are integrated out at the threshold gate.

The asymptotic Hilbert space factorises:

$$\boxed{\;\mathcal{H}_{\rm asymp} = \mathcal{H}_{\mathcal{M}_4} \otimes \mathcal{H}_{\rm KK\,zero\,modes},\;}$$

with $\mathcal{H}_{\mathcal{M}_4}$ the standard 4D Fock space (A2.1).

*This means: the 4D physics we measure is the zero-mode sector of the full 13D theory; heavy KK modes are too heavy to see directly and contribute only as threshold corrections.*

*This result is consumed by Gates 2–10, because every gate's output is an asymptotic-state observable that requires $\mathcal{H}_{\rm asymp}$ to factorise cleanly into a 4D piece and an internal piece.*

**(f) Comparison scale on $\mathcal{M}_4$'s energy axis.**

*The purpose of the next formula is to identify $M_Z$ as a 4D rest-energy on $\mathcal{M}_4$.*

$$M_Z^2 = -P^\mu P_\mu\big|_{Z\,\rm rest} = m_{Z\,\rm boson}^2,\qquad M_Z = 91.1876\,\text{GeV}.$$

(R1.7 hash `a6852c7a6b00`.)

*This means: $M_Z$ is the invariant mass of the $Z$ boson, read off in any Lorentz frame on $\mathcal{M}_4$.*

*This result is consumed by Gate 6 (threshold unification) because every gauge coupling is compared at this 4D energy.*

**(g) Planck normalisation linking the 4D and 13D scales.**

*The purpose of the next formula is to show how the observable 4D Planck mass is built from the fundamental 13D scale plus the compact internal volume.*

$$\boxed{\;M_{\rm Pl}^2 = M_*^{D-2}\,\mathrm{Vol}(X_{\rm int}) = M_*^{11}\,\mathrm{Vol}(X_{\rm active}).\;}$$

With $\mathrm{Vol}(X_{\rm active}) = 3.704417261398702 \times 10^{-148}\,{\rm GeV}^{-9}$ (A1.3), this gives $M_* = 7.467050992135091 \times 10^{16}$ GeV (A1.10).

The exponent $D - 2 = 11$ uses $D = 13 = 4 + 9$, where the $+4$ is $\dim\mathcal{M}_4$.

*This means: the 4D Planck mass is enormous because the compact internal volume is tiny — and the dimension contribution of $\mathcal{M}_4$ enters the exponent that controls the ratio.*

*This result is consumed by Gate 6 (threshold unification): $M_{\rm Pl}$ is one of the four declared anchors of R1.8 and is the high-end of the RG transport range.*

**(h) Chirality, signature, anomaly.**

*The purpose of the next formula is to define the 4D chirality operator that distinguishes left-handed and right-handed fermions.*

$$\gamma_5 = i\gamma^0\gamma^1\gamma^2\gamma^3,\qquad \gamma_5^2 = +1,\qquad \{\gamma_5, \gamma^\mu\} = 0.$$

The chirality projector $P_\chi = \tfrac{1}{2}(1 + \gamma_5\Gamma_8)$ of A1.1.3 inherits its Lorentzian meaning from $\mathcal{M}_4$.

*This means: handedness is meaningful only because the time direction has the opposite sign from the spatial directions; in Euclidean signature, $\gamma_5$ has a different algebraic meaning and the unitary chirality story breaks.*

*This result is consumed by Gates 4 (chirality / no mirrors) and 5 (anomaly closure), both of which require Lorentzian-signature unitary representations on $\mathcal{M}_4$.*

### C1.8.5 Output Produced

| Output | Value / form | Why it matters |
|---|---|---|
| 4D base factor $\mathcal{M}_4 = \mathbb{R}^{3,1}$ | first entry of $\mathfrak{B}_{\rm active}^{\,\times\text{-layer}}$ | satisfies Gate 1 (geometry specification) |
| Mostly-plus metric $\eta_{\mu\nu}$ | $\mathrm{diag}(-1,+1,+1,+1)$ | fixes signature for chirality, anomaly, and unitarity |
| Vanishing curvature | $R_{\mu\nu} = 0$ identically | keeps cosmology out of GUT scope (Gate 11 / Section 9) |
| Isometry group | $\mathrm{ISO}(3,1) = \mathbb{R}^{3,1} \rtimes \mathrm{SO}(3,1)$ (10 generators) | global spacetime symmetry of all gate outputs; **not** a Yang–Mills group (C1.12) |
| Dimension contribution | $\dim_{\mathbb{R}}\mathcal{M}_4 = +4$ in $D = 13$ | sets Planck-normalisation exponent $D-2 = 11$ |
| KK factorisation | $\mathcal{H}_{\rm asymp} = \mathcal{H}_{\mathcal{M}_4} \otimes \mathcal{H}_{\rm KK\,zero}$ | gives cluster decomposition and S-matrix interpretation |
| Comparison-scale axis | $M_Z = 91.1876$ GeV on $\mathcal{M}_4$ | comparison energy at which every PDG-anchored quantity is read |
| Planck-mass relation | $M_{\rm Pl}^2 = M_*^{11}\,\mathrm{Vol}(X_{\rm active})$ | links the four declared anchors of R1.8 to the 13D fundamental scale |
| Chirality operator domain | $\gamma_5$ with $\gamma_5^2 = +1$ on $S_{3,1}$ | inherited Lorentzian meaning for $P_\chi$ used by Gates 4 and 5 |

### C1.8.6 Gate Consumption

| Gate | How this output is consumed by the gate | Would the gate fail without it? |
|---|---|:---:|
| Gate 1 — geometry specification | $\mathcal{M}_4$ is the first $\times$-factor of $\mathfrak{B}_{\rm active}$; the active-branch hash `dcc66f1b2685` encodes its presence | **yes** |
| Gate 2 — gauge recovery | KK reduction projects the surviving 4D gauge algebra $\mathfrak{su}(3) \oplus \mathfrak{su}(2) \oplus \mathfrak{u}(1)$ onto $\mathcal{M}_4$ (without this, the gauge fields have no manifold to propagate on) | **yes**, indirect — the gauge fields are sourced by the internal factors but live on $\mathcal{M}_4$ |
| Gate 3 — charge / hypercharge | electric-charge assignments are observables on $\mathcal{M}_4$'s asymptotic Hilbert space | **yes**, indirect |
| Gate 4 — chirality / no mirrors | Lorentzian $\gamma_5$ on $\mathcal{M}_4$ gives the chirality operator a unitary meaning | **yes** |
| Gate 5 — anomaly closure | anomaly polynomial requires Lorentzian signature for the trace identity to imply unitarity | **yes** |
| Gate 6 — threshold unification | $M_Z$ comparison scale and $M_{\rm Pl}$ anchor live on $\mathcal{M}_4$'s energy axis; RG transport is between two 4D scales | **yes** |
| Gate 7 — stabilization | the moduli mechanism of [Appendix F](#appendix-f-stabilization) is defined relative to the 4D EFT on $\mathcal{M}_4$ | **yes** |
| Gate 8 — Higgs protection | 4D Higgs VEV $v_{\rm pred} = 246.02$ GeV and mass $m_h$ are 4D observables on $\mathcal{M}_4$ | **yes** |
| Gate 9 — flavor closure | quark and lepton mass eigenvalues, CKM and PMNS matrices are evaluated at $M_Z$ on $\mathcal{M}_4$ | **yes**, indirect |
| Gate 10 — proton safety | the 4D Wilson-coefficient identity $\Pi_q M \Pi_\ell = 0$ lives in the 4D EFT on $\mathcal{M}_4$ | **yes** |

### C1.8.7 Removal Failure

> If $\mathcal{M}_4$ is removed, the **4D comparison surface** becomes undefined, so **Gate 1 fails first** and Gates 2–10 all lose their comparison statements.

The specific cascade:

1. Without $\mathcal{M}_4$, there is no 4D Lorentzian factor in $\mathfrak{B}_{\rm active}$. Gate 1 (geometry specification) fails immediately because the active-branch displayed expression $\mathfrak{B}_{\rm active} = [\mathcal{M}_4 \times K_6 \times S^2 \times S_Y^{\,1}] \oplus [F^+ \oplus \mathcal{C}_{\rm admiss}] \otimes [\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter} \oplus \cdots]$ is no longer the submitted active branch.
2. Without $\mathcal{M}_4$, the four declared R1.8 anchors ($M_{\rm Pl}$, $\alpha_i^{-1}(M_Z)$, $y_t(M_Z)$, $|V_{us}|$) have no manifold on which they are defined. They are 4D measured numbers; their referent dissolves.
3. Without $\mathcal{M}_4$, the KK reduction $\Phi = \sum_n \phi_n(x^\mu)\psi_n(y^a)$ has no $x^\mu$ to write down. The surviving low-energy effective theory has no target manifold.
4. Without $\mathcal{M}_4$, the asymptotic Hilbert space $\mathcal{H}_{\rm asymp}$ does not factorise into a 4D Fock-space piece, so cluster decomposition fails and the LSZ S-matrix is undefined. Every gate output that uses asymptotic states (Gates 2–10) loses its meaning.
5. Without $\mathcal{M}_4$'s Lorentz signature, the chirality projector $P_\chi$ loses its unitary meaning, and Gate 4 fails.
6. Without $\mathcal{M}_4$'s flatness, $R_{\mu\nu} \neq 0$ would force a curvature-source claim and violate the Gate-11 claim boundary (Section 9).

This is the operational meaning of the binding statement of C1.0: *every required gate's output comparison is performed against measurements made in four-dimensional Lorentz-invariant spacetime*.

## C1.9 Gate Outputs Supported

$\mathcal{M}_4$ does not deliver any frozen geometric quantity directly; its role is to be the *surface* on which every gate output is compared to observation.

| Gate | Output supplied by $\mathcal{M}_4$ | Certificate location |
|---|---|---|
| Gate 1 (geometry specification) | The 4D base factor of $\mathfrak{B}_{\rm active}$; first entry of $[\mathcal{M}_4 \times K_6 \times S^2 \times S_Y^{\,1}]$ in A1.1.1; row 1 of A1.1.2 ($\dim = 4$, $\times$-layer, primitive); active-branch hash `dcc66f1b2685` | A0 + A1.1.1 + A1.1.2 + A1.9 |
| Gate 2 (gauge recovery) — *indirect* | 4D background on which surviving low-energy gauge algebra $\mathfrak{su}(3)_c \oplus \mathfrak{su}(2)_L \oplus \mathfrak{u}(1)_Y$ is *read* after KK reduction; without $\mathcal{M}_4$ the surviving algebra has no manifold to live on | [Appendix D](#appendix-d-standard-model-recovery); A2.4 ($E_{\rm gauge} = T^*(\mathcal{M}_4) \otimes \mathrm{ad}(P)$) |
| Gate 3 (charge) — *indirect* | 4D background on which SM hypercharge / electric-charge assignments are observable in the asymptotic Hilbert space | [Appendix D](#appendix-d-standard-model-recovery) |
| Gate 4 (chirality / no mirrors) | 4D Lorentz-signature manifold on which left vs right chirality is meaningful via $\gamma_5$; $P_\chi$ inherits Lorentzian meaning; surviving spectrum $(n_L, n_R) = (+3, 0)$ on the asymptotic Hilbert space | [Appendix E](#appendix-e-chirality-and-anomaly-closure); A2.2 |
| Gate 5 (stabilization) | 4D background defining the low-energy 4D EFT; stabilization mechanism of [Appendix F](#appendix-f-stabilization) pins internal moduli at chamber-center such that the resulting 4D EFT on $\mathcal{M}_4$ is well-defined; $\mathcal{M}_4$ is the *target manifold* of the EFT after KK reduction | [Appendix F](#appendix-f-stabilization) |
| Gate 6 (threshold unification at 4D scale $M_U$) | $M_Z = 91.1876$ GeV comparison scale on $\mathcal{M}_4$'s energy axis; PDG $\alpha_i^{-1}(M_Z)$ are 4D quantities; threshold vector $(\delta_1, \delta_2, \delta_3) = (+4.8424, -3.1112, -1.7313)$ produced by KK packets on internal factors but *read* at the 4D scale $M_U = 10^{16}$ GeV on $\mathcal{M}_4$ | [Appendix G](#appendix-g-threshold-unification) (G.3.1 + G.3.2); RG transport `f531205a9159`; comparison scale `a6852c7a6b00` |
| Gate 7 (anomaly) — *indirect* | 4D background on which gauge / mixed / gravitational anomaly traces are defined (4D anomaly polynomial $\mathcal{A}^{(4)}[A,R]$ of C1.8.6) | [Appendix E](#appendix-e-chirality-and-anomaly-closure) (E.4 + E.5) |
| Gate 8 (Higgs protection in 4D EFT) | 4D Higgs scalar $H(x)$ on $\mathcal{M}_4$; 4D VEV $v_{\rm pred} = 246.02 \pm 3.5$ GeV; 4D Higgs mass $m_h = 123.82 \pm 1.8$ GeV; both are 4D quantities on $\mathcal{M}_4$'s asymptotic Hilbert space | [Appendix H](#appendix-h-higgs-protection) |
| Gate 9 (flavor closure) — *indirect* | 4D quark / lepton / neutrino mass eigenvalues at $M_Z$ on $\mathcal{M}_4$; 4D CKM and PMNS matrices in the asymptotic Hilbert space; chamber Yukawa eigenvalues *evaluated on* $\mathcal{M}_4$ | Appendices I / J / K |
| Gate 10 (proton safety in 4D EFT) | 4D effective four-fermion operators $C_{QQQL}$, $C_{u^c u^c d^c e^c}$, etc., on $\mathcal{M}_4$ on which the FCNC / mediator no-go theorem of L.3 is stated as a 4D Wilson-coefficient identity | [Appendix L](#appendix-l-proton-safety); FCNC theorem `fff4b433b7b3`, operator-class `551488d06011` |

**Reading.** Every gate that requires a comparison scale (Gates 6–10) depends on $\mathcal{M}_4$ supplying the energy axis at which $M_Z$ and $M_U$ are meaningful. The specific subset where the 4D Lorentzian *structure* (not just the existence of an energy axis) is load-bearing — Gates 4 (chirality), 5 (stabilization defining 4D EFT), 6 (threshold unification at 4D scale $M_U$), 8 (Higgs protection in 4D EFT), 10 (proton safety in 4D EFT) — is the handoff §7.1 list of gates that *cannot be posed* without $\mathcal{M}_4$.

## C1.10 Failure If Removed

| Removed object | Immediate failure | Downstream gate failure |
|---|---|---|
| $\mathcal{M}_4$ entirely | No 4D base factor; every gate output defined only on internal manifold; surviving low-energy EFT has no target manifold, no asymptotic state space, no rest frame for SM fields, no comparison scale | **Gate 1 fails**; **Gates 2–10 lose comparison surface** (no $M_Z$, no PDG comparison, no asymptotic states); **the entire reduction step from 13D to SM EFT loses its target manifold**; four declared anchors of R1.8 have no manifold on which they are defined |
| Lorentz signature (replace $(-,+,+,+)$ with Euclidean $(+,+,+,+)$) | $P_\chi$ loses Lorentzian-signature meaning; left vs right chirality no longer asymmetric in unitary sense | **Gate 4 fails** (no operator-level chirality distinction); **Gate 7 fails** (anomaly polynomial signature-sensitive; physical interpretation requires Lorentzian signature for unitarity) |
| 4D dimension (replace 4 with 3 or 5) | KK decomposition has wrong number of external indices; gauge bosons propagate on wrong number of external dimensions; dimension count $D = 13$ changes; Planck-normalization exponent $D-2$ changes | **Gate 2 fails** (e.g., 3D photon has 1 polarization, not 2); **Gates 3–10 lose PDG comparison** (PDG values are 4D) |
| Flatness (curve into $dS_4$ or $AdS_4$) | Cosmology / gravitation claim outside GUT scope; asymptotic states no longer Poincaré-invariant Fock states; anchors acquire cosmological-time dependence | **Gate 11 (claim boundary) violated**; certificate-complete status conditional on no such extension |
| Orientability (non-orientable 4D manifold) | $\Gamma_5$ globally undefined; $P_\chi$ has no global meaning; spin-$\mathbb{C}$ structure of $K_6$ (`0fd19c9ae0c1`) cannot consistently couple | **Gate 4 fails** (no global chirality); **Gate 7 fails** (anomaly requires orientation) |
| Lorentz invariance (introduce $c \neq 1$ kinetic terms) | Anchors become frame-dependent; PDG comparison loses unique meaning; modern bounds $|c-1| < 10^{-15}$ exclude observable violation at $10^{-3}$ certificate precision | **Gates 1–10 all become frame-dependent**; preferred-frame declaration required (not done) |
| Asymptotic factorisation $\mathfrak{B}_{\rm active} \sim \mathcal{M}_4 \times (\text{internal})$ | $\mathcal{H}_{\rm asymp}$ no longer factorises; cluster decomposition fails; S-matrix undefined | **Gates 2–10 lose S-matrix interpretation**; LSZ reduction has no factor on which to land |

The first row is the **load-bearing failure**: removing $\mathcal{M}_4$ removes every output's empirical referent. Specifically, removing $\mathcal{M}_4$ means: (i) no low-energy interpretation (every SM output is an internal-manifold quantity with no empirical referent); (ii) no comparison surface for measurements (PDG values, anchor values, certificate outputs all require a 4D Lorentzian background); (iii) no rest frame for SM fields (the anchors of R1.8 are defined in 4D rest frames or scattering frames); (iv) the reduction step from 13D to SM EFT loses its target manifold ($x^\mu$ in $\Phi = \sum_n \phi_n(x^\mu)\psi_n(y^a)$ has no manifold to live on).

This is the operational meaning of the binding statement of C1.0: *every required gate's output comparison is performed against measurements made in four-dimensional Lorentz-invariant spacetime*.
