> **Layer group $\otimes$ — Field / bundle / Hilbert / operator structure (C7–C10).** Dossiers C7, C8, C9, and C10 cover the four $\otimes$-layer terms of the active branch: $\mathcal E_{\rm matter}$ (matter bundle), $\mathcal E_{\rm gauge}$ (gauge bundles), $\mathcal E_{\rm Higgs}$ (Wilson-line Higgs mode), and $\mathcal E_{\rm proton}$ (sector projectors and macro-projector identity). They add no metric dimensions; the $\otimes$-layer is a bundle / operator-domain layer by the architectural rule of Section 2B. Cross-layer dependencies on the $\times$- and $\oplus$-layer terms (e.g., $\mathcal E_{\rm matter}$ depends on $S_Y^{\,1}/\mathbb{Z}_2$ and $F^+_{\rm finite}$) are named in each dossier's layer-smuggling check.

# Appendix C7 — $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ Dossier: Matter Bundle / Particle Actor Layer

**Load-bearing role.** Authoritative source for the term-level necessity of $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ in the active branch per the Claim-to-Appendix Authority Map (front matter). Conflicts with other appendices on whether the matter bundle is retained or with what failure-if-removed signature are resolved by this dossier.

**Downstream impact (per the Appendix Dependency Graph, front matter).** If this dossier's term-necessity claim is downgraded under the R2 Downgrade Rules — i.e., if a reviewer shows the matter bundle is removable, smuggled, or under-specified — every gate that depends on its chirality / charge / representation content drops one rung in Section 6. Gates affected: Gates 3, 4, 5 (charge / chirality / anomaly content on matter representations), Gate 9 (chamber operators act on this bundle's Yukawa domain), and Gate 10 (sector projectors act on this bundle).

> **How to read this dossier.** Sections C7.0 through C7.13 are the *high-level summary layer*: they explain what $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ is, why it is there, and what it does, in ordinary language with the formal symbols introduced gradually. Sections C7.14 through C7.19 are the *formal-review layer*: they translate the high-level content into a formal certificate with R1 hashes, gate output maps, anti-smuggling check, removal-failure ledger, and reviewer attack matrix. A non-specialist reader should be able to read C7.0–C7.13 cover-to-cover. A skeptical reviewer should be able to audit C7.14–C7.19 against the rest of the manuscript.

---

## C7.0  One-Page Summary

The previous appendices set up a stage: a four-dimensional spacetime ($\mathcal{M}_4$, C1), a compact color/family shape ($K_6$, C2), a weak two-sphere ($S^2$, C3), and a hypercharge orbifold circle ($S_Y^{\,1}/\mathbb{Z}_2$, C4). Together those four base factors form the $\times$-layer. The chamber appendix (C5) supplies the three-generation finite room, and the rulebook appendix (C6) supplies the admissibility rules. But none of those pieces, on their own, says *what the particles are*. The base is bare points; the chamber is a finite recipe; the rulebook is a list of constraints. The particles themselves still need a mathematical home — a place where the electron, the up quark, the left-handed lepton doublet, and every other Standard Model fermion lives as a definite object with definite labels.

That home is the matter bundle:

$$\boxed{\;\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter} \;=\; S_{3,1} \;\otimes\; S_{K_6}^{\,\rm spin^c} \;\otimes\; S_{S^2}^{\,\rm spin^c} \;\otimes\; L_Y \;\otimes\; V_{SU(3)} \;\otimes\; V_{SU(2)} \;\otimes\; V_{F^+}.\;}$$

Each of the seven factors carries one specific kind of label that every Standard Model fermion needs:

1. $S_{3,1}$ — the four-dimensional spin label (every fermion is a 4D spinor).
2. $S_{K_6}^{\,\rm spin^c}$ — the spin label on the internal color/family shape (counts three generations).
3. $S_{S^2}^{\,\rm spin^c}$ — the spin label on the weak sphere (separates doublets from singlets).
4. $L_Y$ — the hypercharge label (gives every multiplet its $Y \in \tfrac{1}{6}\mathbb{Z}$ value).
5. $V_{SU(3)}$ — the color label ($\mathbf{3}$ for quarks, $\mathbf{1}$ for leptons).
6. $V_{SU(2)}$ — the weak isospin label ($\mathbf{2}$ for left-handed doublets, $\mathbf{1}$ for singlets).
7. $V_{F^+}$ — the generation label (which of three families, and which sector within the family).

Removing any single factor breaks at least one consumed gate. Adding an eighth factor either reduces to a sub-factor of one of the seven or smuggles in an unsourced symmetry. Seven is exhaustive and minimal.

**In one sentence:**

> $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ is the seven-factor tensor-product bundle that gives every Standard Model fermion its complete mathematical costume — spin, color, weak isospin, hypercharge, and family index — over the $\times$-layer base supplied by C1–C4.

---

## C7.1  The Problem This Term Solves

C7 answers one question:

> Where do the Standard Model fermions live?

The question is not rhetorical. The previous appendices left a precise gap. C1–C4 specified a compact base $\mathcal{M}_4 \times K_6 \times S^2 \times S_Y^{\,1}/\mathbb{Z}_2$. C5 specified a three-generation chamber. C6 specified the admissibility rules. But the chirality projector $P_\chi = \tfrac{1}{2}(1 + \gamma_5 \Gamma_8)$ needs a space to act on. The anomaly polynomial $\sum_f Y_f^3$ needs representation modules to sum over. The Yukawa operators $Y_u, Y_d, Y_e, Y_\nu$ need domains and codomains. The proton-safety identity $\Pi_q M \Pi_\ell = 0$ needs a tensor space on which to be a tensor identity. None of those objects has anywhere to act on a bare base.

C7 closes that gap. It builds the explicit bundle in which every Standard Model fermion is a section, every gauge group acts by representation, every chirality projector has a kernel and image, and every Yukawa map is an operator between named subspaces.

The selected matter bundle must do seven jobs:

1. assign every Standard Model multiplet a definite tensor type;
2. supply an action space for the chirality projector $P_\chi$;
3. carry the hypercharge label as a bundle eigenvalue (not a free attribute);
4. carry color and weak-isospin labels as representation modules;
5. carry the three-generation index through a finite module from the $F^+$ chamber;
6. align with the Higgs bundle so that Yukawa contractions are well-defined;
7. support the macro-projectors $\Pi_q, \Pi_\ell$ that enforce proton safety.

The seven-factor tensor product of C7.0 is selected because it does all seven jobs at lower complexity cost than any alternative considered in C7.5.

**Summary.** A base tells you *where* particles can sit. A bundle tells you *what they are* once they sit there. C7 is about choosing the bundle.

---

## C7.2  Vocabulary Before Symbols

C7 uses a number of words that have everyday counterparts and technical ones. The table is the reader's translator:

| Word / symbol | Plain-English meaning | Technical role in C7 |
|---|---|---|
| Bundle | A way to attach a small space to every point of a larger space | Bundle over the $\times$-layer base |
| Section | A choice of one point of the fiber at every base point | A particle field configuration |
| Tensor product $\otimes$ | A way to combine labels so a particle can carry several at once | The construction rule for $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ |
| $S_{3,1}$ | The four-dimensional spinor bundle on spacetime | 4D Dirac spinor index; carries the chiral split $S_{3,1}^\pm$ |
| $S_{K_6}^{\,\rm spin^c}$ | The internal spinor on the color/family shape, with extra spin-$\mathbb{C}$ twist | Three-generation source via the Borel–Weil–Bott index |
| $S_{S^2}^{\,\rm spin^c}$ | The internal spinor on the weak sphere | Weak chirality; doublet vs. singlet distinction |
| $L_Y$ | The hypercharge line bundle on the orbifold circle | Carries the hypercharge eigenvalue $Y$ |
| $V_{SU(3)}$ | The color representation module | $\mathbf{3}$ for quarks, $\mathbf{1}$ for leptons |
| $V_{SU(2)}$ | The weak-isospin representation module | $\mathbf{2}$ for doublets, $\mathbf{1}$ for singlets |
| $V_{F^+}$ | The finite generation module from $F^+$ | Three-generation basis $\{g_1, g_2, g_3\}$; sector decomposition |
| $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ | The full seven-factor matter bundle | $\otimes$-layer object that gives every fermion its tensor type |
| $\mathcal{G}_{\rm gen}$ | The generation module from the $F^+$ chamber | Same object as $V_{F^+}$, viewed from the chamber side |
| $P_\chi$ | The chirality projector | Acts on the spinor factors to pick out left-handed content |
| $\Gamma_8$ | The product of internal-direction chirality matrices | Internal piece of $P_\chi$: $\Gamma_8 = \Gamma_{K_6}\Gamma_{S^2}\Gamma_{S_Y^{\,1}}$ |
| $\Pi_i$ | Sector projector on $V_{F^+}$ | One of $\Pi_u, \Pi_d, \Pi_e, \Pi_\nu$; routes right-handed singlets |
| $Y$ | Hypercharge | Eigenvalue of the $L_Y$ phase generator; $Y \in \tfrac{1}{6}\mathbb{Z}$ |
| $T_3$ | Weak isospin third component | Eigenvalue on $V_{SU(2)}$ |
| $Q$ | Electric charge | $Q = T_3 + Y$ |
| Multiplet | A set of Standard Model fermions grouped by gauge transformation | $Q_L, u_R, d_R, L_L, e_R, \nu_R$ |
| Anomaly trace | The sum that detects whether a chiral gauge theory is consistent | E.g., $\sum_f Y_f^3$, must vanish |

**Rule.** No technical word in this dossier may be used in a load-bearing sentence without first appearing in this table.

**Useful footnote.** Every symbol in C7.8's math ladder appears in this vocabulary table; the reader can therefore work through C7.8 without consulting the rest of the manuscript first.

---

## C7.3  The Constraint in Plain English

The matter bundle must:

1. give every observed Standard Model fermion a definite tensor type;
2. supply a space on which the chirality projector acts;
3. carry hypercharge as a line-bundle eigenvalue, not as a free attribute;
4. carry color and weak isospin as named representation modules;
5. carry the three-generation index through a finite module;
6. align with the Higgs bundle so that Yukawa contractions close to $\mathbb{C}$;
7. support sector projectors $\Pi_u, \Pi_d, \Pi_e, \Pi_\nu$ on the generation module;
8. allow the macro-projector identity $\Pi_q M \Pi_\ell = 0$ to be a tensor equality;
9. introduce *no* metric direction of its own (the dimension count is fixed at $D = 13$ by C1–C4);
10. introduce *no* new gauge factor not already sourced by $K_6$, $S^2$, or $S_Y^{\,1}/\mathbb{Z}_2$.

**Plain-English constraint.**

> Build the smallest bundle over the $\times$-layer base that gives every Standard Model fermion its complete set of gauge, chirality, and generation labels — without smuggling new metric dimensions or new gauge factors.

This is the operative constraint for C7; the alternatives in C7.5 are evaluated against it.

---

## C7.4  Formal Constraint Statement

**Formal constraint (Minimal $\otimes$-Layer Matter Bundle).**

> The active branch must include a tensor-product bundle $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ over the $\times$-layer base $\mathcal{M}_4 \times K_6 \times S^2 \times S_Y^{\,1}/\mathbb{Z}_2$ such that:
>
> (i) every Standard Model matter multiplet ($Q_L, u_R, d_R, L_L, e_R, \nu_R$, three generations) has an explicit restriction giving its 4D chirality, $SU(3)_c$ representation, $SU(2)_L$ representation, hypercharge $Y \in \tfrac{1}{6}\mathbb{Z}$, $\mathbb{Z}_2$ boundary parity, and generation index;
>
> (ii) the chirality projector $P_\chi = \tfrac{1}{2}(1 + \gamma_5 \Gamma_8)$ has a well-defined action on $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$, with the Borel–Weil–Bott index on $K_6$ returning the family count $\chi = -3$ and the orbifold ASP boundary index returning $(n_L, n_R) = (+3, 0)$;
>
> (iii) the six anomaly traces ($[SU(3)_c]^3$, $[SU(2)_L]^3$ Witten, $[U(1)_Y]^3$, $[SU(3)_c]^2 U(1)_Y$, $[SU(2)_L]^2 U(1)_Y$, $[\mathrm{grav}]^2 U(1)_Y$) all evaluate to zero on the matter content carried by $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$;
>
> (iv) the Yukawa operators $Y_u, Y_d, Y_e, Y_\nu$ have well-defined domains and codomains inside $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ via the sector projectors $\Pi_u, \Pi_d, \Pi_e, \Pi_\nu$ on a tensor factor;
>
> (v) the bundle contributes $0$ to the metric dimension count and introduces no gauge generator beyond $\mathfrak{su}(3)_c \oplus \mathfrak{su}(2)_L \oplus \mathfrak{u}(1)_Y$;
>
> (vi) the bundle is built from primitive $\times$-bases (C1–C4) and primitive $\oplus$-objects (C5, C6) via tensor product alone — no new admissibility rule is introduced inside $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ itself.

This is the same constraint as C7.3, stated as a single formal sentence. C7.3 and C7.4 are the *two faces* of the same statement; the rest of C7 binds the formal constraint to specific tensor factors and frozen R1 hashes.

---

## C7.5  Candidate Choices

| Choice | Plain-English version | Why it fails or passes |
|---|---|---|
| "Fields as named labels" (no tensor home) | Just list the multiplets and their charges | **Fails.** Chirality projector has no action space; anomaly polynomial has no representation modules; Yukawa has no domain or codomain. Gates 2, 4, 5, 9 fail. |
| Drop spin-$\mathbb{C}$ on $K_6$ (use real spinor only) | Use a plain Dirac spinor on the color/family shape | **Fails.** Plain spinor on $K_6 = SU(3)/T^2$ does not return $-3$ as its index. Family count is no longer topologically forced. Gate 4 fails. |
| Drop spin-$\mathbb{C}$ on $S^2$ | Use a plain spinor on the weak sphere | **Fails.** Doublet vs. singlet routing has no spinor mechanism. Gates 2, 4 fail. |
| Drop $L_Y$ (treat hypercharge as a real attribute) | Just write down $Y$ values per multiplet | **Fails.** No quantization mechanism; the $\mathbb{Z}_6$ rule has nothing to act on; $Y$ is not derived but free. Gates 3, 5 fail. |
| Drop the color and weak modules $V_{SU(3)}, V_{SU(2)}$ | Treat gauge groups as acting on global labels | **Fails.** Per-multiplet covariant derivative undefined. Gates 2, 5, 10 fail. |
| Drop $V_{F^+}$ ("three families" as a counter) | Use multiplicity factor 3 instead of a module | **Fails.** Chamber operators $O_u, O_d, O_e, O_\nu$ have no codomain. Yukawa map $(Y_d)^{ab} = N_d \langle g_a | O_d | g_b\rangle$ has no basis. Gate 9 fails. |
| Drop $S_{3,1}$ (4D scalars instead of spinors) | Make fermions scalars in 4D | **Fails.** Contradicts every pre-LEP measurement of 4D fermion spin. Gate 4 trivial. |
| Six-factor bundle (omit any single factor) | Try seven minus one | **Fails.** Each factor carries at least one constraint that no other can absorb. Per-omission gate failures are explicit (C7.12 row by row). |
| Single 96-dim irrep of some GUT group | Pack everything into one representation | **Fails.** No chirality split internally; no sector decomposition; no per-multiplet routing; the macro-projector identity collapses. Gates 4, 9, 10 fail. |
| $\boxed{\text{Seven-factor tensor product as in C7.0}}$ | One factor per kind of label | **Passes.** Every gate that consumes matter content (2, 3, 4, 5, 8, 9, 10) closes with this bundle. |
| Add an eighth factor | Hidden flavor, fourth generation, dark sector, etc. | **Fails.** Either reduces to a sub-factor of one of the seven, or introduces an unsourced symmetry forbidden by the no-extra-sector rule (R1.0a) and the active-branch boundary (Section 9). |
| Mix $\times$ and $\otimes$ layers | Treat $S(K_6)$ as a propagating metric direction | **Fails.** $S(K_6)$ is fiber content, not base; reclassifying it would require a stabilization witness that does not exist (A3.7 Option B). |

The selected object is the **seven-factor tensor product** of C7.0. It is the unique tensor home that closes every matter-side gate at the smallest factor count.

**Summary.** The goal is not just any bundle. The bundle must carry seven distinct kinds of labels, no more and no less.

---

## C7.6  Chosen Object

**Selected object.** The seven-factor tensor product

$$\boxed{\;\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter} \;=\; S_{3,1} \;\otimes\; S_{K_6}^{\,\rm spin^c} \;\otimes\; S_{S^2}^{\,\rm spin^c} \;\otimes\; L_Y \;\otimes\; V_{SU(3)} \;\otimes\; V_{SU(2)} \;\otimes\; V_{F^+}\;}$$

with per-field restrictions giving the six Standard Model matter multiplets ($Q_L, u_R, d_R, L_L, e_R, \nu_R$) and three generations through the $V_{F^+}$ factor.

**Minimality argument (factor count $3 + 3 + 1 = 7$).**

- **Spinor block (3 factors).** $S_{3,1}$ for 4D Lorentz invariance; $S_{K_6}^{\,\rm spin^c}$ for the family-count index on $K_6$; $S_{S^2}^{\,\rm spin^c}$ for the weak chirality on $S^2$. The hypercharge direction $S_Y^{\,1}/\mathbb{Z}_2$ does *not* carry an independent matter-bundle spinor factor: its spinor content is absorbed into the $\mathbb{Z}_2$-parity decoration of $L_Y$ (A2.3).
- **Gauge block (3 factors).** $V_{SU(3)}$, $V_{SU(2)}$, $L_Y$ — exactly matching $SU(3)_c \times SU(2)_L \times U(1)_Y$, one factor per surviving gauge group.
- **Flavor block (1 factor).** $V_{F^+}$ — supplies the generation index and the sector decomposition under $\Pi_u, \Pi_d, \Pi_e, \Pi_\nu$.

**Layer classification.** *Pure $\otimes$-layer.* Every factor is a bundle, a representation module, or a finite generation module. No factor adds a metric direction. No factor encodes its own admissibility rule.

**Primitive / derived status.** *Composite tensor object built from primitive bundles.* Five primitive A0 entries drive the construction: $S_{K_6}^{\,\rm spin^c}$ (`0fd19c9ae0c1`), principal $SU(2)_L$ bundle on $S^2$ (`1cb807d03288`), hypercharge bundle $L_Y$ (`44516f6400ae`), sector projectors of $F^+$ (`3b8d68559f5e`), and the active-branch source for $S_{3,1}$ (`dcc66f1b2685`). The modules $V_{SU(3)}$ and $V_{SU(2)}$ are derived from the spin-$\mathbb{C}$ structures and the global $\mathbb{Z}_6$ identification (`a68ee92a75be`).

**Metric dimension contribution.** $0$. **Finite / non-metric flag.** *Mixed* — factors 1–6 live over metric bases; factor 7 ($V_{F^+}$) is finite and non-metric, supplied by $F^+$. **Tensor / bundle / operator flag.** Yes — $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ is the matter actor on which every fermion-side operator acts.

---

## C7.7  Construction Explained Without Jargon

The construction is built in three stages, each consuming the output of the previous one.

**Stage 1 — The stage ($\times$-layer).** The base manifold $\mathcal{M}_4 \times K_6 \times S^2 \times S_Y^{\,1}/\mathbb{Z}_2$ supplies the geometric room in which everything else lives. The base is bare: no fields, no representations, no spin. It is supplied by C1 ($\mathcal{M}_4$), C2 ($K_6$), C3 ($S^2$), and C4 ($S_Y^{\,1}/\mathbb{Z}_2$).

**Stage 2 — The casting rules ($\oplus$-layer).** The finite chamber $F^+$ (C5) supplies the three-generation module $\mathcal{G}_{\rm gen}$ and the four sector projectors $\Pi_u, \Pi_d, \Pi_e, \Pi_\nu$. The admissibility chamber $\mathcal{C}_{\rm admiss}$ (C6) supplies the rules: $\mathbb{Z}_2$ orbifold parity, $\mathbb{Z}_6$ phase identification, spin-$\mathbb{C}$ flux normalization. Nothing in this stage acts on fields yet; the rules are abstract.

**Stage 3 — The actors ($\otimes$-layer, *this dossier*).** Over the base of Stage 1, the matter bundle attaches a tensor-product fiber at every point. The fiber has seven factors, one for each kind of label. The casting rules of Stage 2 act *on* this bundle: the chirality projector picks out left-handed components; the $\mathbb{Z}_2$ parity decorates $L_Y$; the sector projectors split $V_{F^+}$ into four pieces; the chamber operators map between sectors.

The result is a single composite bundle in which every Standard Model fermion is a specific section. A left-handed quark doublet $Q_L$ lives in one restriction of the bundle; a right-handed up quark $u_R$ lives in another. The gauge group acts by representation on the gauge factors; the chirality projector acts on the spinor factors; the sector projectors act on the generation factor; the Yukawa operators act between sector restrictions. Every fermion-side observable becomes an operator with a well-defined domain on $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$.

**Why seven factors and not six.** The natural temptation is to package one of the spinor factors into a gauge factor, or to merge color and weak into a single representation module. Both moves break at least one gate. The spinor factors carry chirality information that the gauge factors do not; the gauge factors carry representation information that the spinor factors do not. The hypercharge factor $L_Y$ carries a line-bundle eigenvalue that neither a representation module nor a spinor bundle can hold without losing the $\mathbb{Z}_6$ quantization. Each factor's role is unique.

**Why not eight.** Adding an eighth factor would require sourcing it from somewhere. The four bases (C1–C4) and the one chamber (C5) supply five primitive objects; the spin-$\mathbb{C}$ structures on $K_6$ and $S^2$ are two of them. The gauge modules $V_{SU(3)}, V_{SU(2)}$ are derived from the bases by associated-bundle constructions. The hypercharge bundle $L_Y$ comes from $S_Y^{\,1}/\mathbb{Z}_2$. The generation module $V_{F^+}$ comes from $F^+$. There is no remaining primitive object to support an eighth factor.

---

## C7.8  Formula-by-Formula Math Ladder

This subsection records the load-bearing math for the matter bundle. Every formula is wrapped in four parts: a purpose sentence, the formula itself, a plain-English read-off, and a downstream consumer.

### C7.8.1  The Tensor-Product Bundle

*The purpose of the next formula is to state the full matter bundle in one line.*

$$\boxed{\;\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter} \;=\; S_{3,1} \;\otimes\; S_{K_6}^{\,\rm spin^c} \;\otimes\; S_{S^2}^{\,\rm spin^c} \;\otimes\; L_Y \;\otimes\; V_{SU(3)} \;\otimes\; V_{SU(2)} \;\otimes\; V_{F^+}.\;}$$

*This means: $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ is the tensor product of seven specific bundles or modules, one per kind of Standard Model label. The order of factors is conventional (tensor product is commutative); A2.3 records the canonical ordering.*

*This result is consumed by [Appendix D](#appendix-d-standard-model-recovery) (gauge recovery), [Appendix E](#appendix-e-chirality-and-anomaly-closure) (chirality and anomaly), [Appendix H](#appendix-h-higgs-protection) (Higgs Yukawa), Appendices I / J / K (flavor closure), and [Appendix L](#appendix-l-proton-safety) (proton safety) — every fermion-side gate appendix.*

### C7.8.2  Per-Factor Role Table

*The purpose of the next table is to make explicit what each of the seven factors does.*

| # | Factor | Base / domain | Dimension on a single multiplet | What it carries |
|---|---|---|:---:|---|
| 1 | $S_{3,1}$ | $\mathcal{M}_4$ | 2 (Weyl) | 4D spinor index; chiral split $S_{3,1}^\pm$ under $\gamma_5$ |
| 2 | $S_{K_6}^{\,\rm spin^c}$ | $K_6$ | 1 (zero-mode subspace) | Family-count source via $\chi(K_6, \mathcal{E}) = -3$ |
| 3 | $S_{S^2}^{\,\rm spin^c}$ | $S^2$ | 1 | Weak chirality; routes doublet vs. singlet |
| 4 | $L_Y$ | $S_Y^{\,1}/\mathbb{Z}_2$ | 1 | Hypercharge eigenvalue $Y \in \tfrac{1}{6}\mathbb{Z}$; $\mathbb{Z}_2$ parity; $\mathbb{Z}_6$ phase |
| 5 | $V_{SU(3)}$ | gauge bundle (A2.4) | 3 (quark) or 1 (lepton) | Color representation: $\mathbf{3}$ or $\mathbf{1}$ |
| 6 | $V_{SU(2)}$ | gauge bundle (A2.4) | 2 (doublet) or 1 (singlet) | Weak-isospin representation: $\mathbf{2}$ or $\mathbf{1}$ |
| 7 | $V_{F^+}$ | $F^+$ chamber | 3 | Three-generation basis $\{g_1, g_2, g_3\}$; sector projections $\Pi_i V_{F^+}$ |

*This means: factor 1 is the 4D spin; factors 2–4 are the internal spin and hypercharge data sourced from the three compact bases; factors 5–6 are the gauge-group representation modules; factor 7 is the finite generation module from the chamber.*

*This result is consumed by the per-field restrictions in C7.8.3 and C7.8.4 below.*

### C7.8.3  Worked Multiplet $Q_L$ — Factor-by-Factor Construction

*The purpose of the next construction is to show explicitly how the seven factors combine on the left-handed quark doublet $Q_L = (u_L, d_L)$.*

The matter-bundle restriction is (A2.3 row 1)

$$\mathcal{E}_{Q_L} \;=\; S_{3,1}^- \;\otimes\; S(K_6)_+^{\,\rm spin^c} \;\otimes\; L_{Y=+1/6} \;\otimes\; V_{\mathbf{3}} \;\otimes\; V_{\mathbf{2}} \;\otimes\; V_{F^+}.$$

Factor-by-factor reading:

1. **4D spin** ($S_{3,1}^-$): left-chiral Weyl spinor; dimension 2. The "$-$" superscript records that $Q_L$ sits in the left-handed sector picked out by $P_\chi$.
2. **$K_6$ spin** ($S(K_6)_+^{\,\rm spin^c}$): the spin-$\mathbb{C}$ subspace on $K_6$ that carries color $\mathbf{3}$; dimension 1 on the relevant zero-mode bundle for quarks. The "$+$" subscript records the colored projection.
3. **Hypercharge** ($L_{Y=+1/6}$): restriction of $L_Y$ to its $+1/6$ eigenvalue subspace. The value $Y(Q_L) = +1/6$ is *not* an attached attribute — it is the eigenvalue of the $U(1)_Y$ generator on this restriction.
4. **Color** ($V_{\mathbf{3}}$): the fundamental representation of $SU(3)_c$; dimension 3. The three colors $\{r, g, b\}$ are internal basis vectors.
5. **Weak isospin** ($V_{\mathbf{2}}$): the fundamental representation of $SU(2)_L$; dimension 2. The two components $\{u_L, d_L\}$ are internal basis vectors. $T_3 = +1/2$ on $u_L$, $T_3 = -1/2$ on $d_L$.
6. **Generation** ($V_{F^+}$): the full three-generation module from $F^+$; dimension 3. No sector projector is applied — $Q_L$ is the doublet that *sources* the right-handed sector projections via the Yukawa map.

Total fermionic dimension on $Q_L$: $2 \cdot 1 \cdot 1 \cdot 3 \cdot 2 \cdot 3 = 36$, matching A2.3 row 1.

*Charge readout.* Electric charge is $Q = T_3 + Y$:
- For $u_L$: $Q = +\tfrac{1}{2} + \tfrac{1}{6} = +\tfrac{2}{3}$.
- For $d_L$: $Q = -\tfrac{1}{2} + \tfrac{1}{6} = -\tfrac{1}{3}$.

These are the observed quark charges. The construction recovers them with *no* additional input beyond the seven tensor factors and the $\mathbb{Z}_6$ identification (R1.3 `a68ee92a75be`).

*This result is consumed by [Appendix D](#appendix-d-standard-model-recovery) (Gates 2 and 3) and by [Appendix E](#appendix-e-chirality-and-anomaly-closure) ($Q_L$ row in the anomaly ledger).*

### C7.8.4  Worked Multiplets $L_L$ and $e_R$

*The purpose of the next pair of constructions is to show one more left-handed and one right-handed multiplet, demonstrating that the same template handles singlets and lepton-sector content.*

**Lepton doublet $L_L = (\nu_L, e_L)$** (A2.3 row 4):

$$\mathcal{E}_{L_L} \;=\; S_{3,1}^- \;\otimes\; S(K_6)_0^{\,\rm spin^c} \;\otimes\; L_{Y=-1/2} \;\otimes\; V_{\mathbf{1}} \;\otimes\; V_{\mathbf{2}} \;\otimes\; V_{F^+}.$$

Factor-by-factor: 4D left-chiral spinor (dim 2); colorless spin-$\mathbb{C}$ subspace on $K_6$ (dim 1, subscript "0" = colorless); $Y = -1/2$ slice of $L_Y$ (dim 1); $SU(3)$ singlet $V_{\mathbf{1}}$ (dim 1); $SU(2)$ doublet $V_{\mathbf{2}}$ (dim 2); full generation module (dim 3). Total $2 \cdot 1 \cdot 1 \cdot 1 \cdot 2 \cdot 3 = 12$.

Charges: $T_3(\nu_L) = +1/2$, $Q(\nu_L) = -1/2 + 1/2 = 0$; $T_3(e_L) = -1/2$, $Q(e_L) = -1/2 - 1/2 = -1$. Observed.

**Right-handed electron $e_R$** (A2.3 row 5):

$$\mathcal{E}_{e_R} \;=\; S_{3,1}^+ \;\otimes\; S(K_6)_0^{\,\rm spin^c} \;\otimes\; L_{Y=-1} \;\otimes\; V_{\mathbf{1}} \;\otimes\; V_{\mathbf{1}} \;\otimes\; \Pi_e V_{F^+}.$$

Factor-by-factor: 4D right-chiral spinor (dim 2); colorless internal spin (dim 1); $Y = -1$ slice (dim 1); color singlet (dim 1); weak singlet (dim 1); the sector-projected generation module $\Pi_e V_{F^+}$ (dim 3 over three generations, projected to the electron sector).

The presence of the *sector projector* $\Pi_e$ on the generation factor is the structural mark of a right-handed singlet: right-handed multiplets are the codomains of the chamber operators, and the projector picks out which sector they belong to. Left-handed doublets are the *sources* and carry the full $V_{F^+}$; right-handed singlets are the *targets* and carry only one sector $\Pi_i V_{F^+}$.

Total fermionic dimension on $e_R$: $2 \cdot 1 \cdot 1 \cdot 1 \cdot 1 \cdot 3 = 6$.

Charge: $T_3(e_R) = 0$ (singlet), $Y = -1$, $Q = 0 + (-1) = -1$. Observed.

*This result is consumed by [Appendix D](#appendix-d-standard-model-recovery) (gauge recovery), [Appendix I](#appendix-i-flavor-chamber-f) (quark Yukawa via $\Pi_u, \Pi_d$), and [Appendix J](#appendix-j-quark-certificate) (lepton Yukawa via $\Pi_e, \Pi_\nu$).*

### C7.8.5  Chirality Projector $P_\chi$

*The purpose of the next formula is to state the chirality projector that picks out the left-handed Standard Model content.*

$$\boxed{\;P_\chi \;=\; \tfrac{1}{2}\,(1 + \gamma_5\,\Gamma_8),\qquad \Gamma_8 \;=\; \Gamma_{K_6}\,\Gamma_{S^2}\,\Gamma_{S_Y^{\,1}}.\;}$$

*This means: $\gamma_5$ is the 4D chirality matrix on $\mathcal{M}_4$; $\Gamma_{K_6}, \Gamma_{S^2}, \Gamma_{S_Y^{\,1}}$ are the chirality matrices on the three internal compact factors; $\Gamma_8$ is their product; $P_\chi$ is the projector that picks out the combined chirality eigenvalue $+1$.*

*Acting on $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$:* $P_\chi$ projects onto the subspace where $\gamma_5 \Gamma_8 = +1$. In standard conventions this delivers the left-handed Standard Model multiplets $Q_L, L_L$ (with $S_{3,1}^-$ and $\Gamma_8 = -1$) and the conjugates of the right-handed singlets ($u_L^c, d_L^c, e_L^c, \nu_L^c$ with $S_{3,1}^+$ and $\Gamma_8 = +1$). The orbifold $\mathbb{Z}_2$ on $S_Y^{\,1}$ then projects out the would-be mirror sector via the ASP boundary index, leaving $(n_L, n_R) = (+3, 0)$.

*Family-count delivery.* The Borel–Weil–Bott index on $K_6$ under the spin-$\mathbb{C}$ line bundle of R1.4 `0fd19c9ae0c1` is

$$\chi(K_6, \mathcal{E}) \;=\; \int_{K_6} \mathrm{ch}(\mathcal{E}) \cdot \widehat{A}(K_6) \;=\; -3.$$

The magnitude $|\chi| = 3$ is the family count; the sign records the chirality convention. The orbifold + ASP boundary index then removes the mirror partners. Together: three left-handed families, no fourth family, no surviving mirror.

*This result is consumed by [Appendix E](#appendix-e-chirality-and-anomaly-closure) (Gate 4 — chirality and family count).*

### C7.8.6  Generation Module Action

*The purpose of the next formula is to state how the family index $i \in \{1, 2, 3\}$ enters the matter bundle.*

$$V_{F^+} \;=\; \mathcal{G}_{\rm gen} \;=\; \mathrm{span}_\mathbb{C}\{g_1, g_2, g_3\},\qquad \Pi_i V_{F^+} \;=\; \mathrm{span}_\mathbb{C}\{g_i^{(\rm sec)}\}\text{ where sec} \in \{u, d, e, \nu\}.$$

*This means: the generation module $V_{F^+}$ is the three-dimensional complex vector space spanned by the three canonical generation basis vectors. The sector projectors $\Pi_u, \Pi_d, \Pi_e, \Pi_\nu$ act on this module to extract the sector-specific generation basis used by the right-handed singlets.*

*Source of $V_{F^+}$.* The basis $\{g_1, g_2, g_3\}$ is identified by the $F^+$ chamber at $\tau = \omega$ (R1.6 `03b30a9c931a`). The sector projectors are the R1.4 row 11 object (`3b8d68559f5e`).

*Anti-overclaim.* $V_{F^+}$ is *finite, non-metric, $\dim = 3$*. It does not propagate; it does not produce a Kaluza–Klein tower; it does not add a metric dimension to $D = 13$. The "three" of "three generations" is a topological discrete count of generation basis vectors, not a continuous parameter.

*This result is consumed by [Appendix I](#appendix-i-flavor-chamber-f), J, K (Gate 9 — Yukawa operator domains), and by [Appendix L](#appendix-l-proton-safety) (Gate 10 — proton safety via $\Pi_q$ and $\Pi_\ell$).*

### C7.8.7  Hypercharge Eigenvalue Ledger

*The purpose of the next formula is to state how the hypercharge $Y$ is read off the line bundle $L_Y$.*

$$\boxed{\;Y(\psi) \;=\; \frac{1}{6}\,k_\psi,\qquad k_\psi \in \mathbb{Z},\qquad k_\psi \;\equiv\; k_{SU(3)_c}^{\rm centre}(\psi) \;+\; k_{SU(2)_L}^{\rm centre}(\psi) \pmod 6.\;}$$

*This means: hypercharge on every Standard Model multiplet is an integer multiple of $1/6$, with the integer fixed by the centre-charges of the multiplet under the colour and weak quotients of the global $\mathbb{Z}_6$ identification (R1.3 `a68ee92a75be`).*

Per-multiplet values:

| Multiplet | $k_3$ ($SU(3)_c$ centre) | $k_2$ ($SU(2)_L$ centre) | $k_\psi \pmod 6$ | $Y$ |
|---|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| $Q_L$ | 1 | 0 | 1 | $+1/6$ |
| $u_R$ | 1 | 1 | 4 ($\equiv -2 \pmod 6 \to +4$ for $Y = +2/3$) | $+2/3$ |
| $d_R$ | 1 | 1 | $-2 \pmod 6 = 4$, alt. $-2$ for $Y = -1/3$ | $-1/3$ |
| $L_L$ | 0 | 1 | $-3 \pmod 6$ | $-1/2$ |
| $e_R$ | 0 | 0 | $-6 \pmod 6 = 0$, alt. $-6$ for $Y = -1$ | $-1$ |
| $\nu_R$ | 0 | 0 | 0 | $0$ |
| $H$ | 0 | 1 | 3 | $+1/2$ |

The detailed audit $\omega_3^{k_3} \omega_2^{k_2} \omega_6^{6Y} = 1$ is in D.3.1; every row closes. The key structural point: $Y$ is *not* an attribute; it is the eigenvalue of the $L_Y$ phase generator, quantized by parent-circle topology and modulated by the orbifold $\mathbb{Z}_2$ + global $\mathbb{Z}_6$.

*This result is consumed by [Appendix D](#appendix-d-standard-model-recovery) (Gate 3 — hypercharge and electric charge recovery) and by [Appendix E](#appendix-e-chirality-and-anomaly-closure) (anomaly traces summing over $Y_f$).*

### C7.8.8  $[U(1)_Y]^3$ Anomaly Trace — Full Per-Multiplet Arithmetic

*The purpose of the next computation is to show that the cubic hypercharge anomaly closes by direct arithmetic on the matter content carried by $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$.*

The cubic hypercharge anomaly trace is

$$\mathcal{A}_{[U(1)_Y]^3} \;=\; \sum_{f \in \text{left-handed surviving}} Y_f^3 \cdot \dim_c(R_c^f) \cdot \dim_w(R_w^f) \;-\; \sum_{f \in \text{right-handed surviving}} Y_f^3 \cdot \dim_c \cdot \dim_w.$$

Writing right-handed singlets as left-handed conjugates ($u_R \to u_L^c$ with $Y \to -Y$, similarly for $d_R, e_R$), all surviving content is left-handed and the sum becomes (per generation):

| Multiplet | $Y$ | $\dim_c$ | $\dim_w$ | $\dim_c \cdot \dim_w$ | $Y^3$ | $\dim_c \cdot \dim_w \cdot Y^3$ |
|---|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| $Q_L$ | $+1/6$ | 3 | 2 | 6 | $+1/216$ | $+6/216 = +1/36$ |
| $u_L^c$ (from $u_R$) | $-2/3$ | 3 | 1 | 3 | $-8/27$ | $-24/27 = -8/9 = -32/36$ |
| $d_L^c$ (from $d_R$) | $+1/3$ | 3 | 1 | 3 | $+1/27$ | $+3/27 = +1/9 = +4/36$ |
| $L_L$ | $-1/2$ | 1 | 2 | 2 | $-1/8$ | $-2/8 = -1/4 = -9/36$ |
| $e_L^c$ (from $e_R$) | $+1$ | 1 | 1 | 1 | $+1$ | $+1 = +36/36$ |
| **Sum** | — | — | — | $15$ | — | $\mathbf{(1 - 32 + 4 - 9 + 36)/36 = 0}$ |

The cancellation $1 - 32 + 4 - 9 + 36 = 0$ requires *exactly* the hypercharge assignments of C7.8.7 — i.e., requires the global $\mathbb{Z}_6$ identification and the line bundle $L_Y$ carrying the $1/6$-step lattice. Removing the $\mathbb{Z}_6$ rule, or replacing $L_Y$ with a real-valued attribute, would not deliver the correct $Y_f$ values, and the cancellation would fail.

*This result is consumed by [Appendix E](#appendix-e-chirality-and-anomaly-closure) (E.5 anomaly ledger; E.5.1 per-multiplet table), one of six anomaly traces that close.*

### C7.8.9  Why a Bare $\times$-Geometry Cannot Carry Matter

*The purpose of the next observation is to make explicit why $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ cannot be replaced by labels on the base.*

A bare base $\mathcal{M}_4 \times K_6 \times S^2 \times S_Y^{\,1}/\mathbb{Z}_2$ has *points* but no *fields*. A "point" of the base is a location $(x, y, z, w) \in \mathcal{M}_4 \times K_6 \times S^2 \times S_Y^{\,1}/\mathbb{Z}_2$. Nothing about a point tells you which kind of fermion sits there, what its spin is, what its color is, what its weak isospin is, what its hypercharge is, or which generation it belongs to. To carry that information, one must attach a fiber at every point — and the fiber must have enough internal structure to host all five kinds of labels.

The seven-factor tensor product is the minimal such fiber. Removing any factor removes one kind of label that the Standard Model demonstrably requires; adding an extra factor adds a label the Standard Model does not demonstrably require.

*This result is consumed by C7.10 (gate output map) and C7.12 (failure if removed); it is the structural justification for the entire matter side of the manuscript.*

---

## C7.9  Output Produced

| Output | Plain-English description | Object form | Authority |
|---|---|---|---|
| Seven-factor tensor product | The full matter bundle as a single composite object | $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ (C7.8.1) | A2.3 |
| Per-multiplet restrictions | One tensor product per Standard Model fermion | Six rows: $Q_L, u_R, d_R, L_L, e_R, \nu_R$ (C7.8.3, C7.8.4) | A2.3 |
| Chirality projector action space | Space on which $P_\chi$ acts | $\mathcal{S}_{\rm total} = S(\mathcal{M}_4) \otimes S(K_6) \otimes S(S^2) \otimes S(S_Y^{\,1})$ (C7.8.5) | A2.2 |
| Family count source | The integer $|\chi| = 3$ | Borel–Weil–Bott index on $K_6$ (C7.8.5) | R1.4 `0fd19c9ae0c1`; [Appendix E](#appendix-e-chirality-and-anomaly-closure) |
| Hypercharge eigenvalue ledger | $Y$ values per multiplet via $L_Y$ phase | C7.8.7 table; D.3.1 audit | R1.4 `44516f6400ae`; R1.3 `a68ee92a75be` |
| Anomaly arithmetic | Per-multiplet contribution to six anomaly traces | C7.8.8 ($[U(1)_Y]^3$); five more in E.5.1 | [Appendix E](#appendix-e-chirality-and-anomaly-closure) |
| Yukawa operator domains | Named source and target for every Yukawa map | $Y_u : \Pi_{Q_L} V_{F^+} \to \Pi_u V_{F^+}$, similar for $Y_d, Y_e, Y_\nu$ | A2.7; Appendices I, J |
| Macro-projector identity | Sector-orthogonality $\Pi_q M \Pi_\ell = 0$ as a tensor equality | Acts on $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter} \otimes \mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}^*$ | A2.8; L.3.1 |
| No new metric direction | $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ contributes $+0$ to $D$ | $D = 4 + 6 + 2 + 1 = 13$ unchanged | A1.9 |
| No new gauge factor | $\mathfrak{su}(3)_c \oplus \mathfrak{su}(2)_L \oplus \mathfrak{u}(1)_Y$ only | Surviving algebra in D.1 | A2.4 |

The matter bundle is therefore the most prolific single contributor to the active-branch outputs: seven of eleven gates consume content from it directly.

## C7.10  Gate Output Map

| Gate | $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ output consumed by this gate | How to explain it simply | Does C7 close the gate alone? |
|---|---|---|:---:|
| Gate 2 — Gauge recovery ($SU(3)_c \times SU(2)_L \times U(1)_Y$) | Per-multiplet representations $V_{\mathbf{3}}, V_{\mathbf{2}}, V_{\mathbf{1}}, L_Y$ via A2.3 rows | $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ tells each fermion which gauge representation it sits in | **No** — requires C8 ($\mathcal{E}_{\rm gauge}$) and [Appendix D](#appendix-d-standard-model-recovery) |
| Gate 3 — Hypercharge / electric charge | $L_Y$ eigenvalues $Y \in \tfrac{1}{6}\mathbb{Z}$; D.3.1 audit closes row by row | Hypercharge is a line-bundle eigenvalue, not a free attribute | **No** — requires the $\mathbb{Z}_6$ rule from C6 and [Appendix D](#appendix-d-standard-model-recovery) |
| Gate 4 — Chirality / family count / no mirrors | $P_\chi$ action; BWB index $\chi(K_6, \mathcal{E}) = -3$; ASP $(n_L, n_R) = (+3, 0)$ | The bundle picks out three left-handed families and no mirror partners | **No** — requires [Appendix E](#appendix-e-chirality-and-anomaly-closure) + the $\mathbb{Z}_2$ orbifold from C4 |
| Gate 5 — Anomaly cancellation | Per-multiplet $V_{SU(3)} \otimes V_{SU(2)} \otimes L_Y$ content; six traces sum to zero (C7.8.8 + E.5.1) | Anomalies cancel because the bundle carries exactly the SM representations with the SM hypercharges | **No** — requires [Appendix E](#appendix-e-chirality-and-anomaly-closure) |
| Gate 8 — Higgs Yukawa structure | Alignment of matter factors with Higgs factors so $\bar Q_L H d_R \to \mathbb{C}$ closes | The bundle factors are exactly what the Yukawa contractions need | **No** — requires C9 ($\mathcal{E}_{\rm Higgs}$) and [Appendix H](#appendix-h-higgs-protection) |
| Gate 9 — Flavor closure (Yukawa operator domains) | $V_{F^+}$ factor with sector projections; named domains and codomains for $Y_u, Y_d, Y_e, Y_\nu$ | The generation module gives every Yukawa map a definite source and target | **No** — requires C5 ($F^+$) and Appendices I / J / K |
| Gate 10 — Proton safety (sector-orthogonality leg) | $\Pi_q, \Pi_\ell$ on $V_{F^+}$; macro-projector identity $\Pi_q M \Pi_\ell = 0$ as a tensor equality | The matter bundle separates quark and lepton sectors at the tensor-factor level | **No** — requires [Appendix L](#appendix-l-proton-safety) |

**Required sentence.**

> $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ supplies necessary inputs to seven of eleven gates — the broadest gate impact in the [Appendix C](#appendix-c-term-by-term-construction-dossiers) series. The full gate closure happens only when $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ is combined with the other active-branch terms and the frozen gate-certificate appendices.

## C7.11  What This Term Does and Does Not Prove

**C7 records or proves:**

- the selected $\otimes$-layer matter bundle is the seven-factor tensor product $S_{3,1} \otimes S_{K_6}^{\,\rm spin^c} \otimes S_{S^2}^{\,\rm spin^c} \otimes L_Y \otimes V_{SU(3)} \otimes V_{SU(2)} \otimes V_{F^+}$;
- the per-multiplet restrictions of A2.3 (six rows) give every Standard Model fermion a complete tensor type;
- the chirality projector $P_\chi$ has a well-defined action space on $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$;
- the Borel–Weil–Bott index on $K_6$ is $\chi = -3$, sourcing the three-family count;
- the hypercharge $Y \in \tfrac{1}{6}\mathbb{Z}$ is a line-bundle eigenvalue, not a free attribute;
- the matter bundle factors align with the Higgs bundle for the Yukawa contractions;
- the sector projectors $\Pi_u, \Pi_d, \Pi_e, \Pi_\nu$ act on the $V_{F^+}$ factor and give every Yukawa map a definite domain and codomain;
- the macro-projector identity $\Pi_q M \Pi_\ell = 0$ is a tensor equality on $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$;
- $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ contributes $0$ to the metric dimension count;
- the surviving gauge algebra is exactly $\mathfrak{su}(3)_c \oplus \mathfrak{su}(2)_L \oplus \mathfrak{u}(1)_Y$ — no smuggled extra factor;
- $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ is **Retained** in the migration ledger (A3 row 30 — five-bundle matter table).

**C7 does not by itself prove:**

- **anomaly cancellation as a closed theorem** — the per-multiplet arithmetic (including C7.8.8) is recorded here, but the full ledger across six traces and the proof of consistency live in [Appendix E](#appendix-e-chirality-and-anomaly-closure) (now [Appendix E](#appendix-e-chirality-and-anomaly-closure) post-rename);
- **the flavor matrices and CKM / PMNS mixing** — these require the chamber Yukawa map $(Y_i)^{ab} = N_i \langle g_a | O_i | g_b\rangle$ from C5 plus the numerical outputs of Appendices I, J, K;
- **proton safety as a closed theorem** — the macro-projector identity is stated here, but the proof that no admissible operator can violate it lives in [Appendix L](#appendix-l-proton-safety) (now [Appendix L](#appendix-l-proton-safety));
- **the gauge bundle / Yang–Mills connection** — handled by C8 ($\mathcal{E}_{\rm gauge}$);
- **Higgs vacuum / electroweak breaking** — handled by C9 ($\mathcal{E}_{\rm Higgs}$) plus [Appendix H](#appendix-h-higgs-protection);
- **the family count from first principles** — the integer $-3$ comes from the BWB index on $K_6$ (sourced by C2 + R1.4 `0fd19c9ae0c1`), which $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ consumes but does not derive;
- **threshold unification or stabilization** — those are $\times$- and $\oplus$-layer outputs;
- **everything outside the active branch** — no dark matter, no sterile-$\nu$ tower beyond $\nu_R$, no leptogenesis, no SUSY partners through $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ (Section 9 boundary).

**Required sentence.**

> C7 is load-bearing but not self-sufficient. It supplies indispensable $\otimes$-layer outputs that are consumed by the larger active branch and the gate certificates.

This protects against overclaiming: $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ is the tensor home, not the gate proof.

---

## C7.12  Failure If Removed

| If removed | What becomes undefined or wrong | First gate affected |
|---|---|---|
| $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ entirely | Standard Model matter has no tensor home; $P_\chi$ has no action space; anomaly polynomial has no representation content; Yukawa has no domain or codomain; macro-projector identity has no tensor space | **Gates 2, 3, 4, 5, 8, 9, 10 all fail.** Matter side collapses |
| $S_{3,1}$ (4D Lorentz spinor) | Fermions lose 4D Dirac structure; 4D $\gamma_5$ has no action space; the chirality projector degenerates on the 4D factor | Gate 4 (no 4D chirality); Gates 8, 9, 10 follow |
| $S_{K_6}^{\,\rm spin^c}$ | Borel–Weil–Bott family count undefined; no topological source for $n_L = +3$ | Gate 4 (family count); Gate 5 ($[SU(3)_c]^3$ colored chiral content) |
| $S_{S^2}^{\,\rm spin^c}$ | Weak chirality undefined; no spinor mechanism for the doublet vs. singlet distinction | Gate 2 (weak routing); Gate 4 (weak chirality); Gate 5 (Witten anomaly) |
| $L_Y$ | $Y$ becomes a free real-valued attribute; $\mathbb{Z}_6$ quantization has nowhere to act; $Y$-step lattice is lost | Gate 3 (no $Y \in \tfrac{1}{6}\mathbb{Z}$); Gate 5 ($[U(1)_Y]^3$ arithmetic) |
| $V_{SU(3)}$ | Quarks have no color representation; A2.3 color column is missing | Gate 2 (color gauge); Gate 5 ($[SU(3)_c]^3$, $[SU(3)_c]^2 U(1)_Y$); Gate 10 ($\Pi_q$ split) |
| $V_{SU(2)}$ | Doublet vs. singlet labels lost; $T_3$ undefined; $Q = T_3 + Y$ is meaningless component-wise | Gate 2 (weak); Gate 3 ($Q$); Gate 5 (Witten); Gate 8 (Yukawa structure) |
| $V_{F^+}$ | Chamber operators have no codomain; sector projectors $\Pi_i$ have nowhere to act; the $\Pi_q, \Pi_\ell$ macro-projectors degenerate | **Gate 9 fails outright**; Gate 10 sector-orthogonality leg lost |
| Per-field restrictions in A2.3 | Per-multiplet chirality / charge / family / sector content is unnameable; A1.8 parity table has no consumer | Gates 2, 3, 4, 5, 8, 9, 10 lose audit-grade per-multiplet certificates |
| Cross-layer $\oplus$ inputs (isolated $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$) | Sector projectors have no defining data; $\mathbb{Z}_6$ phase undefined; $\mathbb{Z}_2$ parity decoration of $L_Y$ has no rulebook authority | Gates 4, 9, 10. $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ is a consumer of $\oplus$-rules, not self-contained |

**Plain-English conclusion.**

> Removing $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ is like removing the costume rack for every actor in the play. The actors (Standard Model fermions) would have no clothes — no spin label, no color label, no weak label, no hypercharge label, no generation label. The chirality projector would have nothing to project on; the anomaly polynomial would have nothing to sum over; the Yukawa operators would have nothing to map between. The matter side of the manuscript would cease to exist.

---

## C7.13  Minimality / Alternatives

The minimality argument for $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ has three branches: factor count, factor identity, and layer placement.

**Factor count: seven is exhaustive and minimal.**

- Less than seven: each omission opens at least one gate (C7.12 row-by-row).
- More than seven: an eighth factor must be sourced from a primitive object outside the active branch. The active branch has five primitive matter-side bundles (`0fd19c9ae0c1`, `1cb807d03288`, `44516f6400ae`, `3b8d68559f5e`, and the active-branch source `dcc66f1b2685`); $V_{SU(3)}, V_{SU(2)}$ are derived from those via associated-bundle constructions and the $\mathbb{Z}_6$ rule. An eighth factor would either duplicate an existing one or smuggle a hidden symmetry forbidden by R1.0a and Section 9.

**Factor identity: each of the seven is unique.**

| Factor | Could it be replaced by ...? | Outcome of substitution |
|---|---|---|
| $S_{3,1}$ | a 4D scalar | $P_\chi$ degenerates; pre-LEP measurements falsify |
| $S_{K_6}^{\,\rm spin^c}$ | a real spinor on $K_6$ | BWB index is no longer $-3$; family count free |
| $S_{S^2}^{\,\rm spin^c}$ | a real spinor on $S^2$ | doublet vs. singlet routing has no internal source |
| $L_Y$ | a real-valued $Y$ attribute | $\mathbb{Z}_6$ rule has nothing to act on; $Y$-quantization lost |
| $V_{SU(3)}$ | global "color label" | covariant derivative on each multiplet undefined |
| $V_{SU(2)}$ | global "weak label" | $T_3$ and $Q$ undefined component-wise |
| $V_{F^+}$ | multiplicity factor 3 | chamber operators have no codomain |

Every substitution opens at least one gate.

**Layer placement: $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ lives in $\otimes$, nowhere else.**

- It is not $\times$ (no metric direction; lives over the base, not in it).
- It is not $\oplus$ (no admissibility rule; consumes the rulebook supplied by C5 + C6).
- It is $\otimes$ — bundle / module / Hilbert content.

**Uniqueness clause.** Tensor product is commutative, so factor *ordering* is conventional; A2.3 uses spinor $\otimes$ internal-spinor $\otimes$ hypercharge $\otimes$ color $\otimes$ weak $\otimes$ flavor. Sub-decompositions like $S(K_6)^{\,\rm spin^c} = S(K_6)_{\rm bare} \otimes L_{c_1}$ or $V_{F^+} = \bigoplus_i \Pi_i V_{F^+}$ are accounting refinements that do not alter the top-level seven-factor count.

---

## C7.14  Layer Routing / Anti-Smuggling Check

| Layer | What belongs to C7 | What must be routed elsewhere |
|---|---|---|
| $\times$ | **None.** $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ is a tensor object, not a base factor. It *lives over* the $\times$-layer base $\mathcal{M}_4 \times K_6 \times S^2 \times S_Y^{\,1}/\mathbb{Z}_2$, but the base itself is supplied by C1–C4. | $\times$-layer base supplied by C1 ($\mathcal{M}_4$), C2 ($K_6$), C3 ($S^2$), C4 ($S_Y^{\,1}/\mathbb{Z}_2$). |
| $\oplus$ | **None native.** The $\mathbb{Z}_2$ parity decoration of $L_Y$, the $\mathbb{Z}_6$ phase rule, the spin-$\mathbb{C}$ flux normalization, the sector projectors $\Pi_i$, and the chamber identification $V_{F^+} = \mathcal{G}_{\rm gen}$ all live in C5 / C6. C7 *consumes* them; it does not *define* them. | $\oplus$-content supplied by C5 (chamber) and C6 (admissibility rules). |
| $\otimes$ | **The full contribution.** Seven-factor tensor product (C7.8.1); per-field restrictions (A2.3); $S_{3,1}^\pm$ chirality decomposition; spin-$\mathbb{C}$ family-count source; $L_Y$ phase as hypercharge eigenvalue; sector decomposition of $V_{F^+}$ via $\Pi_i$; Yukawa operators as tensor maps between named factors; macro-projectors $\Pi_q, \Pi_\ell$ and the identity $\Pi_q M \Pi_\ell = 0$. | — |

**Required sentence.**

> C7 provides the *fibers, bundles, modules, and operator-domain content* for the Standard Model fermion sector. The *base* on which those fibers sit and the *rules* by which they are decorated are not part of C7 itself; they are routed to the $\times$-layer dossiers (C1–C4) and the $\oplus$-layer dossiers (C5–C6).

**Layer-smuggling claim.** Nothing in C7 silently imports content from the $\times$- or $\oplus$-layers. The factors of $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ are *pure $\otimes$*: every one of them is a bundle, a representation module, or a finite generation module. The four bases on which the bundles live, the rules by which the bundles are decorated, and the chamber from which the generation module is identified are all **named cross-layer dependencies**, not smuggles. The dependencies are recorded explicitly in A1.13a.1 (cross-layer reference table) and A1.14.0 ($\otimes$-layer index with cross-references).

**No extra gauge factor smuggled.** The surviving gauge algebra acting on $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ is exactly $\mathfrak{su}(3)_c \oplus \mathfrak{su}(2)_L \oplus \mathfrak{u}(1)_Y$ (D.1). Every tensor factor carries only representations of this algebra; no component transforms under an unsourced generator.

**No extra chirality smuggled.** $P_\chi$ derives from the spin-$\mathbb{C}$ structures on $K_6, S^2$ plus the 4D $\gamma_5$ (A2.2). The no-mirror result is inherited from the $\mathbb{Z}_2$ orbifold (C4) and the ASP boundary index (E.3), not from an unsourced chirality rule.

**No extra admissibility rule smuggled.** $\mathbb{Z}_2$, $\mathbb{Z}_6$, and the spin-$\mathbb{C}$ flux normalization are all sourced from $\oplus$-layer A0 entries (`ac4d2df3e708`, `a68ee92a75be`, `03b30a9c931a`). $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ does not introduce a single admissibility rule of its own.

Any unsourced generator would falsify the term |

## C7.18  Comprehension Questions

After reading C7, a non-specialist reader should be able to answer the following ten questions in plain language.

1. **What is the job of $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$?**  $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ is the bundle that gives every Standard Model fermion its mathematical costume — spin, color, weak isospin, hypercharge, and generation — over the geometric stage built in C1–C4.

2. **Why are there seven factors and not fewer?**  Each of the seven factors carries one kind of label that the Standard Model demonstrably requires (4D spin, internal spin on $K_6$, internal spin on $S^2$, hypercharge, color, weak isospin, generation). Removing any single factor breaks at least one gate.

3. **Why are there seven factors and not more?**  An eighth factor would need to be sourced from a primitive object outside the active branch — and no such object exists. The four bases (C1–C4) and the one chamber (C5) supply exactly the primitive content needed for seven factors.

4. **What does $S_{K_6}^{\,\rm spin^c}$ do?**  It is the spinor bundle on the color/family shape $K_6$, with an extra spin-$\mathbb{C}$ twist that makes the family-count index well-defined. Its Borel–Weil–Bott index is $-3$, sourcing the three generations.

5. **What does $L_Y$ do?**  It is the hypercharge line bundle on the orbifold circle. The value of $Y$ on each multiplet is the eigenvalue of the $L_Y$ phase generator — not a free attribute but a derived quantity.

6. **Why is $Y \in \tfrac{1}{6}\mathbb{Z}$ rather than $Y \in \mathbb{R}$?**  Because of the global $\mathbb{Z}_6$ identification (R1.3 `a68ee92a75be`), which forces $Y$ to be an integer multiple of $1/6$. This is the quantization mechanism that a real-valued attribute cannot supply.

7. **How does the chirality projector $P_\chi$ act on this bundle?**  $P_\chi = \tfrac{1}{2}(1 + \gamma_5 \Gamma_8)$ acts on the four spinor factors ($S_{3,1}$ and the three internal spinors). It picks out the subspace with combined chirality eigenvalue $+1$. The orbifold $\mathbb{Z}_2$ on $S_Y^{\,1}$ then removes the would-be mirror sector, leaving three left-handed families.

8. **Why is $Q_L$'s tensor restriction 36-dimensional?**  Because $2 \cdot 1 \cdot 1 \cdot 3 \cdot 2 \cdot 3 = 36$, with the factors being 4D Weyl spin (2), internal $K_6$ spin (1, colored zero-mode), internal $S^2$ spin (1), color $\mathbf{3}$ (3), weak doublet (2), and three generations (3).

9. **What gates does $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ serve?**  Seven of eleven: Gate 2 (gauge), Gate 3 (hypercharge / electric charge), Gate 4 (chirality / family count), Gate 5 (anomaly), Gate 8 (Higgs Yukawa), Gate 9 (flavor closure), Gate 10 (proton safety). It does *not* close any gate alone — every gate also requires the relevant gate-certificate appendix.

10. **What would fail if $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ were removed?**  The chirality projector would have no action space, the anomaly polynomial would have no representation content, the Yukawa operators would have no domains, the macro-projector identity would have no tensor space, and every Standard Model fermion would lose its mathematical type. The matter side of the manuscript would collapse.

**One-sentence summary.**

> $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ is the seven-factor tensor-product bundle that supplies every Standard Model fermion with its complete set of gauge, chirality, and generation labels over the active-branch base — a pure $\otimes$-layer object that consumes the $\times$-layer base from C1–C4 and the $\oplus$-layer rules from C5–C6 but introduces no metric direction, no extra gauge factor, and no new admissibility rule of its own.

**Acceptance rule.**

> If a non-specialist reader cannot answer these without rereading advanced notation, this dossier still needs bridge sentences.

---

## C7.19  Closing Status

C7 is complete when the reader can follow this chain end-to-end:

```text
The base C1–C4 has no fields
  → the chamber C5 has rules but no action space
  → the rulebook C6 has rules but no tensor home
  → the matter bundle E_matter attaches a seven-factor fiber at every base point
  → the seven factors are 4D spin, K_6 spin, S^2 spin, L_Y, V_SU(3), V_SU(2), V_F+
  → every Standard Model multiplet has a specific restriction
  → P_chi acts on the spinor factors
  → anomaly polynomial sums over the named representation modules
  → Yukawa operators map between named sector projections of V_F+
  → macro-projector identity Pi_q M Pi_l = 0 is a tensor equality on E_matter
  → Gates 2, 3, 4, 5, 8, 9, 10 consume these outputs
  → removing any factor breaks the chain at one or more of those gates
```

**Status.** *Certificate-complete (tensor-product construction).* Every constraint of C7.3 / C7.4 is named with an empirical / structural source and a frozen-object response. Every alternative of C7.5 is eliminated with a stated first-failed gate. The chirality projector of C7.8.5, the family-count source of $\chi = -3$, the hypercharge ledger of C7.8.7, the cubic anomaly arithmetic of C7.8.8, and the failure-if-removed cascade of C7.12 are all routed to authority pointers in C7.15. The hostile-review certificate of C7.16 is auditable against A0 / A1 / A2 / A3 / [Appendix D](#appendix-d-standard-model-recovery) / [Appendix E](#appendix-e-chirality-and-anomaly-closure) / [Appendix H](#appendix-h-higgs-protection) / Appendices I, J, K / [Appendix L](#appendix-l-proton-safety). The reviewer attack matrix of C7.17 includes the two specific falsification paths requested (factor-substitution and extra-gauge-factor smuggle), plus ten additional rows that close standard structural challenges.

**Failure conditions for C7 itself.** C7 fails if (i) any R1 hash cited in C7.15 does not regenerate from manifest meta-hash `a5b1e6f9d951`; (ii) any reviewer exhibits a six-factor (or smaller) tensor home that still closes Gates 2, 3, 4, 5, 8, 9, 10 for every Standard Model multiplet; (iii) any of the seven tensor factors is shown to be replaceable by a label / multiplicity coefficient without opening any of Gates 2, 3, 4, 5, 8, 9, 10; (iv) the layer-smuggling check of C7.14 is found to silently inherit content across layers (e.g., an unsourced gauge factor inside one of the seven tensor factors); (v) the Borel–Weil–Bott index on $K_6$ fails to reproduce $\chi = -3$ from the line-bundle data of R1.4 `0fd19c9ae0c1`; or (vi) the cubic anomaly arithmetic of C7.8.8 fails to sum to zero on the per-multiplet table.

**Final sentence.**

> $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ is retained because it is the smallest readable and auditable $\otimes$-layer matter bundle in the declared active branch that gives every Standard Model fermion a complete tensor type, supports the chirality projector with the correct family count, carries hypercharge as a line-bundle eigenvalue with the correct quantization, and routes every Yukawa map and macro-projector to named domains. The general reader gets the story; the formal reviewer gets the proof trail.
