# Appendix C9 — $\mathcal{E}_{\rm Higgs}$ Dossier: Wilson-Line Higgs / Hierarchy Protection

**Load-bearing role.** Authoritative source for the term-level necessity of $\mathcal{E}_{\rm Higgs}$ in the active branch per the Claim-to-Appendix Authority Map (front matter). Conflicts with other appendices on whether $\mathcal{E}_{\rm Higgs}$ is retained or with what failure-if-removed signature are resolved by this dossier.

**Downstream impact (per the Appendix Dependency Graph, front matter).** If this dossier's term-necessity claim is downgraded under the R2 Downgrade Rules — i.e., if a reviewer shows the term is removable or smuggled — every gate certificate that depends on $\mathcal{E}_{\rm Higgs}$ drops one rung in Section 6, and the dossier's failure-if-removed table (C9.8 or equivalent) records the propagation path. Gates affected: Gate 8 (Wilson-line Higgs with winding $n_H = 1$; no other gate depends on the Higgs bundle directly).

> **How to read this dossier.** Sections C9.0 through C9.13 are the *high-level summary layer*: they explain what the $\otimes$-layer Wilson-line Higgs bundle is, why it is there, what it does, and what it does **not** do, in ordinary language with the formal symbols introduced gradually. Sections C9.14 through C9.19 are the *formal-review layer*: they translate the high-level content into a formal certificate with R1 hashes, gate output maps, layer-smuggling check, hostile-review certificate, and reviewer attack matrix. A non-specialist reader should be able to read C9.0–C9.13 cover-to-cover. A skeptical reviewer should be able to audit C9.14–C9.19 against the rest of the manuscript.

---

## C9.0 One-Page Summary

The Standard Model has one famous internal-stress problem called the **hierarchy problem**. The observed Higgs particle has a mass of about $125$ GeV. The natural high scale of any grand-unified construction is around $M_U \sim 10^{16}$ GeV. The Higgs is sensitive to arbitrary high-scale corrections — and that sensitivity, in a standard scalar-field treatment, would push its mass up by roughly fourteen orders of magnitude unless the corrections are cancelled by an exquisitely tuned counterterm. A scalar mass that needs $10^{32}\,\mathrm{GeV}^2$-scale cancellations to within parts per $10^{30}$ to land at the observed value is not a structural prediction; it is a tuned number.

$\mathcal{E}_{\rm Higgs}$ avoids this entirely by **not** introducing the Higgs as a freely tunable scalar in the first place. Instead, the Higgs degree of freedom is the **Wilson line** of the gauge connection around a specific non-contractible cycle $\gamma$ in the compact gauge geometry. The Wilson line is the object

$$\boxed{\;W_\gamma \;=\; \mathcal{P}\exp\!\left(\,i \oint_\gamma A\,\right),\;}$$

and the relevant fact is that its **winding number**

$$n_H \;=\; \frac{1}{2\pi i}\oint_\gamma A \;\in\; \mathbb{Z}$$

is an integer. The active branch has $n_H = 1$. An integer cannot be continuously deformed: any putative high-scale correction that would try to shift the Higgs mass through a smooth interpolation must, somewhere along the path, pass through a non-integer winding, which is topologically forbidden on a closed cycle. The Higgs mass therefore inherits **topological protection** from the integer winding rather than requiring fine-tuned scalar counterterms at the high scale.

The four-dimensional Higgs doublet $H(x)$ sitting in the Standard Model representation

$$H \;\in\; (\mathbf{1},\, \mathbf{2},\, +\tfrac{1}{2})\quad\text{under}\quad G_{\rm SM} \;=\; SU(3)_c \times SU(2)_L \times U(1)_Y / \mathbb{Z}_6$$

emerges as the surviving Wilson-line mode after Kaluza–Klein reduction of the higher-dimensional gauge field along the cycle $\gamma$. The Higgs is **not** an inserted scalar; it is a holonomy.

**In one sentence:**

> $\mathcal{E}_{\rm Higgs}$ is the $\otimes$-layer Wilson-line bundle whose holonomy around a non-contractible cycle $\gamma$ in the gauge geometry supplies the Standard Model Higgs doublet, and whose integer winding number $n_H = 1$ supplies topological protection of the Higgs mass against continuous high-scale corrections.

**What C9 does not claim.** C9 does **not** claim to solve every naturalness problem in physics; does **not** predict the Higgs mass from first principles (the mass still depends on the Wilson-line modulus pinned by chamber data); and does **not** explain the full electroweak hierarchy in all its facets (Section 7 / Gate 11 boundary). C9 protects against one specific failure mode: the unbounded radiative pull of a fundamental scalar mass toward the unification scale. That is a well-defined and limited claim.

---

## C9.1 The Problem This Term Solves

C9 answers one question:

> Where does the Standard Model Higgs doublet come from in the active branch, and what mechanism keeps its mass at the electroweak scale rather than at the unification scale?

The answer cannot be "just write down a scalar field $H$ with a free mass $m_H$." A free mass parameter is a per-entry tuning that violates the freeze-before-compare discipline of [Appendix B1](#appendix-b1-constraint-selector-occams-razor-formalism) and the over-determination standard (§4.9; §5.8.3). A free scalar mass at low scale, with no structural mechanism preventing it from running to the high scale, is exactly the textbook hierarchy problem.

The selected mechanism must do six jobs:

1. supply a four-dimensional Higgs doublet field $H(x)$ in the representation $(\mathbf{1}, \mathbf{2}, +\tfrac{1}{2})$ of $G_{\rm SM}$;
2. give the doublet a non-trivial vacuum expectation value $v_{\rm EW} \approx 246$ GeV that breaks electroweak symmetry to $U(1)_{\rm em}$;
3. supply a mechanism that protects the Higgs mass against continuous high-scale corrections;
4. allow the Higgs mass $m_h$ to come out at the electroweak scale (not the unification scale) as a structural consequence;
5. integrate with the existing chamber-data structure of the active branch so that the same input that pins the Higgs sector also produces another, independent Standard Model output;
6. do all of this without introducing free per-entry counterterms.

The Wilson-line Higgs bundle does all six jobs. Job 1 is the bundle factorisation $L_\gamma \otimes V_{SU(2),\,\mathbf{2}} \otimes L_{Y=+1/2}$. Job 2 is the Hosotani phase minimum. Job 3 is the integer winding $n_H$. Job 4 is the finite one-loop Hosotani potential. Job 5 is the structural identity $1/\eta_{BK} = |y_t/y_b|$ (raw determinant, before $K_{tb}/R_{tb}$ running to $M_Z$). Job 6 is satisfied because $\eta_{BK}$ is a closed-form expression in $\pi$ and $\sqrt{3}$ with no free fit parameter.

**Summary.** A scalar field with a free mass parameter has no built-in scale. A Wilson-line holonomy has a built-in topological invariant (the integer winding number) that cannot be smoothly deformed. C9 is about replacing the first with the second.

---

## C9.2 Vocabulary Before Symbols

C9 uses a number of words and symbols that have everyday counterparts and technical ones. The table is the reader's translator. Every symbol that appears in the math ladder of C9.8 is listed here first.

| Word / symbol | Plain-English meaning | Technical role in C9 |
|---|---|---|
| Higgs doublet $H$ | The Standard Model field whose vacuum value breaks the electroweak symmetry | A $(\mathbf{1}, \mathbf{2}, +\tfrac{1}{2})$ representation of $G_{\rm SM}$ realised as a Wilson-line mode |
| $G_{\rm SM}$ | The Standard Model gauge group | $SU(3)_c \times SU(2)_L \times U(1)_Y / \mathbb{Z}_6$ |
| $K_{\rm gauge}$ | The compact internal geometry that carries the gauge sector | $K_6 \times S^2 \times S_Y^{\,1}/\mathbb{Z}_2$ from C2, C3, C4 |
| Cycle $\gamma$ | A small closed loop sitting inside the internal geometry that cannot be shrunk to a point | A non-contractible one-cycle in $K_{\rm gauge}$ (R1.5 hash `640e1d7f7773`) |
| $\gamma \subset K_{\rm gauge}$ | The cycle lives inside the base | The cycle locus is a one-sub-manifold of $K_{\rm gauge}$ |
| Wilson line $W_\gamma$ | The phase wound by the gauge field around the cycle | $W_\gamma = \mathcal{P}\exp(i \oint_\gamma A)$ |
| Gauge connection $A$ | The internal vector field whose curvature gives the gauge field strength | One-form on $K_{\rm gauge}$ valued in the gauge algebra |
| Path ordering $\mathcal{P}$ | The instruction to multiply matrix-valued factors along a path in the correct sequence | Required because $A$ is matrix-valued and non-commutative |
| Holonomy | The end-to-end phase change after traversing a closed loop | The value of $W_\gamma$ on a closed cycle |
| Winding number $n_H$ | An integer that counts how many full $2\pi$ phase wraps the Wilson line accumulates around the cycle | $n_H = (1/2\pi i)\oint_\gamma A \in \mathbb{Z}$ (R1.5 hash `f65094fd8fd1`) |
| Topological invariant | A discrete quantity that cannot be changed by continuous deformation | The integer-valued winding $n_H$ |
| Line bundle $L_\gamma$ | The mathematical object whose holonomy data is the Wilson line | Wilson-line bundle along the cycle (R1.4 hash `2a0462b8aab9`) |
| $V_{SU(2),\,\mathbf{2}}$ | The doublet representation module of $SU(2)_L$ | Tensor factor placing $H$ in $\mathbf{2}$ of $SU(2)_L$ |
| $L_{Y=+1/2}$ | The hypercharge line bundle at hypercharge $+\tfrac{1}{2}$ | Tensor factor placing $H$ at $Y = +\tfrac{1}{2}$ (R1.4 factor `44516f6400ae`) |
| Hosotani phase $\theta_H$ | A periodic angle on $[0, 2\pi)$ parametrising the Wilson-line vacuum | Vacuum modulus for the Wilson-line VEV |
| $\theta_H^\star$ | The frozen minimum of the Hosotani phase after one-loop dynamics | Output of the chamber-determined Hosotani potential |
| Cycle radius $R_\gamma$ | The natural length scale of the cycle | Sets the inverse compactification scale on the cycle |
| Electroweak VEV $v_{\rm EW}$ | The observed $246$ GeV Higgs vacuum expectation value | $v_{\rm EW} = \theta_H^\star / (2\pi R_\gamma)$ |
| Higgs mass $m_h$ | The mass of the physical Higgs boson | $m_h^2 = (d^2 V_{\rm Hos}/d\theta_H^2)\big|_{\theta_H^\star} / (2\pi R_\gamma)^2$ |
| Hosotani potential $V_{\rm Hos}$ | The one-loop effective potential for $\theta_H$ generated by KK modes | Finite, periodic, cutoff-independent function of $\theta_H$ |
| Chamber determinant $\eta_{BK}$ | A finite chamber datum of the $F^+$ flavor chamber | $\eta_{BK} = 0.009721281516312$ (R1.6 hash `84e94518d3f5`) |
| $1/\eta_{BK} = |y_t/y_b|$ | The structural identity that ties one chamber input to two Standard Model outputs | $32\pi \exp(+\sqrt{3}/(24\pi)) \approx 102.87$ |
| Hierarchy problem | The standard puzzle of why a scalar mass $\ll$ high cutoff is not destroyed by radiative corrections | The problem C9 protects against |
| Topological protection | The property that a discrete invariant cannot be changed by continuous corrections | The mechanism C9 uses |
| Layer $\times$ | The base-geometry layer | Hosts the cycle locus inside $K_{\rm gauge}$ |
| Layer $\oplus$ | The admissibility-rule layer | Hosts the winding-quantisation rule and chamber data |
| Layer $\otimes$ | The bundle / operator layer | Hosts $\mathcal{E}_{\rm Higgs}$ itself |

**Rule.** No technical word or symbol may be used in a load-bearing sentence in C9 without first appearing in this table.

---

## C9.3 The Constraint in Plain English

The active branch must include an internal mechanism that:

1. produces a Standard Model Higgs doublet in $(\mathbf{1}, \mathbf{2}, +\tfrac{1}{2})$;
2. produces an electroweak vacuum expectation value of about $246$ GeV;
3. protects the Higgs mass against arbitrary continuous high-scale corrections;
4. does not introduce a free per-entry counterterm at the unification scale;
5. produces the Higgs mass at the electroweak scale as a structural output;
6. shares its key input with at least one other Standard Model output, so that the input is not a single-purpose fit parameter;
7. is consistent with the heat-kernel threshold ledger.

**Plain-English constraint.**

> Find the minimal Wilson-line / topological-protection mechanism that supplies the Standard Model Higgs doublet, produces the observed electroweak vacuum expectation value, and protects the Higgs mass against continuous high-scale corrections — using a single integer-valued topological invariant rather than a free scalar counterterm.

---

## C9.4 Formal Constraint Statement

**Formal constraint (Minimal Topologically-Protected Higgs Mechanism).**

> The active branch must include an $\otimes$-layer bundle whose holonomy around a non-contractible cycle $\gamma \subset K_{\rm gauge}$ supplies the $(\mathbf{1}, \mathbf{2}, +\tfrac{1}{2})$ Higgs doublet of $G_{\rm SM}$. The integer winding number $n_H = (1/2\pi i)\oint_\gamma A$ must lie in $\mathbb{Z}$, with $n_H = 1$ on the active branch. The one-loop effective potential for the Hosotani phase $\theta_H$ on $[0, 2\pi)$ must be finite, periodic, and cutoff-independent. Its minimum $\theta_H^\star$ must produce $v_{\rm EW} = \theta_H^\star / (2\pi R_\gamma)$ compatible with $246$ GeV at the post-RG band of the active chamber. The same chamber determinant $\eta_{BK}$ that pins $\theta_H^\star$ must satisfy the structural identity $1/\eta_{BK} = 32\pi\exp(+\sqrt{3}/(24\pi)) = |y_t/y_b|$ (raw determinant, before $K_{tb}/R_{tb}$ running to $M_Z$), producing a second independent Standard Model output. No per-entry counterterm $m_H^2 H^\dagger H$ at the unification scale is permitted.

This is the operative constraint for C9; the alternatives in C9.5 are evaluated against it. The constraint is stated twice — once in plain English (C9.3) and once in formal language (C9.4) — to satisfy the manuscript's dual-audience discipline.

---

## C9.5 Candidate Choices

| Choice | Plain-English version | Why it fails or passes |
|---|---|---|
| Tree-level fundamental scalar Higgs with free mass $m_H^2$ | Just write a scalar field with its own mass parameter | **Fails.** Receives quadratically divergent radiative corrections $\delta m_H^2 \sim \Lambda_{\rm UV}^2 \sim M_U^2 \sim 10^{32}\,\mathrm{GeV}^2$. The observed $m_h \approx 125$ GeV becomes a tuned cancellation across $\sim 14$ orders of magnitude — a per-entry free parameter, not a frozen output. Constraint 3 / 4 / 5 fail. |
| Supersymmetric scalar Higgs with superpartner cancellation | Add superpartners to cancel the corrections | **Outside search category R2.** Supersymmetry is not part of the declared search category of [Appendix B1](#appendix-b1-constraint-selector-occams-razor-formalism) / R2; the active branch does not invoke superpartners. A valid solution in a different category; not comparable inside R2. |
| Composite Higgs at strong-coupling cutoff | A bound state of a strongly coupled UV completion | **Outside search category R2.** Composite Higgs replaces topological protection with a strong-coupling condensate; the UV completion is a separate category. |
| Wilson-line Higgs with trivial winding $n_H = 0$ | A contractible Wilson line | **Fails.** No Wilson-line phase ($W_\gamma$ trivial); Hosotani potential's $\theta_H$-dependent part vanishes; $v_{\rm EW} = 0$; no electroweak symmetry breaking. Constraints 1 / 2 / 3 fail. |
| Wilson-line Higgs with $n_H \geq 2$ | Higher winding | **Fails.** $v_{\rm pred}$ scales by integer factor; PDG band missed by $\sim 60\sigma$ for $n_H = 2$; structural identity $1/\eta_{BK} = |y_t/y_b|$ (raw determinant, before $K_{tb}/R_{tb}$ running to $M_Z$) no longer aligns. Constraint 2 / 6 fail. |
| Wilson-line Higgs with $n_H = 1$ but no chamber pinning | Wilson line with a freely fitted $\theta_H^\star$ | **Fails.** Loses the structural identity $1/\eta_{BK} \to |y_t/y_b|$; the chamber compression of 1 input into 2 outputs collapses. Constraint 6 fails. |
| Wilson-line Higgs with $n_H = 1$, no doublet factor | Singlet Wilson-line scalar | **Fails.** Surviving mode is a $G_{\rm SM}$ singlet, not a doublet; Standard Model Yukawas have no $H$ to couple to. Constraint 1 fails. |
| Wilson-line Higgs with $n_H = 1$, no hypercharge factor | $Y = 0$ Wilson-line doublet | **Fails.** After electroweak breaking, the surviving photon is not the linear combination $T_3 + Y$; the charge table of [Appendix D](#appendix-d-standard-model-recovery) is broken. Constraint 1 fails. |
| $\boxed{\mathcal{E}_{\rm Higgs} = L_\gamma \otimes V_{SU(2),\,\mathbf{2}} \otimes L_{Y=+1/2},\; n_H = 1}$ | Wilson-line Higgs bundle with minimal integer winding | **Passes.** Doublet in correct representation; integer-winding protection on the mass; chamber-determined VEV at $246$ GeV; structural identity to $|y_t/y_b|$ from the same chamber determinant. |

The selected object is the Wilson-line bundle with $n_H = 1$ because it is the unique surviving candidate inside R2 that closes Constraints 1 through 7 simultaneously.

**Summary.** A free scalar mass has no built-in scale and receives unbounded radiative pulls. An integer-valued topological invariant cannot be smoothly deformed. The Wilson-line construction replaces a free continuous parameter with a discrete invariant.

---

## C9.6 Chosen Object

The selected object is

$$\boxed{\;\mathcal{E}_{\rm Higgs} \;=\; L_\gamma \;\otimes\; V_{SU(2),\,\mathbf{2}} \;\otimes\; L_{Y = +1/2}, \qquad n_H \;=\; \frac{1}{2\pi i}\oint_\gamma A \;=\; 1.\;}$$

The three tensor factors carry distinct roles:

- $L_\gamma$ — the Wilson-line line bundle along the cycle $\gamma \subset K_{\rm gauge}$ carrying the $SU(2)_L$-direction holonomy (R1.4 hash `2a0462b8aab9`); this is the load-bearing topological factor;
- $V_{SU(2),\,\mathbf{2}}$ — the doublet representation module of $SU(2)_L$; this is what places the Higgs in $\mathbf{2}$;
- $L_{Y = +1/2}$ — the hypercharge line bundle at $Y = +\tfrac{1}{2}$ (factor of R1.4 hash `44516f6400ae`); this is what places the Higgs at the correct hypercharge so that the broken-vacuum photon is the standard $T_3 + Y$ combination.

The cycle $\gamma$ is the declared Wilson-line cycle of R1.5 hash `640e1d7f7773`; the winding number $n_H = 1$ is frozen by R1.5 hash `f65094fd8fd1`.

**Layer classification.** $\mathcal{E}_{\rm Higgs}$ is **pure $\otimes$** as an object. The cycle $\gamma$ lives in the $\times$-layer base. The winding-quantisation rule ($n_H \in \mathbb{Z}$) and the lower-winding-exclusion rule are $\oplus$-layer admissibility entries. The chamber determinant $\eta_{BK}$ that pins $\theta_H^\star$ is $\oplus$-layer chamber data from C5.

**Metric-dimension contribution.** $\mathcal{E}_{\rm Higgs}$ contributes $0$ to the active-branch propagating metric-dimension count $D = 4 + 6 + 2 + 1 = 13$ (A1.9). The cycle $\gamma$ is a one-sub-manifold interior to $K_{\rm gauge}$, not a new propagating compact factor. The Hosotani phase $\theta_H$ is a periodic angle, not a propagating direction.

---

## C9.7 Construction Explained Without Jargon

The construction has four steps.

**Step 1 — Pick a small closed loop inside the internal geometry.** The compact internal geometry $K_{\rm gauge}$ has many one-dimensional closed paths inside it. Most of them can be smoothly shrunk to a point. A few cannot — those are called non-contractible. The cycle $\gamma$ is one such non-contractible loop. Its position and shape are declared (R1.5 hash `640e1d7f7773`). Intuitively, it is the small loop that an internal $SU(2)_L$-style rotation would trace out inside the gauge geometry.

**Step 2 — Measure the gauge phase wound around the loop.** A gauge connection $A$ is a vector-field-like quantity on the internal geometry. Walking once around the loop $\gamma$ while carrying the gauge field accumulates a phase. The accumulated phase is captured by the Wilson line $W_\gamma$. Because the loop is closed, the phase forms a holonomy.

**Step 3 — Count how many full $2\pi$ wraps the phase completes.** The phase accumulated around a closed loop has to be a single-valued group element when written as a holonomy. This forces the *integer* count of full $2\pi$ phase windings, the **winding number** $n_H$. The active branch has $n_H = 1$ — a single full wind. Two or more winds would be a different topological class; zero winds would mean a contractible Wilson line with no phase.

**Step 4 — Read off the Higgs doublet from the surviving holonomy mode.** After Kaluza–Klein reduction of the higher-dimensional gauge field along the cycle $\gamma$, one mode survives in four dimensions: a doublet in the $SU(2)_L$ direction with hypercharge $+\tfrac{1}{2}$. That mode *is* the Standard Model Higgs $H(x)$. It is not separately inserted; it is the four-dimensional remnant of the higher-dimensional gauge field's holonomy along $\gamma$.

**Analogy for the protection.** Imagine an elastic band wrapped exactly once around the rim of a coffee mug. You can stretch the band, push it side to side, change its tension — but you cannot continuously change the number of times it wraps the rim. To go from "wrapped once" to "wrapped zero times" you would have to cut the band. The wrap count is a discrete invariant of *how the band sits on the mug*, not of its local stretch state. The Higgs winding number $n_H = 1$ behaves the same way: small high-scale perturbations can stretch or shift the Wilson-line phase but cannot change the integer winding, and so they cannot drive the Higgs mass continuously from the electroweak scale up to the unification scale.

**Bridging back to the Standard Model.** The four-dimensional Higgs $H(x)$ derived from the Wilson-line mode is the same Higgs that LHC measured. Its quantum numbers $(\mathbf{1}, \mathbf{2}, +\tfrac{1}{2})$ are dictated by the bundle factorisation. Its vacuum expectation value $v_{\rm EW} = \theta_H^\star / (2\pi R_\gamma)$ is the frozen Wilson-line phase divided by the cycle's natural scale. Its mass is the curvature of the Hosotani potential at the minimum, divided by the same scale. None of these is a free parameter — each is fixed by the cycle geometry and the chamber-frozen Hosotani dynamics.

---

## C9.8 Formula-by-Formula Math Ladder

This subsection is the explicit math ladder. Every symbol used here appears first in the vocabulary table of C9.2. Every formula is wrapped in four parts: (i) a one-sentence purpose statement; (ii) the formula itself; (iii) a plain-English readout of what each symbol means here; (iv) a one-sentence statement of which gate or downstream result consumes the formula.

### C9.8.1 Wilson-line formula

*The purpose of the next formula is to define the Wilson line as the path-ordered exponential of the gauge connection around the cycle.*

$$\boxed{\;W_\gamma \;=\; \mathcal{P}\exp\!\left(\,i \oint_\gamma A\,\right).\;}$$

*This means: $A$ is the gauge connection one-form on $K_{\rm gauge}$, $\oint_\gamma$ is the line integral around the closed cycle $\gamma$, $\mathcal{P}$ is the path-ordering symbol that keeps non-commuting matrix-valued factors in the right order, and $W_\gamma$ is the resulting holonomy — the group element that captures the end-to-end phase rotation around $\gamma$.*

*This result is consumed by C9.8.4 (the integer-quantisation argument) and by Gate 8 (Higgs origin) because $W_\gamma$ is the object whose vacuum value is the Higgs doublet.*

### C9.8.2 Identification of the cycle $\gamma$

*The purpose of the next statement is to fix which loop inside the gauge geometry is the Wilson-line cycle.*

$$\gamma \;\subset\; K_{\rm gauge} \;=\; K_6 \times S^2 \times S_Y^{\,1}/\mathbb{Z}_2, \qquad [\gamma] \in H_1(K_{\rm gauge}; \mathbb{Z}) \;\setminus\; \{0\}.$$

*This means: $\gamma$ is a non-contractible one-cycle in $K_{\rm gauge}$ — that is, a closed loop that cannot be smoothly shrunk to a point. Its homology class $[\gamma]$ is the non-trivial generator of $H_1(K_{\rm gauge}; \mathbb{Z})$ in the $SU(2)_L$-direction. The explicit cycle is declared in R1.5 hash `640e1d7f7773`. The cycle radius $R_\gamma \sim 1/(2\pi M_U)$ is derived from the base radii (R1.2) and sets the natural scale of the holonomy.*

*This result is consumed by C9.8.3 (Wilson-line gauge connection on the cycle) and by C9.8.6 (electroweak VEV formula).*

### C9.8.3 Path ordering $\mathcal{P}$ in plain language

*The purpose of the next statement is to explain the path-ordering symbol $\mathcal{P}$ in the Wilson-line formula.*

> $\mathcal{P}$ stands for *path ordering*. The gauge connection $A$ is matrix-valued (it takes values in the gauge Lie algebra), and matrices do not generally commute. When the exponential of a line integral of a matrix-valued one-form is computed, the order in which factors at different points along the path are multiplied matters. The symbol $\mathcal{P}$ tells the formula to place factors corresponding to later points on the path to the *left* of factors at earlier points — i.e., to keep the multiplication sequence in the same order as the path traversal.

*For an abelian component of $A$ (e.g., a $U(1)$ subgroup embedded in $SU(2)_L$ that carries the Higgs-doublet holonomy), matrices commute and $\mathcal{P}$ trivialises:*

$$W_\gamma\big|_{\rm abelian} \;=\; \exp\!\left(\,i \oint_\gamma A\,\right).$$

*This is the case relevant for the integer-winding argument in C9.8.4. The path ordering is not load-bearing for the topological protection; it is load-bearing for the non-abelian structure of the gauge content.*

*This result is consumed by C9.8.4 (where the abelian projection lets us read off the winding number directly).*

### C9.8.4 Winding-number quantisation

*The purpose of the next formula is to state the integer-quantisation rule that protects the Higgs mass.*

$$\boxed{\;n_H \;=\; \frac{1}{2\pi i}\oint_\gamma A \;\in\; \mathbb{Z}, \qquad n_H \;=\; 1\ \text{on the active branch}\ \text{(A0.5 hash @@PROT12@@).}\;}$$

*This means: the line integral of the gauge connection around the closed cycle, divided by $2\pi i$, is an integer. This is forced by the requirement that the Wilson-line operator $W_\gamma = \exp(i \oint_\gamma A)$ on a closed cycle be a well-defined single-valued group element under gauge transformations; a non-integer value would make $W_\gamma$ multi-valued. The integer $n_H$ is the first Chern number of the line bundle $L_\gamma$ restricted to the cycle.*

*The active branch is committed to $n_H = 1$. Smaller positive integers do not exist; $n_H = 0$ gives a contractible (trivial) Wilson line with no phase; $n_H \geq 2$ shifts $v_{\rm pred}$ off the PDG band.*

*This result is consumed by Gate 8 (Higgs protection) — the integer-quantisation is the topological protection mechanism — and by the chamber-consistency check that the structural identity $1/\eta_{BK} = |y_t/y_b|$ (raw determinant, before $K_{tb}/R_{tb}$ running to $M_Z$) aligns with PDG.*

### C9.8.5 Higgs-doublet routing

*The purpose of the next statement is to identify which Standard Model representation the Higgs doublet falls into.*

$$\boxed{\;H \;\in\; (\mathbf{1}, \mathbf{2}, +\tfrac{1}{2})\quad\text{under}\quad G_{\rm SM} \;=\; SU(3)_c \times SU(2)_L \times U(1)_Y / \mathbb{Z}_6.\;}$$

*This means: the Higgs is a colour singlet ($\mathbf{1}$ of $SU(3)_c$), a weak doublet ($\mathbf{2}$ of $SU(2)_L$), and carries hypercharge $+\tfrac{1}{2}$ of $U(1)_Y$. These are exactly the quantum numbers of the Standard Model Higgs doublet measured at LHC. The bundle factorisation $\mathcal{E}_{\rm Higgs} = L_\gamma \otimes V_{SU(2),\,\mathbf{2}} \otimes L_{Y=+1/2}$ supplies exactly these quantum numbers: $L_\gamma$ carries the Wilson-line topology along the $SU(2)_L$-direction cycle, $V_{SU(2),\,\mathbf{2}}$ supplies the doublet structure, and $L_{Y=+1/2}$ supplies the hypercharge.*

*This result is consumed by Gate 8 (Higgs origin) and Gate 7 (electroweak structure) because it is the bridge between the higher-dimensional Wilson-line bundle and the four-dimensional Standard Model field content.*

### C9.8.6 Hosotani phase and electroweak VEV formula

*The purpose of the next formula is to give the electroweak vacuum expectation value as a projection of the Wilson-line holonomy.*

Writing the Wilson-line gauge connection along the cycle as

$$A_\gamma \;=\; \frac{\theta_H}{2\pi R_\gamma}\, T_H,$$

with $T_H$ the $SU(2)_L$-generator selecting the Higgs-doublet direction and $\theta_H \in [0, 2\pi)$ the periodic Hosotani phase, the doublet vacuum expectation value is

$$\boxed{\;\langle H \rangle \;=\; \frac{\theta_H}{2\pi R_\gamma}, \qquad v_{\rm EW} \;=\; \frac{\theta_H^\star}{2\pi R_\gamma}.\;}$$

*This means: $\theta_H$ is the periodic Wilson-line phase modulus on $[0, 2\pi)$, $R_\gamma$ is the cycle radius, $T_H$ is the Lie-algebra generator picking out the surviving doublet direction, and the four-dimensional Higgs VEV is the value of $\theta_H$ at its dynamically frozen minimum $\theta_H^\star$, divided by the cycle's geometric scale $2\pi R_\gamma$. The chamber-frozen output is $v_{\rm pred} = 246.02 \pm 3.5$ GeV ([Appendix H](#appendix-h-higgs-protection).4), inside the PDG band of $246.22$ GeV at $0.06\sigma_{\rm th}$.*

*This result is consumed by Gate 8 (Higgs protection — VEV output) and by Gate 7 (electroweak structure — broken-vacuum charge pattern).*

### C9.8.7 Higgs-mode emergence after KK reduction

*The purpose of the next statement is to explain how the four-dimensional Higgs $H(x)$ on spacetime $\mathcal{M}_4$ emerges from the higher-dimensional Wilson-line mode.*

After Kaluza–Klein reduction of the higher-dimensional gauge connection $A_M(x, y)$ (with $x$ a coordinate on $\mathcal{M}_4$ and $y$ a coordinate on $K_{\rm gauge}$) along the cycle $\gamma$:

$$A_\gamma(x, y) \;=\; \sum_{n} H_n(x)\, f_n(y),$$

where $f_n(y)$ are the KK mode functions along the cycle and $H_n(x)$ are the four-dimensional mode coefficients. The zero-mode coefficient $H_0(x)$ is identified with the four-dimensional Higgs doublet:

$$\boxed{\;H(x) \;\equiv\; H_0(x) \;=\; \text{Wilson-line zero mode along}\ \gamma.\;}$$

*This means: the four-dimensional Higgs field $H(x)$ is the four-dimensional remnant of the higher-dimensional gauge field's holonomy along the cycle $\gamma$ — specifically the constant (zero-mode) part along the cycle. There is no separately inserted scalar field; the Higgs emerges from the gauge sector by Kaluza–Klein reduction.*

*This result is consumed by Gate 8 (Higgs origin — the Higgs is gauge-derived, not separately inserted) and by Gate 9 (Yukawa structure — the Higgs that appears in $\bar Q_L H d_R$ Yukawas is this Wilson-line mode).*

### C9.8.8 One-loop Hosotani potential — finite, periodic, cutoff-independent

*The purpose of the next formula is to give the finite one-loop effective potential that pins the Hosotani-phase minimum.*

$$\boxed{\;V_{\rm Hos}(\theta_H) \;=\; -\,\frac{3}{64\pi^6 R_\gamma^4}\,\sum_{n=1}^\infty \frac{1}{n^5}\,\big[\,N_b\cos(n\,\theta_H)\;-\;N_f\cos(n\,\theta_H)\,\big].\;}$$

*This means: $V_{\rm Hos}(\theta_H)$ is the sum of one-loop contributions from Kaluza–Klein modes propagating on $K_{\rm gauge}$, with $N_b$ counting bosonic mode multiplicities and $N_f$ counting fermionic ones, summed over winding number $n$ along the cycle. The sum is finite because $\sum 1/n^5 = \zeta(5) \approx 1.0369$ converges absolutely. The potential is periodic in $\theta_H$ with period $2\pi$ by inspection. It is cutoff-independent because the $\theta_H$-independent ultraviolet divergences cancel exactly in the doublet projection; the $\theta_H$-dependent part needs no regulator.*

*This result is consumed by C9.8.9 (Higgs mass from second derivative) and by Gate 8 (Higgs protection — cutoff independence is the structural protection statement).*

### C9.8.9 Higgs mass from second derivative

*The purpose of the next formula is to give the Higgs mass as the curvature of the Hosotani potential at the minimum.*

$$\boxed{\;m_h^2 \;=\; \left.\frac{d^2 V_{\rm Hos}}{d\theta_H^2}\right|_{\theta_H^\star} \cdot \frac{1}{(2\pi R_\gamma)^2}.\;}$$

*This means: $m_h^2$ is the second derivative of the Hosotani potential evaluated at its frozen minimum $\theta_H^\star$, rescaled by the inverse of the geometric scale squared $(2\pi R_\gamma)^2$. Numerically, the chamber-frozen output is $m_h = 123.82 \pm 1.8$ GeV ([Appendix H](#appendix-h-higgs-protection).4), sitting at $0.48\sigma_{\rm th}$ from the PDG value $125.10 \pm 0.14$ GeV. The mass is a structural output of the chamber-determined potential, not a tuned counterterm.*

*This result is consumed by Gate 8 (Higgs protection — $m_h$ output landing at electroweak scale).*

### C9.8.10 Protection-by-topology — explicit statement

*The purpose of the next statement is to make the topological-protection claim mathematically explicit.*

> **Protection-by-topology claim.** Let $A$ be a gauge connection on $K_{\rm gauge}$ with Wilson-line winding $n_H = (1/2\pi i)\oint_\gamma A$. Any continuous one-parameter family $A_s$ of gauge connections, $s \in [0, 1]$, with $A_0 = A$, satisfies $n_H(A_s) = n_H(A)$ for all $s$ — i.e., the integer winding is invariant under continuous deformation of $A$. Consequently, any putative high-scale correction to the effective Higgs mass that would arise from a continuous deformation of $A$ leaves the integer $n_H$ unchanged; corrections that would require $n_H$ to change (i.e., to leave $\mathbb{Z}$) are not realisable.

*Equivalently: the Higgs mass squared can be expressed schematically as $m_h^2(n_H, \theta_H^\star, R_\gamma, \text{chamber data})$; the $n_H$-dependence is locked to the discrete topological sector, and the $\theta_H^\star$ and chamber-data dependences are locked to the chamber-frozen Hosotani dynamics. There is no smooth function of high-scale parameters that, holding the topological sector fixed, drives $m_h$ to $M_U$.*

*This result is consumed by Gate 8 (Higgs protection — the structural form of the hierarchy resolution).*

### C9.8.11 Structural identity (over-determination check)

*The purpose of the next formula is to record the closed-form expression for the chamber determinant $\eta_{BK}$ and its structural connection to the top/bottom Yukawa ratio.*

$$\boxed{\;\frac{1}{\eta_{BK}} \;=\; e^{2\beta_1^\star} \;=\; 32\pi \cdot \exp\!\left(\,+\frac{\sqrt{3}}{24\pi}\,\right) \;\approx\; 102.87 \;=\; \big|y_t/y_b\big|\;\text{(raw determinant, before } K_{tb}/R_{tb}\text{ running to } M_Z\text{)},\;}$$

with $\eta_{BK} = 0.009721281516312$ (R1.6 hash `84e94518d3f5`).

*This means: $\eta_{BK}$ is a chamber-frozen finite constant of the $F^+$ flavor chamber (C5); its inverse is a closed-form expression in $\pi$ and $\sqrt{3}$. Numerical check: $32\pi \approx 100.531$; $\exp(+\sqrt{3}/(24\pi)) \approx 1.02334$; product $\approx 102.87$. This closed-form value is the *raw determinant* $|y_t/y_b|$ before the $K_{tb}^{\rm crit}/R_{tb}$ running factors are applied; once those factors run it to $M_Z$ it yields the certified between-sector value $|y_t/y_b|(M_Z) \approx 58$ (App J, pull $0.10$). The chamber determinant therefore appears in two independent Standard Model outputs: (a) the Hosotani potential's normalisation that pins $\theta_H^\star$ and hence $v_{\rm EW}$, and (b) the structural identity for $|y_t/y_b|$ (raw determinant; running to $M_Z$ in App J).*

*This result is consumed by Gate 8 (over-determination test) and by Gate 9 (between-sector Yukawa ratio, via [Appendix J](#appendix-j-quark-certificate)).*

### C9.8.12 What is explicitly not solved by this construction

*The purpose of the next list is to spell out, in formula-ladder discipline, which problems the Wilson-line Higgs bundle does and does not address.*

| Problem | Addressed by C9? |
|---|---|
| Higgs mass remains at the electroweak scale rather than running to the unification scale under continuous high-scale corrections | **Yes** — topological protection by integer winding |
| Standard Model Higgs doublet exists with quantum numbers $(\mathbf{1}, \mathbf{2}, +\tfrac{1}{2})$ | **Yes** — bundle factorisation |
| Electroweak symmetry is broken to $U(1)_{\rm em}$ at $v_{\rm EW} \approx 246$ GeV | **Yes** — Hosotani phase minimum |
| Higgs mass predicted *from first principles* with no chamber input | **No** — $m_h$ still depends on chamber data (the Wilson-line modulus $\theta_H^\star$ depends on $\eta_{BK}$) |
| Full naturalness of every Standard Model parameter | **No** — only the specific Higgs-mass-vs-Planck-scale hierarchy is addressed |
| Dark sector / hidden Higgs / axion-like content | **No** — these are excluded by Section 7 / Gate 11 |
| Cosmological constant problem | **No** — excluded from manuscript scope |
| Why the electroweak symmetry-breaking pattern is $SU(2)_L \times U(1)_Y \to U(1)_{\rm em}$ in detail (rather than only $v \neq 0$) | **Partial** — pattern follows from doublet routing in C9.8.5 plus the charge table of [Appendix D](#appendix-d-standard-model-recovery); full statement is at Gate 7 |

*This result is consumed by Gate 11 (claim boundary) and by Section 7 (what-is-not-claimed); it is the anti-overclaim ladder that prevents C9 from being read as a solution to problems it does not address.*

---

## C9.9 Output Produced

C9 produces, as outputs of the construction:

1. A Standard Model Higgs doublet $H \in (\mathbf{1}, \mathbf{2}, +\tfrac{1}{2})$ of $G_{\rm SM}$, realised as the Wilson-line zero mode of the gauge connection along the cycle $\gamma$.
2. An integer winding number $n_H = 1$ giving topological protection of the Higgs mass against continuous high-scale corrections.
3. An electroweak vacuum expectation value $v_{\rm pred} = 246.02 \pm 3.5$ GeV from $v_{\rm EW} = \theta_H^\star / (2\pi R_\gamma)$, matching the PDG value $246.22$ GeV at $0.06\sigma_{\rm th}$.
4. A Higgs boson mass $m_h = 123.82 \pm 1.8$ GeV from the second derivative of the Hosotani potential at $\theta_H^\star$, matching the PDG value $125.10 \pm 0.14$ GeV at $0.48\sigma_{\rm th}$.
5. The structural identity $1/\eta_{BK} = 32\pi\exp(+\sqrt{3}/(24\pi)) \approx 102.87 = |y_t/y_b|$ (raw determinant, before $K_{tb}/R_{tb}$ running to $M_Z$; certified $M_Z$ value $\approx 58$, App J) — one chamber input, two Standard Model outputs.
6. A contributory entry in the heat-kernel threshold ledger (G.3.2 column-2 sum) from the cycle's KK content inside $K_{\rm gauge}$.

The outputs in items 3, 4, and 5 are post-freeze comparisons: the chamber determinant $\eta_{BK}$ is given by a closed-form mathematical expression with no free fit parameter, and the resulting $v$, $m_h$, and $|y_t/y_b|$ are then compared against PDG values without further adjustment.

---

| Gate 7 — Electroweak structure (supportive) | $\theta_H^\star$-driven breaking $SU(2)_L \times U(1)_Y \to U(1)_{\rm em}$; doublet VEV aligned with the charge table of [Appendix D](#appendix-d-standard-model-recovery); no extra Higgs multiplet; $T_3 + Y$ pattern on the broken vacuum | The Higgs VEV breaks the electroweak group in the standard pattern | **No** (requires [Appendix D](#appendix-d-standard-model-recovery) for the charge table + [Appendix G](#appendix-g-threshold-unification) for thresholds) |
| Gate 6 — Threshold unification (contributory) | The cycle $\gamma$ sits inside $K_{\rm gauge}$; its KK content contributes to the threshold-vector ledger G.3.2 column-2 sum (the $\delta_2$ entry); cycle radius $R_\gamma$ enters through the $K_{\rm gauge}$ KK packet | The Wilson-line cycle adds a small contribution to the threshold ledger | **No** (requires [Appendix G](#appendix-g-threshold-unification) for the full threshold computation) |
| Gate 9 — Flavor closure (cross-link) | Same chamber determinant $\eta_{BK}$ that pins $\theta_H^\star$ produces the structural identity $1/\eta_{BK} = |y_t/y_b|$ (raw determinant, before $K_{tb}/R_{tb}$ running to $M_Z$) — one chamber input, two Standard Model outputs | The same chamber number that fixes the Higgs VEV also fixes the top/bottom Yukawa ratio | **No** (requires [Appendix J](#appendix-j-quark-certificate) for the lepton/quark Yukawa structure) |
| Gate 11 — Claim boundary | Topological protection is bounded: addresses Higgs-mass-vs-Planck-scale hierarchy only; does not address full naturalness, dark sector, or cosmological constant | C9 covers one specific protection problem, not every naturalness problem | **No** (Section 7 / Gate 11 statement) |

**Required sentence.**

## C9.11 What This Term Does and Does Not Prove

**C9 proves or records:**

- the Standard Model Higgs doublet $H \in (\mathbf{1}, \mathbf{2}, +\tfrac{1}{2})$ is realised in the active branch as the Wilson-line zero mode of the gauge connection along the cycle $\gamma \subset K_{\rm gauge}$;
- the integer winding number $n_H = 1$ supplies topological protection of the Higgs mass against continuous high-scale corrections;
- the one-loop Hosotani potential $V_{\rm Hos}(\theta_H)$ is finite, periodic, and cutoff-independent;
- the chamber-frozen minimum $\theta_H^\star$ produces $v_{\rm pred} = 246.02 \pm 3.5$ GeV (matching PDG at $0.06\sigma_{\rm th}$);
- the second derivative at $\theta_H^\star$ produces $m_h = 123.82 \pm 1.8$ GeV (matching PDG at $0.48\sigma_{\rm th}$);
- the structural identity $1/\eta_{BK} = 32\pi\exp(+\sqrt{3}/(24\pi)) = 102.87 = |y_t/y_b|$ (raw determinant, before $K_{tb}/R_{tb}$ running to $M_Z$; certified $M_Z$ value $\approx 58$, App J) ties one chamber input to two Standard Model outputs;
- $\mathcal{E}_{\rm Higgs}$ is Retained in the migration ledger (A3.14, no historical migration).

**C9 explicitly does *not* prove (anti-overclaim ledger):**

- C9 does **not** solve every naturalness problem in physics. The hierarchy addressed is the specific one of Higgs-mass-vs-Planck-scale; other naturalness puzzles (e.g., the strong-CP problem, the cosmological constant, neutrino mass naturalness) are not in scope.
- C9 does **not** predict the Higgs mass from first principles. The mass $m_h$ still depends on the Wilson-line modulus $\theta_H^\star$, which is pinned by chamber data $\eta_{BK}$; that chamber datum is a closed-form expression but is not derived from a deeper geometric input in C9. The achievement is *structural protection* of $m_h$ from high-scale destabilisation, not *first-principles derivation* of the numerical value.
- C9 does **not** explain the full electroweak hierarchy in all its facets. It addresses the radiative-stability part. The detailed value of $v_{\rm EW}$, the exact symmetry-breaking pattern beyond doublet routing, the fermion-mass hierarchy, and the CKM / PMNS mixing are handled by other dossiers (C5 / C7 / [Appendix J](#appendix-j-quark-certificate)).
- C9 does **not** address dark Higgs / hidden scalar / axion-like Wilson-line content. These are boundary-excluded by Section 7 / Gate 11.
- C9 does **not** address the cosmological constant or vacuum-energy problems. These are explicitly outside manuscript scope.

**Required sentence.**

> C9 is load-bearing for one specific protection mechanism, not for every naturalness problem. The hierarchy problem in its standard form (radiative pull of a scalar mass toward an arbitrary high scale) is addressed by the integer-winding rule; problems outside this scope are not.

---

## C9.12 Failure If Removed

| If removed | What becomes undefined or wrong | First gate affected |
|---|---|---|
| Wilson-line origin of $H$ (replaced by a fundamental scalar with free mass) | The Higgs mass receives quadratically divergent radiative corrections $\delta m_H^2 \sim M_U^2 \sim 10^{32}\,\mathrm{GeV}^2$; the observed $125$ GeV becomes a tuned cancellation across $\sim 14$ orders of magnitude; the over-determination test fails | **Gate 8 fails** (hierarchy not structural); over-determination test of §5.8.3 (over-determination standard; §4.9) fails |
| Integer winding set to $n_H = 0$ | Wilson-line operator trivial ($W_\gamma = 1$); Hosotani potential's $\theta_H$-dependent part vanishes; no electroweak symmetry breaking; $v_{\rm EW} = 0$ | **Gate 8 fails outright**; **Gate 7 fails** (no EW breaking); cascade to all SM mass terms |
| Integer winding set to $n_H \geq 2$ | $v_{\rm pred}$ scales by integer factor (e.g., $\approx 492$ GeV for $n_H = 2$); structural identity $1/\eta_{BK} = |y_t/y_b|$ no longer aligns with PDG; the chamber compression of one input into two outputs breaks | **Gate 8 fails** (PDG mismatch on $v$); over-determination broken |
| Wilson-line line bundle $L_\gamma$ removed | Wilson-line phase $\theta_H$ has no domain; the holonomy operator $W_\gamma$ has no bundle to act on | **Gate 8 fails** (Hosotani potential has no domain) |
| Doublet representation factor $V_{SU(2),\,\mathbf{2}}$ dropped | $H$ is no longer in $(\mathbf{1}, \mathbf{2}, +\tfrac{1}{2})$; the SM charge table is broken; no $T_3 + Y$ structure for EW breaking; Yukawa couplings $\bar Q_L H d_R$ have no $H$ field with the right gauge transformation | **Gates 2 / 3 fail** (no SM doublet Higgs); **Gate 9 fails** (Yukawa couplings have no $H$) |
| Hypercharge factor $L_{Y=+1/2}$ dropped | Higgs doublet has $Y = 0$; after EW breaking, the surviving photon is not $T_3 + Y$; charge table broken | **Gate 3 fails** (wrong $H$ hypercharge); **Gate 7 fails** (wrong EW breaking pattern) |
| Chamber determinant $\eta_{BK}$ unpinned | $\theta_H^\star$ becomes a free fit parameter; structural identity $1/\eta_{BK} = |y_t/y_b|$ no longer connects $v$ and the Yukawa ratio; chamber compression of one input into two outputs collapses | **Gate 8 fails on the over-determination leg** |
| Wilson-line cycle $\gamma$ unspecified | Effective potential $V_{\rm Hos}(\theta_H)$ has no domain; integer winding has no cycle to wind on | **Gate 8 fails** (Hosotani potential has no domain) |

**Plain-English conclusion.**

> Removing the Wilson-line origin of the Higgs is like removing the integer-valued anchor of the Higgs mass scale. The theory would no longer have a structural reason for the Higgs to sit at $\sim 125$ GeV; the mass would either need to be tuned by hand against high-scale corrections, or the theory would have no electroweak symmetry breaking at all.

---

## C9.13 Minimality / Alternatives

The Wilson-line bundle with $n_H = 1$ is the unique surviving candidate inside the declared search category R2 that closes Constraints 1 through 7 simultaneously. The minimality argument has four parts.

1. **Among non-fundamental-scalar Higgs constructions in R2**, the Wilson-line / Hosotani mechanism is the canonical construction. Composite Higgs and supersymmetric Higgs are in different categories (outside R2). The Wilson-line construction is the unique R2 candidate for topological protection.
2. **Among Wilson-line constructions with integer winding**: $n_H = 0$ gives no electroweak breaking; $n_H \geq 2$ shifts $v_{\rm pred}$ off the PDG band and breaks the over-determination identity to $|y_t/y_b|$. The active branch is committed to $n_H = 1$ as the *minimum* admissible integer producing observed EW breaking.
3. **Among $n_H = 1$ Wilson-line constructions**, the bundle factorisation must include $V_{SU(2),\,\mathbf{2}}$ and $L_{Y=+1/2}$ to produce the observed $(\mathbf{1}, \mathbf{2}, +\tfrac{1}{2})$ quantum numbers; removing either factor breaks Gates 2 / 3 / 7 / 8 / 9 directly.
4. **Among chamber-determined Hosotani potentials**, the pinning by $\eta_{BK}$ is what produces the over-determination $\eta_{BK} \mapsto (v, |y_t/y_b|)$; any other pinning (separate fit, per-coefficient tuning) violates the §5.8.3 (over-determination standard; §4.9) standard.

The retained construction is therefore the minimal surviving candidate inside R2; the alternatives ledger of C9.5 covers all eight nearby candidates and eliminates each by a named first-failed constraint.

**Anti-Occam-violation check.** No alternative inside R2 closes Gate 8 with the over-determination to $|y_t/y_b|$ at lower complexity than the Wilson-line bundle with $n_H = 1$. Any reviewer who can exhibit such an alternative would invalidate the minimality argument; no such exhibition has been supplied for the submitted manuscript.

---

## C9.14 Layer Routing / Anti-Smuggling Check

| Layer | What belongs to C9 ($\mathcal{E}_{\rm Higgs}$) | What must be routed elsewhere |
|---|---|---|
| $\times$ | none native | The cycle $\gamma \subset K_{\rm gauge}$ as a *base sub-manifold* lives in the $\times$-layer; its locus authority is in C2 / C3 / C4 (the base factors of $K_{\rm gauge}$) and the base radii in R1.2 |
| $\oplus$ | none native | The winding-quantisation rule $n_H \in \mathbb{Z}$, the lower-winding-exclusion rule (active branch committed to $n_H = 1$), the finiteness rule on $V_{\rm Hos}(\theta_H)$, and the chamber determinant $\eta_{BK}$ all belong to $\oplus$ (admissibility rulebook C6; chamber data C5; A1.10 / A1.12 / A1.13) |
| $\otimes$ | Wilson-line line bundle $L_\gamma$ (R1.4 `2a0462b8aab9`); doublet representation factor $V_{SU(2),\,\mathbf{2}}$; hypercharge line bundle $L_{Y=+1/2}$ (R1.4 factor `44516f6400ae`); integer winding number $n_H = 1$ (R1.5 `f65094fd8fd1`) as a topological invariant of $L_\gamma$; Hosotani phase mode space $\mathcal{H}_\gamma$; Wilson-line holonomy operator $W_\gamma$ | nothing — this is the layer C9 is native to |

**Layer classification (final).** $\mathcal{E}_{\rm Higgs}$ is **pure $\otimes$**. The cycle $\gamma$ lives over the compact base ($\times$). The winding-quantisation rule and the lower-winding-exclusion rule are $\oplus$. These cross-layer dependencies are *named* (not smuggled): the bundle authority is R1.4, the cycle authority is R1.5, the admissibility authority is C6 / A1.12.

**Required sentence.**

> C9 provides the *operator-layer bundle and topological invariant*. The *cycle locus*, the *admissibility rules*, and the *chamber data* that the bundle depends on are not part of C9 itself; they are routed to the $\times$-layer dossiers (C2 / C3 / C4 for $K_{\rm gauge}$), the $\oplus$-layer admissibility rulebook (C6), and the flavor chamber (C5).

**Layer-smuggling claim.** Nothing in C9 silently imports content from the $\times$- or $\oplus$-layers. The cycle $\gamma$ is *referenced* by C9 because the Wilson-line bundle lives over it, but its *locus authority* lives in $K_{\rm gauge}$ ($\times$). The winding-quantisation rule is *referenced* by C9 because the winding is the protection mechanism, but its *admissibility authority* lives in C6 ($\oplus$). The chamber determinant $\eta_{BK}$ is *referenced* by C9 because it pins $\theta_H^\star$, but its *chamber-data authority* lives in C5 ($\oplus$). These are *named cross-layer dependencies*, not smuggles.

**Connection to the global B2 result.** Gate 8 illustrates the global three-layer necessity result: the base $K_{\rm gauge}$ ($\times$-layer) supplies the cycle locus but no Higgs doublet; the chamber $F^+$ ($\oplus$-layer) supplies $\eta_{BK}$ but no Wilson-line bundle; the line bundle $L_\gamma$ with integer winding ($\otimes$-layer / topological) supplies the protection. The local instance: Gate 8 cannot be closed by any proper-subset construction; the full layered object $\gamma$ ($\times$) + $L_\gamma$ ($\otimes$) + $n_H \in \mathbb{Z}$ + lower-winding exclusion ($\oplus$) + $\eta_{BK}$ ($\oplus$) is the minimal admissible survivor.

To falsify, the reviewer would have to exhibit a fundamental-scalar prescription where $m_H^2$ sits at $(125\,\mathrm{GeV})^2$ by a *structural* mechanism (not tuning). No such prescription exists inside R2. |
| $n_H = 1$ is a *choice*, not a derivation | The active branch could equally well use $n_H = 0$ or $n_H = 2$ | *Defended.* $n_H = 0$ gives no Wilson-line phase, no $v_{\rm EW}$, no electroweak breaking (Gates 7 / 8 fail); $n_H = 2$ shifts $v_{\rm pred}$ by a factor of $\sim 2$ off PDG (Gate 8 fails); non-integer is topologically inadmissible. $n_H = 1$ is the *minimum* admissible integer (A1.12 + lower-winding exclusion rule). |
| Over-determination claim is circular | $\eta_{BK}$ is fitted to one quantity and then re-used to predict the other | *Defended.* $\eta_{BK}$ is frozen by the closed-form expression $1/\eta_{BK} = 32\pi\exp(+\sqrt{3}/(24\pi))$ — a mathematical identity with no free fit parameter. Both $v$ and $|y_t/y_b|$ are post-freeze comparisons. Two outputs from one closed-form input is over-determination, not circularity. |
| $m_h = 123.82$ GeV is tuned to match PDG | The Higgs mass output is tuned, not computed | *Defended.* $m_h$ is computed from the second derivative of the Hosotani potential with frozen $n_H = 1$, derived $R_\gamma$, and closed-form $\eta_{BK}$. The output sits at $0.48\sigma_{\rm th}$ from PDG. No post-comparison rotation or per-coefficient tuning is performed. ([Appendix H](#appendix-h-higgs-protection).4 + H.6.) |
| Term smuggled across layers | The Wilson-line bundle silently imports $\times$-layer metric dimensions or $\oplus$-layer chamber data | *Defended.* A1.9 lists $D = 13$ with no Wilson-line factor; the cycle $\gamma$ is interior to $K_{\rm gauge}$, not a new propagating direction. A1.10 records $\eta_{BK}$ as $\oplus$-layer chamber data (not a metric quantity). A2.5 routes $L_\gamma$, $V_{SU(2),\,\mathbf{2}}$, $L_{Y=+1/2}$, $n_H$ to the $\otimes$-layer. No smuggle. |
| Boundary-extension challenge | Reviewer extends $\mathcal{E}_{\rm Higgs}$ into a non-GUT sector (dark Higgs, hidden scalar, axion-like Wilson line) | *Defended.* Any such extension is boundary-excluded by Section 7 / Gate 11. C9's certificate-complete status is conditional on no such extension being introduced. A non-GUT extension does not strengthen C9 — it moves the claim outside the manuscript's scope. |
| Term redundant | Another retained term in the active branch supplies the Higgs doublet and topological protection | *Defended.* $K_6$ supplies $SU(3)_c$ isometry only (no doublet); $S^2$ supplies $SU(2)_L$ but no specific Higgs cycle; $S_Y^{\,1}/\mathbb{Z}_2$ supplies $U(1)_Y$ but no doublet; $F^+$ supplies chamber operators but no Wilson-line bundle. The Higgs term is independently required. |

**Specific scope-limit statement (for the row in bold).**

> The Wilson-line construction of C9 addresses the specific problem of radiative destabilisation of a fundamental scalar Higgs mass by integer-winding topological protection. It does **not** claim to solve every naturalness problem. Full naturalness — including the cosmological constant, neutrino-mass naturalness, the strong-CP problem, and dark-sector content — is **beyond GUT scope** per the Section 7 / Gate 11 claim boundary. The defence against the "does Wilson-line protection actually solve the full hierarchy problem?" attack is therefore: **no, and the manuscript never claims it does**; what C9 claims is a specific, bounded, fully-supported subset of the full hierarchy question, and that bounded claim is structurally defended.

A reviewer who supplies any of these attacks demonstrates that C9's necessity claim must be re-examined. None of the attacks has been supplied for the submitted manuscript.

---

## C9.18 Comprehension Questions

After reading C9, a non-specialist reader should be able to answer the following ten questions in plain language.

1. **What is the job of $\mathcal{E}_{\rm Higgs}$?**  $\mathcal{E}_{\rm Higgs}$ is the operator-layer object that supplies the Standard Model Higgs doublet and protects its mass from being driven up to the unification scale by high-scale corrections.
2. **Why isn't the Higgs just a scalar field with a free mass?**  A free scalar mass receives quadratically divergent radiative corrections that would push it to the unification scale; getting the observed $125$ GeV from such a mechanism requires tuning to $\sim 14$ orders of magnitude, which is not a structural prediction.
3. **What is a Wilson line?**  The Wilson line is the phase wound up by the gauge field while traversing a closed loop $\gamma$ inside the internal geometry; mathematically it is $W_\gamma = \mathcal{P}\exp(i \oint_\gamma A)$.
4. **What does the path-ordering symbol $\mathcal{P}$ do?**  $\mathcal{P}$ keeps non-commuting matrix-valued factors of the gauge field $A$ multiplied in the correct order along the path; for the abelian projection relevant to the Higgs winding it trivialises.
5. **What is the winding number $n_H$ and why is it an integer?**  $n_H = (1/2\pi i)\oint_\gamma A$ is the integer count of how many full $2\pi$ phase wraps the Wilson line accumulates around the cycle; it must be an integer for the Wilson-line operator on a closed cycle to be single-valued under gauge transformations.
6. **Why does $n_H = 1$ in the active branch?**  $n_H = 0$ gives no Wilson-line phase and no electroweak breaking; $n_H \geq 2$ shifts the predicted electroweak VEV off the observed value; non-integer is topologically forbidden. So $n_H = 1$ is the minimum admissible integer producing observed breaking.
7. **How does the Higgs $H(x)$ in four dimensions come out of this?**  After Kaluza–Klein reduction of the higher-dimensional gauge connection along the cycle, the surviving zero-mode in four dimensions sits in the representation $(\mathbf{1}, \mathbf{2}, +\tfrac{1}{2})$ — that mode *is* the Standard Model Higgs.
8. **What is topological protection in plain words?**  An integer cannot be changed by a continuous deformation; any radiative correction that tries to alter the Higgs mass through smooth interpolation cannot push the integer winding off $1$ and therefore cannot pull the mass continuously up to the unification scale.
9. **What problem does C9 *not* solve?**  C9 does not solve every naturalness problem. It addresses only the specific Higgs-mass-vs-Planck-scale hierarchy from radiative destabilisation of a fundamental scalar; the cosmological constant, dark sector, neutrino mass naturalness, and full prediction of $m_h$ from first principles are out of scope.
10. **What fails if $\mathcal{E}_{\rm Higgs}$ is removed?**  Gate 8 fails outright (no topological protection), Gate 7 fails (no electroweak breaking), the Yukawa couplings have no Higgs to couple to, and the over-determination identity $1/\eta_{BK} = |y_t/y_b|$ (raw determinant, before $K_{tb}/R_{tb}$ running to $M_Z$) no longer connects the Higgs sector to the Yukawa ratio.

**One-sentence summary.**

> $\mathcal{E}_{\rm Higgs}$ replaces a freely tunable scalar mass with an integer-valued topological invariant, and that invariant is what keeps the Higgs at the electroweak scale.

**Acceptance rule.**

> If a non-specialist reader cannot answer these without rereading advanced notation, this dossier still needs bridge sentences.

---

## C9.19 Closing Status

C9 is complete when the reader can follow this chain end-to-end:

```text
The Standard Model needs a Higgs doublet at 125 GeV
  → a free scalar mass would receive ~M_U^2 corrections (hierarchy problem)
  → the active branch instead realises the Higgs as a Wilson-line holonomy
  → Wilson line W_γ = P exp(i ∮_γ A) wraps a non-contractible cycle γ in K_gauge
  → the winding n_H = (1/2πi) ∮_γ A is an integer (forced by gauge invariance)
  → on the active branch n_H = 1 (minimum admissible integer)
  → the 4D Higgs H(x) is the Wilson-line zero mode after KK reduction
  → the Hosotani potential V_Hos(θ_H) is finite, periodic, cutoff-independent
  → its minimum θ_H^* gives v_EW = θ_H^*/(2πR_γ) = 246.02 GeV (PDG match)
  → its curvature gives m_h = 123.82 GeV (PDG match)
  → the chamber determinant η_BK pins θ_H^* AND satisfies 1/η_BK = |y_t/y_b|
  → integer winding is invariant under continuous deformation of A
  → therefore the Higgs mass is structurally (not tunably) at the EW scale
  → Gates 6, 7, 8, 9, 11 consume these outputs
  → removing E_Higgs breaks the chain at Gate 8 (and cascades)
```

**Status.** *Certificate-complete (term-by-term construction).* Every constraint of C9.3 / C9.4 is named with a source and a frozen-object response. Every alternative of C9.5 is eliminated with a stated first-failed constraint. The Wilson-line construction of C9.7 / C9.8, the integer-winding protection of C9.8.4 / C9.8.10, the chamber-frozen outputs of C9.8.6 / C9.8.9, and the failure-if-removed cascade of C9.12 are all routed to authority pointers in C9.15. The hostile-review certificate of C9.16 is auditable against A0 / A1 / A2 / A3 / [Appendix H](#appendix-h-higgs-protection) / [Appendix J](#appendix-j-quark-certificate) / [Appendix R0](#appendix-r0-freeze-certificates-and-code-hashes). The reviewer attack matrix of C9.17 includes the specific scope-limit statement that Wilson-line protection addresses one bounded aspect of the hierarchy problem, not the full set of naturalness puzzles.

**Gate 8 status: *Claimed certificate pass*.** The Higgs mass on the active branch is the result of a structural Wilson-line protection mechanism, not a tuned scalar counterterm; the resulting $v_{\rm pred} = 246.02$ GeV and $m_h = 123.82$ GeV are post-freeze outputs in agreement with measurement ([Appendix H](#appendix-h-higgs-protection)); the over-determination test of §5.8.3 (over-determination standard; §4.9) is satisfied through the structural identity $1/\eta_{BK} = |y_t/y_b|$ (raw determinant, before $K_{tb}/R_{tb}$ running to $M_Z$) ([Appendix J](#appendix-j-quark-certificate)).

**Failure conditions for C9 itself.** C9 fails if (i) any R1 hash cited in C9.15 does not regenerate from the manifest meta-hash `a5b1e6f9d951`; (ii) any reviewer exhibits an alternative active branch inside R2 that closes Gate 8 with topological protection at lower complexity than the Wilson-line bundle with $n_H = 1$; (iii) the layer-smuggling check of C9.14 is found to silently inherit content across layers; (iv) the reviewer attack matrix of C9.17 is met by an explicit fundamental-scalar prescription with structural (not tuned) $m_H^2$ at the electroweak scale; or (v) any output in C9.9 fails to reproduce from the frozen objects in C9.15 under the reproduction procedure of [Appendix R0](#appendix-r0-freeze-certificates-and-code-hashes).

**Final sentence.**

> $\mathcal{E}_{\rm Higgs} = L_\gamma \otimes V_{SU(2),\,\mathbf{2}} \otimes L_{Y=+1/2}$ with $n_H = 1$ is retained because it is the minimal $\otimes$-layer Wilson-line bundle in the declared active branch whose holonomy supplies the Standard Model Higgs doublet, whose integer winding supplies topological protection of the Higgs mass against continuous high-scale corrections, and whose chamber-frozen output sits at the electroweak scale in agreement with measurement. The general reader gets the story; the formal reviewer gets the proof trail; the scope limit is explicit.
