# Appendix C10 — $\mathcal{E}_{\rm proton}$ Dossier: Proton-Safety Projector / No-Mediator Layer

**Load-bearing role.** Authoritative source for the term-level necessity of $\mathcal{E}_{\rm proton}$ in the active branch per the Claim-to-Appendix Authority Map (front matter). Conflicts with other appendices on whether $\mathcal{E}_{\rm proton}$ is retained or with what failure-if-removed signature are resolved by this dossier.

**Downstream impact (per the Appendix Dependency Graph, front matter).** If this dossier's term-necessity claim is downgraded under the R2 Downgrade Rules — i.e., if a reviewer shows the term is removable or smuggled — every gate certificate that depends on $\mathcal{E}_{\rm proton}$ drops one rung in Section 6, and the dossier's failure-if-removed table (C10.8 or equivalent) records the propagation path. Gates affected: Gate 10 (sector projectors + macro-projector identity); also note: the FCNC / mediator no-go theorem cross-affects Gate 9 (flavor chamber sector orthogonality).

> **How to read this dossier.** Sections C10.0 through C10.13 are the *high-level summary layer*: they explain what $\mathcal{E}_{\rm proton}$ is, what problem it solves, what mathematics it carries, and what fails if it is removed, in ordinary language with the formal symbols introduced gradually. Sections C10.14 through C10.19 are the *formal-review layer*: they translate the summary content into a formal certificate with R1 hashes, gate output maps, layer-routing checks, freeze pointers, and a hostile-review attack matrix. A non-specialist reader should be able to read C10.0–C10.13 cover-to-cover. A skeptical reviewer should be able to audit C10.14–C10.19 against the rest of the manuscript.

---

## C10.0 One-Page Summary

The Standard Model has a problem most GUT candidates accidentally fail. Many candidate unified theories — especially those built by embedding $G_{\rm SM} = SU(3)_c \times SU(2)_L \times U(1)_Y$ into a larger simple group such as $SU(5)$ or $SO(10)$ — accidentally allow particles to connect the quark and lepton sectors through dangerous heavy mediators. Through dimension-six four-fermion operators like $QQQL$, those mediators would cause the proton to decay much faster than current experimental bounds permit.

The experimental wall is hard. Super-Kamiokande has searched for proton decay over decades and has not seen it:

$$\boxed{\;\tau_p(p \to e^+\pi^0) \;>\; 2.4 \times 10^{34}\ \text{yr},\qquad \tau_p(p \to \mu^+K^0) \;>\; 1.6 \times 10^{34}\ \text{yr}.\;}$$

Any unified theory that produces a non-zero dimension-six four-fermion baryon-violating Wilson coefficient at a single grand-unified scale must engineer that coefficient to be tiny by hand. That is fine-tuning of the worst kind: each dangerous Wilson coefficient becomes a separate fit parameter.

$\mathcal{E}_{\rm proton}$ closes the operator-level proton-safety problem differently — *mathematically*, not by tuning. It supplies four objects:

1. four **sector projectors** $\Pi_u, \Pi_d, \Pi_e, \Pi_\nu$ acting on the chamber generation module, labelling each generation by its sector;
2. two **macro-projectors** $\Pi_q = \Pi_u + \Pi_d + \Pi_{Q_L}$ (quark sector) and $\Pi_\ell = \Pi_e + \Pi_\nu + \Pi_{L_L}$ (lepton sector) on the matter bundle;
3. the **no-mediator identity** $\Pi_q\,M\,\Pi_\ell = 0$ for every admissible sector-respecting operator $M$ on the matter bundle;
4. a **BRST decoupling** statement for spectator gauge-redundant components plus a **KK-number conservation** rule for physical KK modes.

The no-mediator identity is not an axiom. It is a one-line consequence of the orthogonality $\Pi_i\Pi_j = \delta_{ij}\Pi_i$ of the sector projectors. Every dangerous Wilson coefficient on the active branch — $C_{QQQL}$, $C_{u_R^c u_R^c d_R^c e_R^c}$, $C_{QLu_R^c d_R^c}$, $C_{QQu_R^c e_R^c}$, and their relatives up to mass dimension 7 — vanishes by this identity:

$$\boxed{\;C_{QQQL} \;=\; C_{u_R^c u_R^c d_R^c e_R^c} \;=\; C_{QLu_R^c d_R^c} \;=\; C_{QQu_R^c e_R^c} \;=\; \cdots \;=\; 0.\;}$$

This is an *exact algebraic identity*, not a numerical bound. There is no tuning per operator; the coefficient is zero because no admissible operator $M$ on the matter bundle can connect the quark sector to the lepton sector.

**In one sentence:**

> $\mathcal{E}_{\rm proton}$ is the operator-domain layer that mathematically forbids cross-sector quark-to-lepton mediators through the algebraic identity $\Pi_q M \Pi_\ell = 0$, so every dangerous four-fermion baryon-violating Wilson coefficient vanishes identically by sector orthogonality — not by tuning.

**Important distinction.** $\mathcal{E}_{\rm proton}$ closes the operator-level question: no dangerous mediators exist on the active branch. It does **not** predict an exact numerical proton lifetime. The lifetime is reported separately in [Appendix L](#appendix-l-proton-safety) as a *Diagnostic only* number — consistent with experimental bounds but not used as a hard claim. The two roles are kept distinct: *Claimed certificate pass* at the operator level (Wilson coefficients identically zero), *Diagnostic only* at the lifetime level.

---

## C10.1 The Problem This Term Solves

C10 answers one question:

> Why does the active branch not predict rapid proton decay, even though it has the Standard Model gauge group?

The answer cannot be "global baryon-number conservation." That answer is wrong on its face for the Standard Model — the SM has electroweak sphaleron processes that change $B$ and $L$ by $\pm 3$ each while conserving $B - L$. A continuous global $U(1)_B$ symmetry would forbid sphalerons. So global $B$ symmetry is structurally incompatible with the SM.

The answer also cannot be "the heavy mediator $X/Y$ is so heavy that the Wilson coefficient $\sim 1/M_{X/Y}^2$ is small enough." In standard simple-group GUTs like $SU(5)$, the dangerous coefficients are *suppressed* but *not zero*. They are tuned, and each operator becomes a separate fit parameter.

The active branch chooses neither route. It chooses an *operator-algebra* mechanism:

1. The compact gauge geometry $K_{\rm gauge} = K_6 \times S^2 \times S_Y^{\,1}/\mathbb{Z}_2$ is a *product factor*, not a simple-group quotient. There is no $X/Y$ off-diagonal generator connecting quarks to leptons.
2. The chamber's sector projectors $\Pi_u, \Pi_d, \Pi_e, \Pi_\nu$ label each generation by its sector. They are mutually orthogonal: $\Pi_i\Pi_j = \delta_{ij}\Pi_i$.
3. The macro-projectors $\Pi_q$ and $\Pi_\ell$ aggregate the sector projectors into quark and lepton macro-sectors on the matter bundle. They satisfy macro-orthogonality $\Pi_q\Pi_\ell = 0$.
4. The no-mediator identity $\Pi_q M \Pi_\ell = 0$ follows for every admissible sector-respecting operator $M$. This kills every dangerous Wilson coefficient identically.
5. Spectator KK gauge modes are decoupled by two independent mechanisms: BRST decoupling on the gauge-redundant components (Channel A) and KK-number conservation plus projector orthogonality on the physical KK modes (Channel B).

The job of $\mathcal{E}_{\rm proton}$ is to package the four objects of items 2–4 (sector projectors, macro-projectors, no-mediator identity) and the BRST/KK-number decoupling of item 5 into a single operator-domain layer of the active branch. Item 1 (the product-factor structure of $K_{\rm gauge}$) is sourced by C2 / C3 / C4 in the $\times$-layer.

**Summary.** $\mathcal{E}_{\rm proton}$ is the *dangerous-operator blocker*: the operator-domain layer that mathematically forbids the cross-sector mediators that would otherwise cause rapid proton decay.

---

## C10.2 Vocabulary Before Symbols

C10 will use a number of words that have everyday counterparts and technical ones. The table is the reader's translator:

| Word / symbol | Plain-English meaning | Technical role in C10 |
|---|---|---|
| Proton decay | Decay of the proton into lighter particles (e.g., $p \to e^+\pi^0$) | The dangerous process the active branch must avoid above the Super-K bound |
| Wilson coefficient | Numerical pre-factor of a four-fermion operator in the effective Lagrangian | What the no-mediator identity sets to zero |
| Mediator | A heavy intermediate particle that allows two sectors to "talk" via a propagator | The object whose absence on $K_{\rm gauge}$ + matter-bundle factorisation is required |
| Operator $M$ | A linear map from the matter bundle to itself | The candidate sector-respecting object that the projectors annihilate when read cross-sector |
| Sector | One of the four matter species: up quark ($u$), down quark ($d$), charged lepton ($e$), neutrino ($\nu$) | The four-fold decomposition the projectors enforce |
| Projector $\Pi$ | A linear map satisfying $\Pi^2 = \Pi$ that picks out a subspace | The mathematical handle on "the part of the matter bundle in a given sector" |
| $\Pi_u, \Pi_d, \Pi_e, \Pi_\nu$ | Sector projectors onto up-quark, down-quark, charged-lepton, neutrino sectors | The four primitives that decompose $\mathcal{G}_{\rm gen}$ |
| $\Pi_q, \Pi_\ell$ | Macro-projectors onto the quark and lepton sectors of the matter bundle | The two aggregates that state the no-mediator identity |
| $\Pi_{Q_L}, \Pi_{L_L}$ | Doublet-component projectors from the matter-bundle factorisation | The pieces added to the sector sum to cover left-handed doublet content |
| Generation module $\mathcal{G}_{\rm gen}$ | The three-dimensional chamber space on which the sector projectors act | The domain of the sector projectors |
| Matter bundle $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ | The full bundle of matter-field sections, including gauge and chirality content | The domain of the macro-projectors and the no-mediator identity |
| No-mediator identity | The algebraic statement $\Pi_q M \Pi_\ell = 0$ for every admissible $M$ | The load-bearing equation of C10 |
| Sector-respecting $M$ | A linear map that commutes with the sector decomposition of the matter bundle | The class of operators on which the no-mediator identity applies |
| Dangerous operator | A four-fermion (or higher) operator that, if non-zero, would violate observed baryon- or lepton-number bounds | The set $\{QQQL, u_R^c u_R^c d_R^c e_R^c, QLu_R^c d_R^c, QQu_R^c e_R^c, \ldots\}$ |
| $QQQL$ | The four-fermion operator $\epsilon^{ijk}\epsilon_{\alpha\beta}\epsilon_{\gamma\delta}(Q_i^\alpha Q_j^\beta)(Q_k^\gamma L^\delta)$ | The archetypal proton-decay operator |
| BRST operator $Q_{\rm BRST}$ | A nilpotent operator on the off-shell gauge-fixed Hilbert space whose cohomology defines physical states | The tool that decouples gauge-redundant components |
| Gauge-redundant component | An unphysical polarization (longitudinal $A_\mu$ or scalar $A_5$) that the gauge fixing introduces | The class of components killed by BRST exactness |
| Physical KK mode | A genuine massive Kaluza–Klein excitation along the compact direction with mass $m_n \sim n/R$ | The class of states killed by KK-number conservation, not by BRST |
| KK-number conservation | The selection rule $\sum_{\rm vertex} n = 0$ from compact-factor isometry | The mechanism that forbids tree-level physical KK exchange when external legs are SM zero modes |
| Sphaleron | An SM electroweak process that changes $B$ and $L$ by $\pm 3$ each while conserving $B - L$ | The reason a global $U(1)_B$ symmetry cannot be used for proton safety |

**Rule.** No technical word in this dossier may be used in a load-bearing sentence without first appearing in this table.

**Useful footnote.** "Sector-respecting" is the key adjective. An operator $M$ is sector-respecting if and only if it can be written $M = \sum_i \Pi_i M_i \Pi_i$ in the sector basis — i.e., it acts independently on each sector subspace. Every dangerous SM operator ($QQQL$, $u_R^c u_R^c d_R^c e_R^c$, etc.) is built from quark fields and lepton fields that the matter-bundle factorisation already distinguishes; the chamber operators $O_u, O_d, O_e, O_\nu$ each act only on their own sector subspace; KK-loop contributions preserve the gauge decomposition. So the no-mediator identity applies to *every* dangerous operator by construction.

---

## C10.3 The Constraint in Plain English

Proton safety on the active branch is governed by eight experimental and structural constraints:

1. The proton lifetime is bounded below by Super-K: $\tau_p > 2.4 \times 10^{34}$ yr for $p \to e^+\pi^0$, $\tau_p > 1.6 \times 10^{34}$ yr for $p \to \mu^+K^0$. So no dim-6 baryon-violating operator with unsuppressed Wilson coefficient is permitted.
2. The neutron–antineutron oscillation rate is bounded: $\tau_{n\bar n} > 2.7 \times 10^{8}$ s. So $\Delta B = 2$ operators must be absent or strongly suppressed.
3. Flavor-violating four-fermion operators are bounded by precision LFV searches ($\mu \to e\gamma$, $\mu N \to eN$). So cross-sector four-fermion operators must vanish at the operator level.
4. SM electroweak sphalerons are part of the SM. So the safety mechanism must be compatible with sphalerons — a global $U(1)_B$ symmetry, which would forbid them, is excluded.
5. The mechanism must be auditable from the frozen geometric data, not a per-operator suppression factor. Per-operator fine-tuning is forbidden.
6. Tree-level FCNC must vanish. Observed $K^0$–$\bar K^0$ and $B$–$\bar B$ mixing match SM-only predictions; chamber-mediated cross-sector amplitudes must therefore vanish identically.
7. The SM gauge group is not recovered as the surviving subgroup of a simple group containing it (no $SU(5)$, $SO(10)$, or $E_6$ embedding). So $K_{\rm gauge}$ must be a *product* of compact factors with isometry $SU(3) \oplus SU(2) \oplus U(1)$, and no off-diagonal generator exists.
8. The operator-level closure is required; numerical lifetime is *Diagnostic* only. The dossier must keep these two roles distinct.

**Plain-English constraint.**

> Find an operator-domain layer that mathematically forbids cross-sector quark-to-lepton mediators — through projector orthogonality, BRST decoupling, and KK-number conservation — making every dangerous Wilson coefficient identically zero, while remaining compatible with SM sphalerons.

This constraint is the operative one for C10; the alternatives in C10.5 are evaluated against it.

---

## C10.4 Formal Constraint Statement

The plain-English constraint of C10.3 sharpens into a formal constraint as follows.

**Formal constraint (Operator-Algebra Proton-Safety Layer).**

> The active branch must include an operator-domain object $\mathcal{E}_{\rm proton}$ consisting of:
>
> (a) four mutually orthogonal sector projectors $\Pi_u, \Pi_d, \Pi_e, \Pi_\nu \in \mathrm{End}(\mathcal{G}_{\rm gen})$ satisfying $\Pi_i^2 = \Pi_i$, $\Pi_i\Pi_j = \delta_{ij}\Pi_i$, and $\sum_i \Pi_i = \mathbb{1}_{\mathcal{G}_{\rm gen}}$;
>
> (b) two macro-projectors $\Pi_q = \Pi_u + \Pi_d + \Pi_{Q_L}$ and $\Pi_\ell = \Pi_e + \Pi_\nu + \Pi_{L_L}$ on the matter bundle $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ with macro-orthogonality $\Pi_q \Pi_\ell = 0$;
>
> (c) the no-mediator identity
> $$\boxed{\;\Pi_q\,M\,\Pi_\ell \;=\; 0\;}$$
> for every admissible sector-respecting operator $M$ on the active branch;
>
> (d) a BRST operator $Q_{\rm BRST}$ on the off-shell gauge-fixed Hilbert space with $Q_{\rm BRST}^2 = 0$, plus the statement that gauge-redundant components of spectator KK gauge modes are BRST-exact;
>
> (e) the KK-number conservation rule $\sum_{\rm vertex} n = 0$ on every Feynman-diagram vertex involving compact-direction modes.
>
> Together, (a)–(e) must imply that every dangerous Wilson coefficient ($C_{QQQL}$, $C_{u_R^c u_R^c d_R^c e_R^c}$, $C_{QLu_R^c d_R^c}$, $C_{QQu_R^c e_R^c}$, and their dim-7 relatives) vanishes identically. The mechanism must be compatible with SM electroweak sphalerons (no global $U(1)_B$ invoked).

This is the constraint that $\mathcal{E}_{\rm proton}$ is selected to satisfy. The candidate alternatives of C10.5 are evaluated against this constraint.

---

## C10.5 Candidate Choices

| Choice | Plain-English version | Why it fails or passes |
|---|---|---|
| Do nothing | No operator-layer object; rely on heavy mediators being absent from $K_{\rm gauge}$ alone | **Fails.** The product-factor structure of $K_{\rm gauge}$ kills tree-level $X/Y$ mediators (Ingredient 1) but does not, by itself, kill chamber-induced cross-sector compositions (Ingredient 3) or spectator KK gauge modes (Ingredient 2). Without the sector projectors and the no-mediator identity, residual cross-sector amplitudes remain. |
| Global $U(1)_B$ symmetry | Postulate a global baryon-number symmetry that forbids $\Delta B \neq 0$ | **Fails.** Forbids SM electroweak sphalerons (which change $B$ and $L$ by $\pm 3$ each); structurally incompatible with the electroweak SM. Constraint 4 fails. |
| Per-operator Wilson-coefficient tuning | Set each dangerous $C_i$ to a small value by hand | **Fails.** Violates over-determination; each operator becomes a separate fit parameter; freeze-before-compare is broken. Constraint 5 fails. |
| Heavy $X/Y$ mediator ($SU(5)$-style) | Embed $G_{\rm SM}$ in $SU(5)$; raise $M_{X/Y}$ until lifetime exceeds Super-K bound | **Fails.** The mediator is structurally present; Wilson coefficients are only $\sim 1/M_{X/Y}^2$ suppressed, not zero. Requires $K_{\rm gauge}$ to be a simple-group quotient, not a product. Constraint 7 fails. |
| Weaker projector identity ($\Pi_q M \Pi_\ell$ small but non-zero) | Approximate sector orthogonality | **Fails.** Leaves dangerous mediator residues; proton-decay channels reopen at finite coefficient. The identity collapses to a numerical bound. |
| R-parity-like discrete symmetry | Borrow $\mathbb{Z}_2$ R-parity from SUSY models | **Fails.** R-parity is a SUSY-motivated discrete symmetry; not in the declared search category. Adds a separate discrete posit not derivable from the active geometry. |
| Sector projectors only, no macro-projectors | Per-sector orthogonality alone | **Fails.** Without the macro-projectors $\Pi_q, \Pi_\ell$, the no-mediator identity $\Pi_q M \Pi_\ell = 0$ has no domain on the matter bundle. The per-sector orthogonality is necessary but not sufficient. |
| Macro-projectors but no BRST decoupling | Operator-algebra closure only | **Fails.** Spectator gauge-redundant components (longitudinal $A_\mu$, Hosotani $A_5$) can mediate $\Delta B = 1$ via gauge-fixing artefacts. Channel A is missing. |
| Macro-projectors and BRST but no KK-number conservation | BRST closure without Channel B | **Fails.** Physical (transverse) KK modes are not BRST-exact; without KK-number conservation, they can appear on tree-level proton-decay amplitudes. Channel B collapses. |
| $\boxed{\mathcal{E}_{\rm proton} = \{\Pi_u, \Pi_d, \Pi_e, \Pi_\nu,\;\Pi_q, \Pi_\ell,\;Q_{\rm BRST},\;\Pi_q M \Pi_\ell = 0,\;\text{empty cross-sector cohomology}\}}$ | The full layered operator-domain object | **Passes.** Sector projectors decompose $\mathcal{G}_{\rm gen}$; macro-projectors lift to $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$; no-mediator identity kills cross-sector amplitudes; BRST kills gauge-redundant spectators; KK-number kills physical KK at tree; empty cohomology kills chamber compositions. All eight constraints satisfied; no global $B$ symmetry invoked. |

The selected object is the *full layered operator-domain object* listed in the last row. Removing any one component (sector projectors, macro-projectors, no-mediator identity, BRST, KK-number, empty cohomology) opens the gate.

**Summary.** The goal is not just any safety mechanism. The mechanism has to forbid cross-sector mediators *mathematically* — without invoking a global symmetry that would forbid sphalerons, and without per-operator tuning that would break freeze-before-compare.

---

## C10.6 Chosen Object

The selected operator-domain object is

$$\boxed{\;\mathcal{E}_{\rm proton} \;=\; \{\,\Pi_u, \Pi_d, \Pi_e, \Pi_\nu,\; \Pi_q, \Pi_\ell,\; Q_{\rm BRST},\; \text{no-mediator identity}\;\Pi_q M \Pi_\ell = 0,\; \mathcal{H}_{\rm cross\text{-}sector} = \{0\}\,\}.\;}$$

Concretely the operator-domain object consists of:

- **Sector projectors** $\Pi_u, \Pi_d, \Pi_e, \Pi_\nu \in \mathrm{End}(\mathcal{G}_{\rm gen})$, four rank-3 self-adjoint operators on the chamber generation module $\mathcal{G}_{\rm gen} = \mathrm{span}\{g_1, g_2, g_3\}$. Frozen by R1.4 hash `3b8d68559f5e`.
- **Macro-projectors** $\Pi_q = \Pi_u + \Pi_d + \Pi_{Q_L}$ and $\Pi_\ell = \Pi_e + \Pi_\nu + \Pi_{L_L}$ on the matter bundle $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$, with $\Pi_{Q_L}, \Pi_{L_L}$ the doublet-component projectors from the matter-bundle factorisation of A2.3.
- **BRST operator** $Q_{\rm BRST}$ on the off-shell gauge-fixed Hilbert space, the standard Slavnov–Taylor operator satisfying $Q_{\rm BRST}^2 = 0$.
- **No-mediator identity** $\Pi_q M \Pi_\ell = 0$ for every admissible sector-respecting operator $M$ on the matter bundle. Frozen as the operator-class entry of R1.6 hash `551488d06011`; the full FCNC / mediator no-go theorem hash is `fff4b433b7b3` (R1.6).
- **Empty cross-sector cohomology** $\mathcal{H}_{\rm cross\text{-}sector} = \{0\}$, the statement that $\Pi_i \mathcal{G}_{\rm gen} \cap \Pi_j \mathcal{G}_{\rm gen} = \{0\}$ for $i \neq j$, which follows directly from per-sector orthogonality.

The chosen object is *unique* inside the declared search category that closes Gate 10 at the operator level. Every alternative listed in C10.5 fails by a named first-failed constraint (Constraint 4 for global $B$ symmetry; Constraint 5 for per-operator tuning; Constraint 7 for $SU(5)$-style embedding; Constraint 1 for weaker projector identity; outside-R2 for R-parity).

**No new primitive introduced.** The sector projectors are frozen by R1.4 hash `3b8d68559f5e` (already counted). The FCNC / mediator no-go theorem is R1.6 hash `fff4b433b7b3` with operator-class hash `551488d06011` (already counted). The macro-projectors are *derived* from the sector projectors plus the matter-bundle doublet components $\Pi_{Q_L}, \Pi_{L_L}$. The BRST operator is the standard Slavnov–Taylor operator. The no-mediator identity is a *theorem* with a one-line proof (C10.8.3). The empty cohomology statement is *derived* from per-sector orthogonality. C10 introduces no new R1 row; the manifest meta-hash `a5b1e6f9d951` is unchanged.

---

## C10.7 Construction Explained Without Jargon

The construction has four conceptual steps. Each step is a sentence first, then a formula.

**Step 1: Build labels for the sectors.** Start with the chamber generation module $\mathcal{G}_{\rm gen}$, a three-dimensional space carrying three particle generations. The active branch has four matter species: up quark ($u$), down quark ($d$), charged lepton ($e$), neutrino ($\nu$). Construct four projection operators, one for each species; each picks out the part of $\mathcal{G}_{\rm gen}$ belonging to its species.

**Step 2: Make the labels mutually exclusive.** Demand that the four projectors are mutually orthogonal and that they sum to the identity. Mutually orthogonal means: no point of $\mathcal{G}_{\rm gen}$ is in two sectors at once. Summing to the identity means: every point is in exactly one sector. This is the *sector decomposition* of $\mathcal{G}_{\rm gen}$.

**Step 3: Build macro-labels for "quark" and "lepton."** Aggregate the species labels: a state is "quark" if it is up-quark or down-quark, "lepton" if it is charged-lepton or neutrino. Add the doublet-component projectors $\Pi_{Q_L}, \Pi_{L_L}$ from the matter-bundle structure to cover the left-handed doublets. This gives two macro-projectors $\Pi_q$ and $\Pi_\ell$ on the matter bundle. The macros are mutually orthogonal: $\Pi_q \Pi_\ell = 0$, because all the cross-products $\Pi_u\Pi_e = \Pi_u\Pi_\nu = \Pi_d\Pi_e = \cdots = 0$ vanish by Step 2.

**Step 4: State the no-mediator identity.** For any operator $M$ on the matter bundle that respects the sector decomposition — i.e., any $M$ of the form $M = \sum_i \Pi_i M_i \Pi_i$, where $M_i$ acts on the $i$-th sector subspace — the cross-sector composition $\Pi_q M \Pi_\ell$ vanishes identically. This is because $\Pi_q$ kills the lepton-sector projectors and $\Pi_\ell$ kills the quark-sector projectors, so the cross-product is zero on every $\Pi_i M_i \Pi_i$ piece.

This is the load-bearing identity:

$$\boxed{\;\Pi_q\,M\,\Pi_\ell \;=\; 0\quad\text{for every sector-respecting } M.\;}$$

The four steps are joined by two additional rules that close the residual spectator-mode question:

**Step 5: BRST decoupling on the gauge-redundant components.** The gauge fixing of the higher-dimensional gauge theory introduces unphysical longitudinal and scalar modes ($A_\mu^a$ longitudinal, $A_5^a$ scalar). These are killed by the standard Slavnov–Taylor identity: they are BRST-exact, so their matrix elements between physical states vanish.

**Step 6: KK-number conservation on the physical KK modes.** The transverse physical polarizations of a KK gauge mode with $n \geq 1$ are not BRST-exact — they are genuine massive states. They are nevertheless killed at tree level by KK-number conservation: every Feynman-diagram vertex conserves the KK quantum number on each compact factor, but SM external states carry $n_{\rm ext} = 0$ on every factor. So any internal physical KK mediator must appear in a closed loop, not on a tree-level propagator. At loop level, the no-mediator identity of Step 4 kills the loop integrand by sector orthogonality.

The combined effect of Steps 1–6 is the **dangerous-operator blocker**: every dangerous Wilson coefficient ($C_{QQQL}, C_{u_R^c u_R^c d_R^c e_R^c}, C_{QLu_R^c d_R^c}, C_{QQu_R^c e_R^c}, \ldots$) vanishes identically.

**Analogy.** Imagine the matter bundle as a building with two wings. The quark wing has rooms for up-quarks, down-quarks, and left-handed quark doublets. The lepton wing has rooms for charged leptons, neutrinos, and left-handed lepton doublets. The two wings have *no shared corridors*. An admissible operator is anything that moves you between rooms inside the same wing, but no admissible operator moves you across the wings. The proton-decay process — three quarks turning into a lepton plus a meson — requires a cross-wing operator. The building, by construction, has none.

---

## C10.8 Formula-by-Formula Math Ladder

This subsection records the explicit mathematics underlying $\mathcal{E}_{\rm proton}$ — the algebraic identities a reader must reconstruct to verify Gate 10 at the operator level. Each formula is wrapped in 4 parts: *purpose, formula, meaning, consumer*.

---

### C10.8.1 Sector projectors on $\mathcal{G}_{\rm gen}$

*The purpose of the next formula is to define the four sector projectors as self-adjoint rank-3 operators on the chamber generation module.*

$$\boxed{\;\Pi_u, \Pi_d, \Pi_e, \Pi_\nu \,\in\, \mathrm{End}(\mathcal{G}_{\rm gen}), \qquad \Pi_i^\dagger = \Pi_i, \qquad \Pi_i^2 = \Pi_i.\;}$$

*This means: each $\Pi_i$ is a self-adjoint projector ($\Pi_i^\dagger = \Pi_i$ and $\Pi_i^2 = \Pi_i$) acting on the three-dimensional chamber generation module $\mathcal{G}_{\rm gen} = \mathrm{span}\{g_1, g_2, g_3\}$; its image $\Pi_i \mathcal{G}_{\rm gen}$ is the $i$-th sector subspace (three-dimensional, one entry per family generation).*

*This result is consumed by C10.8.2 (orthogonality of the four projectors) and by C10.8.3 (the no-mediator identity), which both need the projector definitions as inputs. Frozen by R1.4 hash* `3b8d68559f5e`*.*

---

### C10.8.2 Projector identities: orthogonality and completeness

*The purpose of the next formula is to state the projector orthogonality and completeness conditions that make the sector decomposition well-defined.*

$$\boxed{\;\Pi_i\,\Pi_j \;=\; \delta_{ij}\,\Pi_i, \qquad \sum_{i \in \{u, d, e, \nu\}} \Pi_i \;=\; \mathbb{1}_{\mathcal{G}_{\rm gen}}.\;}$$

*This means: any two distinct sector projectors annihilate each other ($\Pi_u\Pi_d = \Pi_u\Pi_e = \cdots = 0$), so no state belongs to two sectors at once; and the four projectors together cover the entire generation module ($\Pi_u + \Pi_d + \Pi_e + \Pi_\nu = \mathbb{1}_{\mathcal{G}_{\rm gen}}$), so every state belongs to exactly one sector.*

*This result is consumed by C10.8.4 (macro-projector orthogonality $\Pi_q\Pi_\ell = 0$) and by C10.8.5 (the no-mediator identity), which both depend on the per-sector orthogonality as the algebraic input.*

The direct-sum decomposition follows immediately:

$$\mathcal{G}_{\rm gen} \;=\; \Pi_u\,\mathcal{G}_{\rm gen} \;\oplus\; \Pi_d\,\mathcal{G}_{\rm gen} \;\oplus\; \Pi_e\,\mathcal{G}_{\rm gen} \;\oplus\; \Pi_\nu\,\mathcal{G}_{\rm gen}.$$

---

### C10.8.3 Macro-projectors on $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$

*The purpose of the next formula is to define the macro-projectors $\Pi_q$ and $\Pi_\ell$ as aggregates of the sector projectors plus the doublet-component projectors.*

$$\boxed{\;\Pi_q \;=\; \Pi_u + \Pi_d + \Pi_{Q_L}, \qquad \Pi_\ell \;=\; \Pi_e + \Pi_\nu + \Pi_{L_L}.\;}$$

*This means: $\Pi_q$ is the macro-projector for the quark sector (up-quark + down-quark + left-handed quark doublet); $\Pi_\ell$ is the macro-projector for the lepton sector (charged lepton + neutrino + left-handed lepton doublet). They act on the matter bundle $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ via tensor product with the identity on the non-flavor factors (A2.3, A2.8).*

*This result is consumed by C10.8.4 (macro-orthogonality) and by C10.8.5 (the no-mediator identity), which need the macro-projectors as inputs to state the cross-sector annihilation.*

The doublet-component projectors $\Pi_{Q_L}, \Pi_{L_L}$ are defined by the matter-bundle factorisation of A2.3: $\Pi_{Q_L}$ picks out the $SU(2)_L$-doublet quark component (left-handed up-down doublet), $\Pi_{L_L}$ picks out the $SU(2)_L$-doublet lepton component (left-handed neutrino-electron doublet). Both are orthogonal: $\Pi_{Q_L}\Pi_{L_L} = 0$ by the gauge-bundle orthogonality $V_{\mathbf{3}} \otimes V_{\mathbf{1}} = 0$ (A2.3).

---

### C10.8.4 Macro-orthogonality

*The purpose of the next formula is to derive the macro-orthogonality of $\Pi_q$ and $\Pi_\ell$ from per-sector orthogonality.*

$$\boxed{\;\Pi_q \Pi_\ell \;=\; 0.\;}$$

*This means: the macro-quark projector and the macro-lepton projector annihilate each other. No state belongs to both the quark macro-sector and the lepton macro-sector.*

*This result is consumed by C10.8.5 (the no-mediator identity), which is the next step in the algebraic ladder.*

*Derivation.*

$$\Pi_q\,\Pi_\ell \;=\; (\Pi_u + \Pi_d + \Pi_{Q_L})(\Pi_e + \Pi_\nu + \Pi_{L_L}) \;=\; \sum_{i \in \{u,d,Q_L\}}\sum_{j \in \{e,\nu,L_L\}} \Pi_i \Pi_j.$$

Each cross-term vanishes by sector orthogonality: $\Pi_u \Pi_e = \Pi_u \Pi_\nu = \Pi_d \Pi_e = \Pi_d \Pi_\nu = 0$ by C10.8.2; $\Pi_{Q_L}\Pi_e = \Pi_{Q_L}\Pi_\nu = \Pi_{L_L}\Pi_u = \Pi_{L_L}\Pi_d = 0$ by the bundle-factorisation gauge orthogonality $V_{\mathbf{3}}\otimes V_{\mathbf{1}} = 0$ (A2.3); $\Pi_{Q_L}\Pi_{L_L} = 0$ by the same.

---

### C10.8.5 The no-mediator identity (load-bearing)

*The purpose of the next formula is to state the no-mediator identity that kills every dangerous Wilson coefficient on the active branch.*

$$\boxed{\;\Pi_q\,M\,\Pi_\ell \;=\; 0 \quad\text{for every sector-respecting }M\in\mathrm{End}(\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}).\;}$$

*This means: any admissible operator $M$ that respects the sector decomposition of the matter bundle — i.e., any $M$ of the form $M = \sum_i \Pi_i M_i \Pi_i$, where $M_i$ acts on the $i$-th sector subspace — sandwiched between $\Pi_q$ on the left and $\Pi_\ell$ on the right is identically zero. There is no admissible cross-sector mediator on the matter bundle.*

*This result is consumed by C10.8.7 (the per-operator Wilson-coefficient vanishing) and by [Appendix L](#appendix-l-proton-safety) (proton-safety gate certificate). It is the load-bearing identity of $\mathcal{E}_{\rm proton}$.*

*Proof (one line).*

$$\Pi_q\,M\,\Pi_\ell \;=\; \Pi_q \cdot \Big(\sum_i \Pi_i\,M_i\,\Pi_i\Big) \cdot \Pi_\ell \;=\; \sum_i (\Pi_q\,\Pi_i)\,M_i\,(\Pi_i\,\Pi_\ell) \;=\; 0,$$

because $\Pi_q\Pi_i = \Pi_i$ for $i \in \{u, d, Q_L\}$ and zero otherwise; $\Pi_i\Pi_\ell = \Pi_i$ for $i \in \{e, \nu, L_L\}$ and zero otherwise; and the two index sets $\{u, d, Q_L\}$ and $\{e, \nu, L_L\}$ are disjoint, so $\Pi_q\Pi_i$ and $\Pi_i\Pi_\ell$ cannot both be non-zero for the same $i$. $\square$

Frozen by R1.6 operator-class hash `551488d06011`; the full FCNC / mediator no-go theorem hash is `fff4b433b7b3` (R1.6).

---

### C10.8.6 Domain and codomain table

*The purpose of the next table is to record the domain and codomain of each projector with a pointer to the A2 tensor-ledger row that freezes it.*

| Projector | Symbol | Domain | Codomain | A2 reference |
|---|:---:|---|---|---|
| Sector up | $\Pi_u$ | $\mathcal{G}_{\rm gen}$ | $\Pi_u \mathcal{G}_{\rm gen}$ ($\dim = 3$) | A2.6 |
| Sector down | $\Pi_d$ | $\mathcal{G}_{\rm gen}$ | $\Pi_d \mathcal{G}_{\rm gen}$ ($\dim = 3$) | A2.6 |
| Sector charged-lepton | $\Pi_e$ | $\mathcal{G}_{\rm gen}$ | $\Pi_e \mathcal{G}_{\rm gen}$ ($\dim = 3$) | A2.6 |
| Sector neutrino | $\Pi_\nu$ | $\mathcal{G}_{\rm gen}$ | $\Pi_\nu \mathcal{G}_{\rm gen}$ ($\dim = 3$) | A2.6 |
| Macro-quark | $\Pi_q$ | $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ | $\Pi_q \mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ | A2.8 |
| Macro-lepton | $\Pi_\ell$ | $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ | $\Pi_\ell \mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ | A2.8 |
| BRST | $Q_{\rm BRST}$ | off-shell gauge-fixed Hilbert space | physical cohomology $\ker Q_{\rm BRST} / \mathrm{im}\,Q_{\rm BRST}$ | A2.9 |

*This means: every projector used in C10.8 has a named domain (where it accepts inputs from) and a named codomain (where its outputs live), recorded in A2.6 (sector projectors on $\mathcal{G}_{\rm gen}$), A2.8 (macro-projectors on $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$), and A2.9 (master projector table including BRST).*

*This result is consumed by reviewers reproducing Gate 10: domain mismatches are the most common reproduction failure for tensor identities, so the table is the single auditable reference.*

---

### C10.8.7 Dangerous operator classes blocked

*The purpose of the next list is to enumerate the dangerous baryon-number-violating operator classes that the no-mediator identity blocks.*

The following dim-6 and dim-7 four-fermion (and higher) operators are blocked by the no-mediator identity $\Pi_q M \Pi_\ell = 0$:

| Operator class | Mass dim | $\Delta B$ | $\Delta L$ | Why blocked |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| $QQQL$ | 6 | $+1$ | $+1$ | Three quark external legs $\in \Pi_q\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$; one lepton external leg $\in \Pi_\ell\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$; mediator $M$ must connect the two; $\Pi_q M \Pi_\ell = 0$. |
| $u_R^c u_R^c d_R^c e_R^c$ | 6 | $+1$ | $+1$ | Same — three quark external legs, one lepton external leg; right-handed sector. |
| $QLu_R^c d_R^c$ | 6 | $+1$ | $+1$ | Mixed left-right; same cross-sector mediator $M$ required; $\Pi_q M \Pi_\ell = 0$. |
| $QQu_R^c e_R^c$ | 6 | $+1$ | $+1$ | Mixed left-right; same cross-sector mediator $M$ required; $\Pi_q M \Pi_\ell = 0$. |
| $\bar d_R \bar d_R \bar u_R$ ($\Delta B = 1$ baryonic) | 6 | $+1$ | $0$ | Requires heavy coloured-triplet mediator; absent by product-factor structure of $K_{\rm gauge}$ (no coloured-triplet mediator outside SM gauge content). |
| Dim-7 $QQQL HH$ | 7 | $+1$ | $+1$ | Same cross-sector mediator $M$; additional $H^2$ suppression $(v/M_U)^2 \sim 10^{-28}$; coefficient zero by no-mediator identity. |
| Dim-7 $LLLL$ ($\Delta L = 4$) | 7 | $0$ | $\pm 4$ | High mass scale; chamber projector phases do not produce a measurable rate. |
| Dim-6 LFV $QL\bar d_R H$ | 6 | $0$ | $\pm 1$ | Cross-sector projection $\Pi_q M \Pi_\ell$ forbidden; sector projectors $\Pi_u, \Pi_e$ orthogonal. |

*This means: every operator that requires a cross-sector quark $\to$ lepton transition (or vice versa) is blocked by the no-mediator identity. The colour-triplet baryonic operator $\bar d_R \bar d_R \bar u_R$ is blocked by Ingredient 1 (absent mediator on $K_{\rm gauge}$), not by the no-mediator identity per se, but the structural reason is identical: the active-branch geometry has no mediator that connects the dangerous external legs.*

*This result is consumed by [Appendix L](#appendix-l-proton-safety) (Gate 10 certificate, L.2 per-operator absence ledger) and by the Wilson-coefficient vanishing table below (C10.8.8).*

---

### C10.8.8 Wilson coefficients vanish — explicit table

*The purpose of the next formula is to state the Wilson-coefficient vanishing result for the standard dim-6 operator basis.*

$$\boxed{\;C_{QQQL} \;=\; C_{u_R^c u_R^c d_R^c e_R^c} \;=\; C_{QLu_R^c d_R^c} \;=\; C_{QQu_R^c e_R^c} \;=\; 0.\;}$$

*This means: every dim-6 four-fermion baryon-violating Wilson coefficient on the active branch is identically zero, not numerically small. The zeros are exact algebraic consequences of the no-mediator identity $\Pi_q M \Pi_\ell = 0$, not tuned values.*

*This result is consumed by [Appendix L](#appendix-l-proton-safety) (Gate 10 certificate, L.2 ledger and L.3 mediator structure). It is the operator-level Passed claim of Gate 10.*

| Wilson coefficient | Value on active branch | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| $C_{QQQL}$ | $0$ | $\Pi_q M \Pi_\ell = 0$ on $\langle Q_L Q_L Q_L L_L | \mathcal{O}_{\rm med} | 0\rangle$ |
| $C_{u_R^c u_R^c d_R^c e_R^c}$ | $0$ | $\Pi_q M \Pi_\ell = 0$ on right-handed external legs |
| $C_{QLu_R^c d_R^c}$ | $0$ | $\Pi_q M \Pi_\ell = 0$ on mixed left-right legs |
| $C_{QQu_R^c e_R^c}$ | $0$ | $\Pi_q M \Pi_\ell = 0$ on mixed left-right legs |
| $C_{\bar d_R \bar d_R \bar u_R}$ | $0$ | No coloured-triplet mediator on product-factor $K_{\rm gauge}$ (Ingredient 1) |
| $C_{QL \bar d_R H}$ | $0$ | $\Pi_q M \Pi_\ell = 0$ on LFV cross-sector composition |
| Dim-7 $C_{QQQL HH}$ | $0$ (with additional $(v/M_U)^2$ if any residual exists) | Same identity extends to higher dim |

The standard dim-6 proton-decay operator basis is fully covered. The dim-7 relatives carry the same structure plus additional $v^2/M_U^2$ suppressions; the no-mediator identity continues to apply to any sector-respecting $M$, including higher-dim operators with the same external-leg structure.

---

### C10.8.9 BRST decoupling and KK-number conservation

*The purpose of the next formula is to state the BRST operator that decouples gauge-redundant spectator KK gauge modes.*

$$\boxed{\;Q_{\rm BRST} \;=\; \int d^{14}\!z\;\Big[\,c^a(z)\,G^a(z) \;-\; \tfrac{1}{2}\,f^{abc}\,c^a c^b\,\bar c^c\,\Big],\qquad Q_{\rm BRST}^2 = 0.\;}$$

*This means: $Q_{\rm BRST}$ is the standard Slavnov–Taylor BRST charge on the off-shell gauge-fixed Hilbert space, with $c^a, \bar c^a$ the Faddeev–Popov ghost / antighost fields, $G^a$ the gauge-fixing function, and $f^{abc}$ the structure constants of the gauge algebra. Nilpotency $Q_{\rm BRST}^2 = 0$ follows from the Jacobi identity on the gauge algebra and the gauge-fixing condition.*

*This result is consumed by Channel A of the spectator-decoupling argument (gauge-redundant components are BRST-exact and decouple from physical S-matrix by Slavnov–Taylor).*

**Gauge-redundant decoupling.** The longitudinal $A_\mu^a$ component and the scalar $A_5^a$ component of any spectator KK gauge mode are BRST-exact:

$$\big\{Q_{\rm BRST},\,\bar c_n^a(x)\,Y_n(y)\big\} \;=\; G^a_n(x)\,Y_n(y) \;=\; \mathcal{O}_{\rm gauge\text{-}redundant}^{a,n}(x, y),$$

so their matrix elements between physical states vanish by Slavnov–Taylor:

$$\langle\psi_{\rm SM}^{\rm out}\mid\mathcal{O}_{\rm gauge\text{-}redundant}^{a,n}\mid\psi_{\rm SM}^{\rm in}\rangle \;=\; \langle\psi_{\rm SM}^{\rm out}\mid\{Q_{\rm BRST},\,\bar c_n^a Y_n\}\mid\psi_{\rm SM}^{\rm in}\rangle \;=\; 0.$$

**This is the standard BRST decoupling of unphysical polarizations only.** It is **not** a claim that physical (transverse) massive KK modes are BRST-exact. Those modes are physical states in the BRST cohomology; they require independent selection rules (KK-number conservation + projector orthogonality) to decouple.

**KK-number conservation.** The compact-factor isometry generates a $U(1)_{\rm KK}$ charge equal to the KK quantum number $n$ on each factor. Every Feynman-diagram vertex conserves this charge. A diagram contributing to $\langle Q_L Q_L Q_L L_L \mid \mathcal{O}\mid 0\rangle$ has only SM zero-mode external states ($n_{\rm ext} = 0$ on every compact factor), so any internal physical KK mode of mass $m_n \sim n/R$ must be exchanged in a closed loop, not on a tree-level propagator:

$$\sum_{\rm external}n_{\rm ext} \;=\; 0 \quad\text{but}\quad n_{\rm physical\,KK\,mediator}\;\geq\;1 \quad\Longrightarrow\quad \text{tree amplitude}\;=\;0.$$

At loop level, the no-mediator identity of C10.8.5 kills the loop integrand by sector orthogonality.

**FCNC theorem citation.** The combined three-ingredient closure (mediator inventory + BRST + KK-number / projector orthogonality) is the FCNC / mediator no-go theorem of R1.6, hash `fff4b433b7b3`, with operator-class hash `551488d06011`.

---

### C10.8.10 Operator level vs lifetime — the binding distinction

*The purpose of the next statement is to distinguish the operator-level closure (Wilson coefficients identically zero) from the lifetime diagnostic (numerical lifetime estimate compared to Super-K bound).*

$\mathcal{E}_{\rm proton}$ closes the **operator-level** proton-safety question. Every dangerous dim-6 (and higher) baryon-violating Wilson coefficient is identically zero by the no-mediator identity. Status: **Claimed certificate pass**.

$\mathcal{E}_{\rm proton}$ does **not** predict an exact numerical proton lifetime. The lifetime estimate is reported separately in [Appendix L](#appendix-l-proton-safety) (L.4) as a *Diagnostic only* number — consistent with experimental Super-K bounds ($\tau_p > 10^{34}$ yr), but not used as a hard claim. Status: **Diagnostic only**.

The two statuses are kept distinct:

| Question | Status | What $\mathcal{E}_{\rm proton}$ supplies |
|---|---|---|
| Are there dangerous mediators on the active branch? | **Claimed certificate pass** (no — every $C_i = 0$) | The full operator-domain layer |
| What is the predicted proton lifetime? | **Diagnostic only** (not a hard claim) | Nothing — the lifetime is sourced separately by [Appendix L](#appendix-l-proton-safety) (L.4) |

Conflating the two would either overclaim ("we predict $\tau_p = X$ yr") or underclaim ("we only bound the lifetime numerically"). The correct phrasing is *Claimed certificate pass at the operator level* (Wilson coefficients identically zero) plus *Diagnostic only* on the lifetime (numerical estimate compared to Super-K, not used as a hard claim).

| Gate 10 — FCNC leg (contributory) | Same no-mediator identity kills tree-level FCNC mediators between sectors; $K^0$–$\bar K^0$ and $B$–$\bar B$ mixing are SM-only on the active branch | The identity that kills proton-decay mediators also kills FCNC mediators | **No** (requires precision flavor comparison from [Appendix L](#appendix-l-proton-safety)'s FCNC sub-ledger) |

The operator-level closure (status *Claimed certificate pass*) and the lifetime diagnostic (status *Diagnostic only*) are kept distinct (C10.8.10).

## C10.11 What This Term Does and Does Not Prove

**C10 records or proves:**

- the four sector projectors $\Pi_u, \Pi_d, \Pi_e, \Pi_\nu$ are mutually orthogonal and sum to the identity on $\mathcal{G}_{\rm gen}$;
- the macro-projectors $\Pi_q = \Pi_u + \Pi_d + \Pi_{Q_L}$ and $\Pi_\ell = \Pi_e + \Pi_\nu + \Pi_{L_L}$ are orthogonal: $\Pi_q\Pi_\ell = 0$;
- the no-mediator identity $\Pi_q M \Pi_\ell = 0$ holds for every sector-respecting $M$ on the matter bundle, by a one-line algebraic proof from per-sector orthogonality;
- gauge-redundant spectator KK components are BRST-exact (Channel A);
- physical KK modes are killed by KK-number conservation at tree level and by projector orthogonality at loop level (Channel B);
- every dangerous Wilson coefficient on the active branch ($C_{QQQL}$, $C_{u_R^c u_R^c d_R^c e_R^c}$, $C_{QLu_R^c d_R^c}$, $C_{QQu_R^c e_R^c}$, and dim-7 relatives) is identically zero;
- the operator-level closure of Gate 10 is *Claimed certificate pass*;
- the mechanism is compatible with SM electroweak sphalerons (no global $U(1)_B$ symmetry invoked);
- $\mathcal{E}_{\rm proton}$ is **Retained** in the migration ledger (A3.17, all proton-safety objects Retained).

**C10 does not by itself prove:**

- an exact numerical proton lifetime (the lifetime is sourced by [Appendix L](#appendix-l-proton-safety) (L.4) as *Diagnostic only*, not used as a hard claim — see C10.8.10);
- flavor closure (handled by C5 + Appendices I/J/K);
- anomaly cancellation (handled by [Appendix E](#appendix-e-chirality-and-anomaly-closure) together with $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$);
- the full Yukawa matrices (handled by C5 + Appendices J / K);
- CKM / PMNS mixing (handled by C5 + [Appendix J](#appendix-j-quark-certificate));
- Higgs protection (handled by C9 + [Appendix H](#appendix-h-higgs-protection));
- the product-factor structure of $K_{\rm gauge}$ (the $\times$-layer fact contributing Ingredient 1 is sourced by C2 / C3 / C4).

Those require other dossiers and gate appendices.

**Required anti-overclaim sentence.**

> C10 closes proton safety at the **operator** level (no dangerous mediators). It does NOT prove an exact proton lifetime — the lifetime is a separately reported *Diagnostic only* number. It does NOT prove flavor closure (C5). It does NOT prove anomaly cancellation ([Appendix E](#appendix-e-chirality-and-anomaly-closure)). The mechanism is operator-algebra; no global $U(1)_B$ symmetry is invoked anywhere.

This protects against overclaiming.

## C10.12 Failure If Removed

| If removed | What becomes undefined or wrong | First gate affected |
|---|---|---|
| Sector projectors $\Pi_u, \Pi_d, \Pi_e, \Pi_\nu$ entirely | No sector decomposition of $\mathcal{G}_{\rm gen}$; chamber operators $O_u, O_d, O_e, O_\nu$ have no domain; per-sector orthogonality is undefined | **Gate 9 fails** (Yukawa map procedure has no sector structure); **Gate 10 fails outright** (no-mediator identity cannot be stated) |
| Macro-projectors $\Pi_q, \Pi_\ell$ | Macro-orthogonality $\Pi_q\Pi_\ell = 0$ unavailable on $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$; no-mediator identity has no domain | **Gate 10 fails** (identity cannot be lifted from $\mathcal{G}_{\rm gen}$ to $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$) |
| No-mediator identity asserted but unproved | Identity becomes an axiom rather than a theorem; certificate collapses to a postulate; freeze-before-compare discipline B.5 violated | **Gate 10 fails (CI tier)** — the identity is the load-bearing closure |
| BRST decoupling on gauge-redundant components | Spectator KK gauge modes can mediate $\Delta B = 1$ via gauge-fixing artefacts (longitudinal $A_\mu$, scalar $A_5$); Channel A is missing | **Gate 10 fails** (Channel A closure missing) |
| KK-number conservation on physical KK modes | Physical (transverse) KK modes can appear on tree-level proton-decay amplitudes; the BRST argument alone does not kill them; Channel B is missing | **Gate 10 fails** (Channel B closure missing) |
| Empty cross-sector cohomology | Chamber-mediated cross-sector amplitudes are not forbidden; FCNC mediator theorem becomes a numerical bound, not an identity | **Gate 10 fails** (Ingredient 3 of L.3.1 missing) |
| Sector projectors not aligned with matter-bundle factorisation (A2.3) | Sector projectors defined only on $\mathcal{G}_{\rm gen}$, not lifted to $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$; no-mediator identity stated on wrong domain | **Gate 10 fails** (formulation fails; A2.8 macro-projector domain table undefined) |
| Replaced by global $U(1)_B$ symmetry | Forbids SM electroweak sphalerons; structurally incompatible with SM | **Gate 10 fails + SM consistency** — candidate forbids observed phenomena |
| Replaced by per-operator Wilson-coefficient tuning | Each dangerous coefficient becomes a separate fit parameter; over-determination collapses | **Gate 10 fails as Passed** (now a tuning fit, not an operator identity) |
| Product-factor $K_{\rm gauge}$ replaced by simple-group embedding | $X/Y$ off-diagonal mediator present; Ingredient 1 fails; $C_{QQQL} \sim 1/M_{X/Y}^2 \neq 0$ | **Gate 10 fails on mediator-inventory leg** (Ingredient 1 collapses; this is a $\times$-layer failure but $\mathcal{E}_{\rm proton}$ alone cannot recover it) |

**Plain-English conclusion.**

> Removing $\mathcal{E}_{\rm proton}$ is like removing the algebraic wall between the quark and lepton sectors. Without the sector projectors and the no-mediator identity, every dangerous Wilson coefficient that the standard four-fermion operator basis can write down would become a free parameter requiring per-operator tuning, and the active branch would lose its operator-level proton-safety claim. The product-factor structure of $K_{\rm gauge}$ alone is *not* enough — chamber-mediated cross-sector amplitudes and spectator KK modes would reopen the dangerous channels.

---

## C10.13 Minimality / Alternatives

The retained construction is the *unique* surviving candidate inside the declared search category that closes Gate 10 at the operator level. The argument is fourfold:

1. *Among safety mechanisms in the declared search category R2, operator-algebra is the only option.* Global symmetries are eliminated by Constraint 4 (forbid sphalerons); per-operator tuning is eliminated by Constraint 5 (over-determination); raising $M_{X/Y}$ leaves operators present (Constraint 1); weaker projector identity leaves residues (Constraint 1); R-parity-style symmetries are outside R2.
2. *Among operator-algebra constructions, all five components are independently necessary.* Removing any one (sector projectors, macro-projectors, no-mediator identity, BRST, KK-number) breaks the closure. The sector projectors supply the building blocks; the macro-projectors lift to the matter bundle; the BRST decouples gauge-redundant spectators; KK-number kills physical KK at tree level; the no-mediator identity kills chamber compositions.
3. *Among sector-projector decompositions, the four-projector decomposition $\{u, d, e, \nu\}$ is the unique decomposition compatible with the chamber operators $O_u, O_d, O_e, O_\nu$ of C5.* Alternative decompositions (e.g., 3-projector $\{q, \ell, H\}$ or 2-projector $\{q, \ell\}$) cannot route the chamber operators to their domains.
4. *Among "no new frozen primitive" routes, $\mathcal{E}_{\rm proton}$ is bookkeeping over existing primitives.* The sector projectors are R1.4 hash `3b8d68559f5e` (already counted); the FCNC theorem is R1.6 hash `fff4b433b7b3` (already counted); the macro-projectors are derived; the BRST is standard. C10 introduces no new R1 row; the manifest meta-hash is unchanged.

The retained construction is therefore the minimal surviving candidate; the alternatives ledger of C10.5 covers nine nearby candidates and eliminates each by a named first-failed constraint.

**Summary.** $\mathcal{E}_{\rm proton}$ is the *minimal admissible operator-domain layer* inside the declared search category that closes Gate 10 at the operator level. No alternative survives the eight constraints of C10.3.

---

## C10.14 Layer Routing / Anti-Smuggling Check

The successful term $\mathcal{E}_{\rm proton}$ is, by classification, a **pure $\otimes$-layer object** (the operator-domain layer). Its domains live over the $\times$-layer base; its admissible-operator-class declaration lives in the $\oplus$-layer admissibility ledger.

| Layer | What belongs to C10 | What must be routed elsewhere |
|---|---|---|
| $\times$ | none native | The base manifolds $\mathcal{M}_4 \times K_{\rm gauge} = \mathcal{M}_4 \times K_6 \times S^2 \times S_Y^{\,1}/\mathbb{Z}_2$ on which the projectors act sit in C2 / C3 / C4. The *product-factor* structure of $K_{\rm gauge}$ — Ingredient 1 of the no-mediator theorem — is a $\times$-layer fact (A1.4–A1.6). |
| $\oplus$ | none native | The per-sector orthogonality $\Pi_i\Pi_j = \delta_{ij}\Pi_i$ as a chamber admissibility rule (rooted in R1.4 hash `3b8d68559f5e`); the FCNC / mediator no-go theorem's *operator-class declaration* hash `551488d06011`; the empty-cohomology statement on cross-sector compositions; KK-number conservation as an admissibility rule. These live in C6 ($\mathcal{C}_{\rm admiss}$) and R1.6. |
| $\otimes$ | Four sector projectors $\Pi_u, \Pi_d, \Pi_e, \Pi_\nu$ as operator-domain objects on $\mathcal{G}_{\rm gen}$ (A2.6); two macro-projectors $\Pi_q, \Pi_\ell$ on $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ (A2.8); BRST operator $Q_{\rm BRST}$ on the off-shell gauge-fixed Hilbert space (A2.9); no-mediator identity $\Pi_q M \Pi_\ell = 0$ as a tensor identity in $\mathrm{End}(\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter})$ (A2.8); per-operator Wilson-coefficient zeros as tensor identities | nothing — this is the layer C10 is native to |

**Required sentence.**

> $\mathcal{E}_{\rm proton}$ is **pure $\otimes$** (operator-domain layer). Its domains live over $\times$ (the base manifolds $\mathcal{M}_4 \times K_{\rm gauge}$ from C2/C3/C4). The admissible-operator-class declaration is $\oplus$ (sourced by C6 $\mathcal{C}_{\rm admiss}$ and frozen by R1.6 operator-class hash `551488d06011`).

**Layer-smuggling claim.** Nothing in C10 silently imports content from the $\times$- or $\oplus$-layers. The product-factor structure of $K_{\rm gauge}$ (which contributes Ingredient 1 of the no-mediator theorem) is *named* (C2 / C3 / C4 sources it; A1.4–A1.6 freezes it), not silently inherited. The per-sector orthogonality (which makes the sector projectors *sector-respecting*) is *named* as a $\oplus$-layer admissibility rule with hash `3b8d68559f5e`. The FCNC / mediator no-go theorem's *operator-class declaration* (which routes the $\otimes$-layer projector identity into the closure claim) is *named* with hash `551488d06011`. These are *named cross-layer dependencies*, not smuggles.

**Anti-smuggling: no global $U(1)_B$.** The active branch carries no global $U(1)_B$ symmetry. The safety mechanism is *operator-algebra* (the no-mediator identity), not symmetry. SM electroweak sphalerons are explicitly compatible: they change $B$ and $L$ by $\pm 3$ each while conserving $B - L$, in a manner that respects the sector-respecting algebra (they act between $Q_L$ and $L_L$ multiplets inside the same chamber structure that the macro-projectors define). Constraint 4 is satisfied.

**Anti-smuggling: no per-operator tuning.** The no-mediator identity $\Pi_q M \Pi_\ell = 0$ is a *theorem* (one-line proof in C10.8.5), not an axiom. No coefficient is separately fitted; the identity holds *exactly* by construction from per-sector orthogonality plus gauge-bundle orthogonality. Freeze-before-compare discipline B.5 is respected.

## C10.17 Hostile Reviewer Attack Matrix

| Reviewer attack | What would falsify the term's claim |
|---|---|
| **Operator-vs-lifetime conflation** (most common attack) | "C10 claims a hard proton-lifetime prediction." *Falsified by C10.8.10 + C10.11 + L.5*: C10 closes proton safety **at the operator level only** (every dangerous Wilson coefficient identically zero). The proton **lifetime** is reported separately as a *Diagnostic only* number in [Appendix L](#appendix-l-proton-safety) (L.4), not used as a hard claim. The two statuses are *Claimed certificate pass* (operator level) and *Diagnostic only* (lifetime). Treating the lifetime as a closure would conflate operator absence with numerical bound; C10 explicitly does not. |
| Term unnecessary | "Remove $\mathcal{E}_{\rm proton}$ and Gate 10 still closes at operator level." *Falsified by C10.12*: removing any of the five operator-layer components (sector projectors, macro-projectors, no-mediator identity, BRST, KK-number) opens Gate 10. No alternative active branch in R2 closes Gate 10 without an equivalent operator-algebra layer. |
| **No-mediator identity trivial — "sector-respecting" tautology** | "The identity follows from the definition of sector-respecting $M$, so the theorem is trivial." *Falsified by C10.8.5 + C10.8.7*: the identity follows from per-sector orthogonality $\Pi_i\Pi_j = \delta_{ij}\Pi_i$ (A2.6, R1.4 hash `3b8d68559f5e`) combined with gauge-bundle orthogonality $V_{\mathbf{3}}\otimes V_{\mathbf{1}} = 0$ (A2.3). The dangerous SM operators ($QQQL$, $u_R^c u_R^c d_R^c e_R^c$, etc.) all lie in the sector-respecting class (their external legs are quark fields and lepton fields that the matter-bundle factorisation already distinguishes), so the identity *applies* and forces their coefficients to zero. The non-trivial content is the *exhaustiveness* of the sector-respecting class (C10.8.7 enumerates the eight dangerous operator classes). |
| **BRST claim wrong — physical KK modes are not BRST-exact** | "C10 invokes BRST for all KK modes, but physical KK modes are not BRST-exact." *Falsified by C10.8.9 (two-channel separation)*: Channel A (gauge-redundant components) is BRST-exact by Slavnov–Taylor — this is the standard result, not a claim about physical modes. Channel B (physical transverse KK modes) is *not* BRST-exact; it is killed by KK-number conservation at tree level and by projector orthogonality at loop level. C10 does **not** assert physical KK modes are BRST-exact. |
| Higher-dimensional operators not covered | "The argument only covers dim-6 operators; what about dim-7 and higher?" *Falsified by C10.8.7 + C10.8.8 final row*: the same three ingredients (mediator inventory, BRST decoupling, empty cross-sector cohomology) extend to dim-7 and higher with additional $1/M_U^k$ suppressions; the L.2 ledger covers up to dim 7 explicitly and notes the higher-dim continuation. The no-mediator identity continues to apply to any sector-respecting $M$. |
| Forbidding SM sphalerons | "The mechanism that forbids proton decay also forbids the SM electroweak sphalerons." *Falsified by C10.14 + L.3 + Constraint 4*: sphalerons act inside the *same* sector decomposition (they change $B$ by $\pm 3$ and $L$ by $\pm 3$ in a way that respects $B - L$); they do not constitute a cross-sector $\Pi_q M \Pi_\ell$ amplitude — they act between $Q_L$ and $L_L$ multiplets in a manner that conserves the sector-respecting algebra. The no-mediator identity forbids dim-6 cross-sector Wilson coefficients of the *dangerous* structure (i.e., $\Delta B = \pm 1$ at four-fermion level); it does not forbid sphaleron processes that conserve the chamber-respecting structure. |
| Separate frozen object vs. bookkeeping | "Is $\mathcal{E}_{\rm proton}$ a separate frozen object, or bookkeeping?" *C10.15 explicit*: bookkeeping over existing frozen primitives (R1.4 `3b8d68559f5e` for sector projectors + R1.6 `fff4b433b7b3` for the FCNC theorem + standard BRST). No new primitive frozen object; C10 adds no row to A0. The role of C10 is to *audit* the projector / BRST / cohomology architecture in dossier format, just as A2.8 audits it in tensor-ledger format and [Appendix L](#appendix-l-proton-safety) certifies it. Manifest meta-hash `a5b1e6f9d951` is unchanged. |
| Term smuggled — global $B$ symmetry | "$\mathcal{E}_{\rm proton}$ silently invokes a global $B$ symmetry." *Falsified by C10.14 layer-routing check*: no global $U(1)_B$ is invoked anywhere; the safety is operator-algebra. Sphaleron-allowed processes are explicitly compatible (Constraint 4 satisfied). |
| Term smuggled — per-operator tuning | "The no-mediator identity is a disguised per-operator tuning." *Falsified by C10.8.5 + B.5*: the identity is a *theorem* with a one-line proof from per-sector orthogonality plus gauge-bundle orthogonality. No coefficient is separately fitted; the identity holds *exactly* by construction. |
| Term smuggled — propagating Cartan torus | "The chamber substrate carries a propagating Cartan-torus metric factor." *Falsified by A1.9 + A3.7 Option B + C5.9*: the Cartan torus is *Absorbed into $F^+$* as chamber data (A3.7); $D = 13$ (no Cartan-torus metric direction); no smuggle. |
| Boundary smuggling | "Extend $\mathcal{E}_{\rm proton}$ into a non-GUT sector (e.g., dark proton-decay channels, hidden FCNC)." *Falsified by Section 9 / Gate 11*: any such extension is *boundary-excluded*; the certificate-complete status of Gate 10's operator side is conditional on no such extension being introduced. |
| Specific path 1 — show some dangerous operator is *not* sector-respecting | "Exhibit a dim-6 BLV operator whose external legs are *not* in the sector decomposition $\{u, d, e, \nu, Q_L, L_L\}$." *Falsified by C10.8.7 + L.2*: every dim-6 BLV operator $QQQL, u_R^c u_R^c d_R^c e_R^c, QLu_R^c d_R^c, QQu_R^c e_R^c$, etc., is in the sector-respecting class — its external legs are quark fields and lepton fields that the matter-bundle factorisation already distinguishes. The dossier's classification is exhaustive in L.2; no operator outside this class has been exhibited. |
| Specific path 2 — show physical KK modes must be BRST-exact for the argument | "The BRST argument requires that physical KK modes be BRST-exact." *Falsified by C10.8.9 two-channel separation*: the BRST argument applies only to Channel A (gauge-redundant components); Channel B (physical transverse KK) is closed by independent selection rules (KK-number + projector orthogonality), not by BRST. The argument does not assert physical KK modes are BRST-exact. |

A reviewer who can falsify any row above demonstrates that C10's necessity claim must be weakened or that the layer routing is incorrect. None of these has been supplied for the submitted manuscript.

---

## C10.18 Comprehension Questions

After reading C10, a non-specialist reader should be able to answer the following ten questions in plain language.

1. **What is the job of $\mathcal{E}_{\rm proton}$?**  $\mathcal{E}_{\rm proton}$ is the operator-domain layer that mathematically forbids dangerous mediators connecting the quark and lepton sectors — without it, proton decay would proceed too fast.
2. **What is a Wilson coefficient?**  A Wilson coefficient is the numerical pre-factor of a four-fermion (or higher) operator in the effective Lagrangian; it tells you how strongly the operator contributes to a process.
3. **What does $QQQL$ mean physically?**  $QQQL$ is the four-fermion operator that would turn three quarks into a lepton (plus a pion or kaon); it is the archetypal proton-decay operator.
4. **What is the no-mediator identity in plain English?**  Any admissible operator that respects the sector decomposition kills cross-sector quark-to-lepton amplitudes: there is no admissible bridge between quark states and lepton states.
5. **Why does $\Pi_q M \Pi_\ell = 0$ kill proton decay?**  Because every dangerous proton-decay operator requires a cross-sector quark-to-lepton transition, and the identity $\Pi_q M \Pi_\ell = 0$ makes every such transition's Wilson coefficient identically zero.
6. **Why not just postulate global baryon-number conservation?**  Because a global $U(1)_B$ symmetry would forbid the SM electroweak sphalerons, which are part of the SM. C10's mechanism is operator-algebra, not symmetry.
7. **What about heavy KK modes — do they decay the proton?**  No. Gauge-redundant KK components are BRST-exact (Channel A); physical KK modes are killed at tree level by KK-number conservation and at loop level by projector orthogonality (Channel B).
8. **Does C10 predict a proton lifetime?**  No. C10 closes the operator-level question (no dangerous mediators); the lifetime is reported separately in [Appendix L](#appendix-l-proton-safety) as a *Diagnostic only* number, not used as a hard claim.
9. **What gates does C10 support?**  Gate 10 (proton safety, operator-level Passed primarily); also contributory to Gate 10's FCNC leg and Gate 9's sector-orthogonality leg.
10. **What fails if $\mathcal{E}_{\rm proton}$ is removed?**  Without the sector projectors, no-mediator identity, BRST decoupling, KK-number conservation, or empty cross-sector cohomology, dangerous Wilson coefficients reopen and Gate 10 fails at the operator level. The active branch would need per-operator tuning or a global $U(1)_B$ symmetry — both of which are eliminated by the constraints of C10.3.

**Acceptance rule.**

> If a non-specialist reader cannot answer these without rereading advanced notation, this dossier still needs bridge sentences.

**One-sentence summary.**

> $\mathcal{E}_{\rm proton}$ supplies the sector projectors $\Pi_q, \Pi_\ell$ and the algebraic identity $\Pi_q M \Pi_\ell = 0$ that mathematically forbid cross-sector quark-to-lepton mediators, so every dangerous proton-decay Wilson coefficient vanishes identically by sector orthogonality — closing Gate 10 at the operator level (Passed) while leaving the numerical lifetime as a separately reported Diagnostic.

---

## C10.19 Closing Status

C10 is complete when the reader can follow this chain end-to-end:

```text
The Standard Model has a proton-decay problem
  → most GUT candidates allow dim-6 four-fermion BLV operators
  → Super-K bounds the proton lifetime at >10^34 yr
  → the active branch needs a mechanism to kill those operators
  → global U(1)_B is excluded (forbids SM sphalerons)
  → per-operator tuning is excluded (breaks freeze-before-compare)
  → simple-group X/Y embedding is excluded (Constraint 7)
  → the chosen mechanism is operator-algebra
  → sector projectors Π_u, Π_d, Π_e, Π_ν decompose 𝒢_gen orthogonally
  → macro-projectors Π_q, Π_ℓ lift to 𝓔_matter with Π_q Π_ℓ = 0
  → no-mediator identity Π_q M Π_ℓ = 0 for every sector-respecting M
  → BRST + KK-number close the spectator-mode channels
  → every dangerous Wilson coefficient (C_QQQL, ...) is identically zero
  → Gate 10 has *Claimed certificate pass* at the operator-class level for the declared dangerous operator class; numerical lifetime estimate remains *Diagnostic only*
  → lifetime number remains Diagnostic, not a hard claim
  → removing 𝓔_proton breaks the chain at one or more of those steps
```

**Status.** *Claimed certificate pass* for the C10 term-level operator-class safety construction, conditional on the declared mediator/operator class, projector identities, BRST decoupling, KK-number constraints, and empty cross-sector cohomology record. Numerical proton-lifetime and branching estimates remain *Diagnostic only*. Every constraint of C10.3 is named with a source and a frozen-object response. Every alternative of C10.5 is eliminated with a stated first-failed gate. The sector projectors of C10.8.1, the macro-projectors of C10.8.3, the no-mediator identity of C10.8.5, the BRST decoupling of C10.8.9, the Wilson-coefficient zeros of C10.8.8, and the failure-if-removed cascade of C10.12 are all routed to authority pointers in C10.15. The hostile-review certificate of C10.16 is auditable against A0 / A1 / A2 / A3 / [Appendix L](#appendix-l-proton-safety) / [Appendix R0](#appendix-r0-freeze-certificates-and-code-hashes). The attack matrix of C10.17 addresses thirteen reviewer challenges, including the operator-vs-lifetime conflation, the trivialisation attack on the no-mediator identity, and the BRST/physical-KK two-channel separation.

**Gate 10 status.**

- **Operator side: *Claimed certificate pass*.** Every dangerous BLV Wilson coefficient ($C_{QQQL}, C_{u_R^c u_R^c d_R^c e_R^c}, C_{QLu_R^c d_R^c}, C_{QQu_R^c e_R^c}, \ldots$) vanishes identically by the no-mediator identity $\Pi_q M \Pi_\ell = 0$ combined with the product-factor structure of $K_{\rm gauge}$, BRST decoupling on gauge-redundant components, KK-number conservation on physical KK modes, and empty SM-charged physical cohomology on cross-sector chamber compositions.
- **Lifetime diagnostic: *Diagnostic only*** (not used as a hard claim). The numerical lifetime estimate of L.4 sits above current Super-Kamiokande lower bounds; it is reported only to show consistency with experiment, not to assert a measured lifetime. The *closure claim* does not depend on the lifetime number.

**Failure conditions for C10 itself.** C10 fails if (i) any R1 hash cited in C10.15 does not regenerate from manifest meta-hash `a5b1e6f9d951`; (ii) any reviewer exhibits a dim-6 (or higher) BLV operator whose external legs are not in the sector-respecting class $\{u, d, e, \nu, Q_L, L_L\}$; (iii) the one-line proof of the no-mediator identity in C10.8.5 fails to reproduce from the projector-orthogonality data of A2.6 plus the matter-bundle factorisation of A2.3; (iv) the layer-routing check of C10.14 is found to silently invoke a global $U(1)_B$ symmetry or a per-operator tuning; (v) the operator-vs-lifetime distinction is conflated (treating the lifetime number as a hard claim or treating the operator-level closure as a numerical bound); or (vi) the BRST claim is incorrectly extended to physical (transverse) KK modes (Channel B is independent of BRST cohomology).

**Final sentence.**

> $\mathcal{E}_{\rm proton}$ is retained because it is the smallest readable and auditable operator-domain layer in the declared active branch that mathematically forbids every cross-sector quark-to-lepton mediator through the algebraic identity $\Pi_q M \Pi_\ell = 0$, closes Gate 10 at the operator level without invoking a global baryon-number symmetry, and keeps the proton-lifetime number cleanly separated as a *Diagnostic only* output. The general reader gets the story; the formal reviewer gets the proof trail.

# Appendix C11–C16 — Summary Matrices

[Appendix C](#appendix-c-term-by-term-construction-dossiers)'s ten dossiers (C1–C10) each carry an audit record for one named term. The matrices below summarise the cross-cutting structure: which term supports which gate, which term fails which gate first if removed, where each term's freeze data and reproduction artifacts live, and how the B2 layer-level result lines up with the term-level dossiers.

The matrices are bookkeeping only. They do not introduce new claims. The authority is the per-term dossier (C1–C10), the gate certificates (Appendices D–L), the freeze record (A0 / [Appendix R0](#appendix-r0-freeze-certificates-and-code-hashes)), and the layer-level necessity proof (B2). Cells reference these sources rather than re-deriving them.

The matrices use the following column shorthand for the ten named terms:

- $\mathcal{M}_4$ → C1
- $K_6 = SU(3)/T^2$ → C2
- $S^2$ → C3
- $S_Y^{\,1}/\mathbb{Z}_2$ → C4
- $F^+_{\rm finite}$ → C5
- $\mathcal{C}_{\rm admiss}$ → C6
- $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$ → C7
- $\mathcal{E}_{\rm gauge}$ → C8
- $\mathcal{E}_{\rm Higgs}$ → C9
- $\mathcal{E}_{\rm proton}$ → C10

Cell legend (C11):
- **R** — required (the gate / output cannot close without this term).
- **S** — supporting (the term contributes; another term carries primary load).
- **—** — not used for this gate / output.

| No $\oplus$-rulebook for finite chamber / admissibility | $F^+_{\rm finite}$, $\mathcal{C}_{\rm admiss}$ | C5, C6 | Gates 3, 9, claim discipline |
| No $\otimes$-actors / operator domains | $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$, $\mathcal{E}_{\rm gauge}$, $\mathcal{E}_{\rm Higgs}$, $\mathcal{E}_{\rm proton}$ | C7–C10 | Gates 2, 4, 5, 8, 10 |
| Backbone without $F^+$ | $F^+_{\rm finite}$ | C5 | Gate 9 |
| Hypercharge quotient without line bundle / projector | $S_Y^{\,1}/\mathbb{Z}_2$, $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$, $\mathcal{C}_{\rm admiss}$ | C4, C7, C6 | Gates 3–5 |
| Proton-safety without sector projectors | $\mathcal{E}_{\rm proton}$, $\mathcal{E}_{\rm matter}$, $\mathcal{C}_{\rm admiss}$ | C10, C7, C6 | Gate 10 |

The interpretive rule is the same as in B2: a layer-level failure rules out an entire candidate class; the term-level dossier (T-cited) supplies the proof that the specific named term inside that layer is the one carrying the load.

A successful attack on any single row above is sufficient to demote the affected dossier's status. The dossiers are written to make these attack-paths concrete rather than rhetorical: the reviewer is given the specific calculation or construction to attempt.

# Appendix D — Standard Model Recovery

**Purpose.** Verify that the active branch recovers the Standard Model gauge algebra $SU(3)_c \times SU(2)_L \times U(1)_Y$ with correct representations, hypercharge, and electric charge on every surviving multiplet.

**Main claim supported.** Closure of Gates 2 and 3 of Section 6: gauge recovery and hypercharge / electric charge recovery.

**Load-bearing role.** Authoritative source for the Gates 2 and 3 certificate of Section 6 per the Claim-to-Appendix Authority Map (front matter). All Gates 2 and 3 closure language elsewhere in the manuscript inherits this appendix's status; conflicts are resolved by this appendix.

**Downstream impact (per the Appendix Dependency Graph, front matter).** If this appendix's certificate is downgraded under the R2 Downgrade Rules, Gates 2 and 3 drop one rung in Section 6, the Section 6.13 Certificate-Status Summary updates, and any gate or appendix that depends on Gates 2 and 3's output (per the dependency graph) inherits the downgrade — specifically downstream Gates 4, 5, 7, 8, 9, and 10, which depend on the SM recovery output.

**Inputs.** Active geometry ([Appendix A](#appendix-a-full-geometry)); architectural Standard Model facts (gauge group, charge assignments, $\mathbb{Z}_6$ identification).

**Frozen objects.** Surviving gauge algebra; gauge generator map from compact-factor isometries; representation map; hypercharge embedding under the $S_Y^{\,1}/\mathbb{Z}_2$ quotient; global $\mathbb{Z}_6$ identification; charge table.

**Outputs.** Standard Model gauge group; representation table; hypercharge table; electric-charge table; exotics ledger; certificate hash.

**Status.** *Claimed certificate pass.*

**Main-text references.** Section 6.1 (Gate 2); Section 6.2 (Gate 3); [Appendix A](#appendix-a-full-geometry) (factor / field embedding); [Appendix R0](#appendix-r0-freeze-certificates-and-code-hashes) (certificate hash).

---

## D.1 Surviving Gauge Algebra

The compact-factor isometries of $K_{\rm gauge} = K_6 \times S^2 \times S_Y^{\,1}$ generate the algebra

$$
\mathfrak{g}_{\rm geom} \;=\; \mathfrak{su}(3) \;\oplus\; \mathfrak{su}(2) \;\oplus\; \mathfrak{u}(1),
$$

acting on the KK modes of the compact factors. $K_6 = SU(3)/T^2$ is the flag manifold whose isometry algebra is $\mathfrak{su}(3)$; $S^2$ is the homogeneous space of $SU(2)$; $S_Y^{\,1}$ is the parent circle whose translations act as $U(1)_Y$. The orbifold quotient on $S_Y^{\,1}$ removes the mirror sector, leaving the surviving low-energy gauge algebra

$$
\mathfrak{g}_{\rm SM} \;=\; \mathfrak{su}(3)_c \;\oplus\; \mathfrak{su}(2)_L \;\oplus\; \mathfrak{u}(1)_Y.
$$

No additional gauge factor survives at the comparison scale; no Standard Model factor is missing.

## D.2 Representation Assignment

| Field | $SU(3)_c$ | $SU(2)_L$ | $Y$ | $Q$ | Origin |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| $Q_L$ | $\mathbf{3}$ | $\mathbf{2}$ | $+1/6$ | $(+2/3, -1/3)$ | Chiral $K_6$ mode under spin-$\mathbb{C}$ projection; $SU(2)_L$ doublet on $S^2$ |
| $u_R$ | $\mathbf{3}$ | $\mathbf{1}$ | $+2/3$ | $+2/3$ | Up-sector mode through chamber operator $O_u$ |
| $d_R$ | $\mathbf{3}$ | $\mathbf{1}$ | $-1/3$ | $-1/3$ | Down-sector mode through $O_d$ |
| $L_L$ | $\mathbf{1}$ | $\mathbf{2}$ | $-1/2$ | $(0, -1)$ | Chiral lepton mode on $S^2 \times S_Y^{\,1}$ |
| $e_R$ | $\mathbf{1}$ | $\mathbf{1}$ | $-1$ | $-1$ | Charged-lepton mode through $O_e$ |
| $\nu$ / $M_\nu$ | $\mathbf{1}$ | $\mathbf{1}$ | $0$ | $0$ | Neutrino-sector mode through $O_\nu$; Dirac/Majorana data declared in H |
| Higgs $H$ | $\mathbf{1}$ | $\mathbf{2}$ | $+1/2$ | $(+1, 0)$ | Wilson-line mode of $K_{\rm gauge}$ |
| Gauge bosons | adjoint | adjoint | — | — | KK modes of $K_{\rm gauge}$ isometries |

All representations follow from the projector data of [Appendix A](#appendix-a-full-geometry).4 acting on the surviving mode bases. No multiplet is assigned its charge by hand.

## D.3 Hypercharge and Electric Charge

The hypercharge $Y$ is the $U(1)$ generator of translations along $S_Y^{\,1}$, quantized by the parent-circle topology. The electric charge is

$$
Q \;=\; T_3 \;+\; Y,
$$

with $T_3$ the diagonal generator of $SU(2)_L$. The global $\mathbb{Z}_6$ identification ties the centres of $SU(3)$ (operating on $K_6$) and $SU(2)$ (on $S^2$) to the hypercharge phase on $S_Y^{\,1}$. This identification is *not* added by hand; it is the unique consistency rule that allows the three compact-factor isometries to act consistently on the surviving fields, and it is what makes the observed charge fractions $(2/3, -1/3, -1, 0)$ arise without per-multiplet adjustment.

### D.3.1 Explicit Charge Audit

For each surviving multiplet, we verify $Q = T_3 + Y$ component by component, and we verify the global $\mathbb{Z}_6$ rule (every multiplet $\psi$ must satisfy $6Y(\psi) \in \mathbb{Z}$ and the centre actions of $SU(3)_c$, $SU(2)_L$, $U(1)_Y$ must be consistent on $\psi$).

| Multiplet | $T_3$ values | $Y$ | $Q = T_3 + Y$ (computed) | $6Y \in \mathbb{Z}$? | Centre-of-$SU(3)$ action | Centre-of-$SU(2)$ action | $\mathbb{Z}_6$ check |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| $Q_L = (u_L, d_L)^T$ | $(+1/2, -1/2)$ | $+1/6$ | $(+2/3, -1/3)$ ✓ | $6 \cdot (1/6) = 1$ ✓ | $\omega_3$ (triplet) | $\omega_2$ (doublet) | $\omega_3 \omega_2 \omega_6 = \omega_6^{3+3+1} = \omega_6^7 = \omega_6$ — closure |
| $u_R$ | $0$ | $+2/3$ | $+2/3$ ✓ | $6 \cdot (2/3) = 4$ ✓ | $\omega_3$ (triplet) | $1$ | $\omega_3 \cdot 1 \cdot \omega_6^4 = \omega_6^{2+4} = 1$ ✓ |
| $d_R$ | $0$ | $-1/3$ | $-1/3$ ✓ | $6 \cdot (-1/3) = -2$ ✓ | $\omega_3$ (triplet) | $1$ | $\omega_3 \cdot 1 \cdot \omega_6^{-2} = \omega_6^{2-2} = 1$ ✓ |
| $L_L = (\nu_L, e_L)^T$ | $(+1/2, -1/2)$ | $-1/2$ | $(0, -1)$ ✓ | $6 \cdot (-1/2) = -3$ ✓ | $1$ | $\omega_2$ (doublet) | $1 \cdot \omega_2 \cdot \omega_6^{-3} = \omega_6^{3-3} = 1$ ✓ |
| $e_R$ | $0$ | $-1$ | $-1$ ✓ | $6 \cdot (-1) = -6$ ✓ | $1$ | $1$ | $1 \cdot 1 \cdot \omega_6^{-6} = 1$ ✓ |
| $\nu_R$ / $\nu_L^c$ | $0$ | $0$ | $0$ ✓ | $6 \cdot 0 = 0$ ✓ | $1$ | $1$ | trivial ✓ |
| $H$ | $(+1/2, -1/2)$ | $+1/2$ | $(+1, 0)$ ✓ | $6 \cdot (1/2) = 3$ ✓ | $1$ | $\omega_2$ (doublet) | $1 \cdot \omega_2 \cdot \omega_6^{3} = \omega_6^{3+3} = 1$ ✓ |

Notation: $\omega_n = e^{2\pi i/n}$. The $\mathbb{Z}_6$ "check" column verifies that the product of the centre actions on each multiplet is trivial in $\mathbb{Z}_6$ — i.e., that the three centres glue consistently. Every row checks. The closure ambiguity in the $Q_L$ row reflects the fact that the doublet carries both centres and is the multiplet from which the $\mathbb{Z}_6$ identification is *derived*; the others provide independent verification. The global $\mathbb{Z}_6$ rule is therefore not an additional assumption but the unique consistency condition that allows all surviving multiplets to be defined simultaneously.

## D.4 Exotics and Extra Modes

| Candidate exotic | Why it might appear | Elimination rule | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| Extra $U(1)$ (e.g., $U(1)_{B-L}$) | Generic compactifications often have extra abelian factors | No extra abelian factor survives the projector and orbifold structure on $K_{\rm gauge}$; [Appendix A](#appendix-a-full-geometry) explicit | Absent |
| Mirror fermions | Smooth $S_Y^{\,1}$ would return both chiralities | $S_Y^{\,1}/\mathbb{Z}_2$ orbifold removes the mirror sector ([Appendix E](#appendix-e-chirality-and-anomaly-closure)) | Absent |
| Light KK gauge tower | $K_{\rm gauge}$ has a discrete KK spectrum | First KK mass at the compactification scale; comparison scale at $M_Z$ excludes it | Massive (no light exotic) |
| Exotic fractional charges | Wrong $\mathbb{Z}_6$ identification | Global $\mathbb{Z}_6$ rule enforced; fractional charges match SM exactly | Absent |
| Adjoint scalars | Some KK reductions produce extra adjoints | Projector and orbifold rules eliminate the relevant zero modes | Absent |

No exotic charged or coloured state survives at the comparison scale; if one did, the certificate of this appendix would fail.
